job.title: Experienced Field Maintenance Technician (3+ Years Required)
job.description:
Most property maintenance operations run on reactive chaos: calls pile up, vendors get dispatched blind, and nobody really owns the outcome. Lessen is built differently. As a tech-enabled, nationwide property services platform handling over 3.5 million work orders a year across more than a million properties, they've structured maintenance around data and accountability rather than guesswork. That structure means their field technicians actually own their work, from the first inspection to the closed-out work order.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

This is a field-based maintenance role covering properties in the Orlando portfolio. You're not floating between a hundred units waiting for a radio call. You carry your own work orders, manage your own materials budget, and you're accountable for the quality of every completed job. Lessen explicitly prioritizes quality over volume, which is a meaningful distinction in field maintenance. It means your performance isn't measured purely by tickets closed per day.

Day to day, you're diagnosing and resolving issues across a range of property types, working independently with the support of Lessen's technology platform, which routes work orders and tracks job history. You'll have a company fleet vehicle and fuel card, a tool and equipment allowance, and a cell phone allowance. No on-call rotation. Weekday schedule.

The pay range starts at $23 to $27 per hour depending on experience, skill level, and competencies. There's also a quarterly discretionary bonus of up to 5% of quarterly earnings, plus corporate-level benefits and OSHA training provided.

What They're Looking For

  • Minimum 3 years of verifiable, hands-on experience in property maintenance or a related trade
  • Ability to independently diagnose and resolve a broad range of maintenance issues
  • Comfortable managing work order documentation and materials budgets
  • Solution-oriented approach: you troubleshoot first rather than immediately escalating
  • Strong communication with clients and property contacts
  • Valid driver's license (fleet vehicle is provided)

What separates strong candidates here isn't just technical range, though that matters. It's ownership mentality. Lessen's model gives technicians real autonomy, and that works best with people who close out a job the way they'd want it done on their own property. Technicians who need close supervision or who treat work orders as boxes to check tend to struggle in this kind of structure.

Orlando's rental market has expanded significantly over the past several years, with a large single-family rental and build-to-rent footprint that demands exactly the kind of field-based, independently managed maintenance this role is built around. If you've been working in that space locally, you already understand the geography and pace this job requires.

There's a clear path forward here, too. Lessen is growing nationally, and they've identified Lead Technician and Field Maintenance Manager roles as the next steps for strong performers. If you want field work now but have your eye on managing a team or a region eventually, this is a reasonable place to build toward that.

job.title: Maintenance Technician
job.description:
Bozzuto manages a large portfolio of apartment communities, and their maintenance teams are the reason those properties hold their reputation. This role is a ground-level technician position at one of their Denver communities, handling the daily work that keeps residents happy and buildings running.

What the Work Looks Like

Your days split between two main areas: service requests and turns. On the service side, you're resolving HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance issues for residents. On the turns side, you're prepping vacant units for move-in, which means working through punch lists and making sure each apartment is ready before a new resident walks through the door. You'll also handle grounds upkeep, snow removal during Denver winters, and general building cleanliness.

This role includes an on-call rotation. After-hours emergencies happen, and you need reliable personal transportation to respond. That's not a footnote; it's a real part of the job.

What They're Looking For

  • Hands-on experience in building maintenance or a trades background
  • Beginner to intermediate skill level in HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Ability to lift and carry between 25 and 50 pounds regularly
  • Strong communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Comfort using software and mobile apps for work orders
  • Availability for weekends, evenings, and on-call shifts
  • Personal, reliable transportation

EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification isn't required on day one. Bozzuto will pay for it in full after your 12-month anniversary, which is a concrete benefit if you're working toward broader HVAC credentials.

Pay and Growth

The salary range is $45,176 to $52,000, with bonus opportunities on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

For technicians building a career, this kind of role is worth taking seriously. Technicians who develop strong HVAC and electrical skills at the apartment level often move into lead tech or maintenance supervisor roles within a few years. Bozzuto operates across multiple markets, so internal mobility is realistic for people who perform and want to grow.

Applications are accepted through April 27, 2026.

job.title: Maintenance Technician ($2,500 Sign on Bonus)
job.description:
Bozzuto runs a tight operation across its managed communities, and the maintenance technician role sits at the center of that. You're the person residents call when something breaks, and you're the one keeping units ready between turns. This position is based in Venice, FL and comes with a $2,500 sign-on bonus.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your days will split between reactive and proactive work. Service tickets come in covering HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. You'll work through those systematically. Between tickets, you're handling preventative maintenance and keeping the property clean and safe. When a unit goes vacant, you're running the turn: punch list, repairs, and getting it move-in ready.

This role includes weekend and evening shifts on a rotating basis, and you'll carry on-call responsibility for after-hours emergencies. That's standard for any maintenance role worth taking seriously. If you need reliable transportation to get to the property and respond quickly when something comes up at 10pm, that's a real requirement here, not a formality.

  • HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair at a beginner to intermediate level
  • Unit turns and make-ready work between residents
  • General upkeep, grounds maintenance, and building cleanliness
  • On-call emergency response on a rotating schedule
  • Resident-facing service with a professional, communicative approach
  • Basic computer skills for work order software and apps

What Separates Strong Candidates

Technicians who do well here aren't just wrench-turners. They communicate clearly with residents, document their work, and take pride in how a unit looks when they hand it back. The ability to move between a plumbing call and an HVAC diagnostic without losing momentum matters. Bozzuto will pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification after your first year on the job, so if you don't have it yet, that's a concrete path to a more valuable credential at no cost to you.

The salary range is $47,000 to $50,000, with additional bonus opportunities on top of the sign-on. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

The Bigger Picture

Maintenance technicians at well-run companies like Bozzuto often move into lead technician or service manager roles as they build out their trade skills. If you're early in your career and want structured exposure across multiple systems, a property with a strong training culture is a better place to start than going it alone. This role gives you that foundation.

job.title: Maintenance Technician - $2,500 Sign-On Bonus
job.description:
Maintenance technicians at residential properties carry more responsibility than most job descriptions let on. You're not just fixing things. You're the person residents actually see, the one who shows up when something is broken, when a unit needs to turn, when a pipe fails at midnight. At Bozzuto's Washington, DC community, this role sits at the intersection of skilled trades work and genuine resident experience.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Day to day, you'll work through service tickets covering HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and appliance repair across the community. Make-readies are part of the rhythm here too. When a unit turns, you're completing punch list items and getting the apartment into move-in condition. You'll also handle grounds upkeep, building cleanliness, and snow removal when the DC winters require it.

The on-call component is real and worth naming directly. After-hours emergencies happen, and you'll rotate into that responsibility. Bozzuto offers a $2,500 sign-on bonus and a $50,000 base salary for this role, which reflects the expectation that you're available when the property needs you, not just during business hours.

One thing worth knowing about Bozzuto specifically: after your 12-month anniversary, they'll pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification. For a technician looking to build HVAC credentials without funding it out of pocket, that's a concrete benefit, not a vague "growth opportunity."

What Makes Someone a Strong Fit

Bozzuto isn't looking for someone who can only work from a checklist. The technicians who thrive here tend to combine solid trade fundamentals with real communication skills. You're talking to residents constantly. How you explain a repair timeline, or handle a frustrated call at 7 PM, matters as much as the technical fix itself.

  • Experience in building maintenance or a trades education background
  • Beginner to intermediate skills in HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Availability for on-call emergency rotation
  • Ability to lift and exert 25 to 50 pounds of force frequently
  • Reliable personal transportation for commuting and emergency response
  • Comfortable using software and apps for work order management
  • Strong written and verbal communication, and a professional presentation

The candidates who separate themselves from the pack aren't necessarily the most technically advanced. They're the ones who take ownership, communicate clearly with residents and the property team, and treat a service ticket as a relationship touchpoint, not just a task. In a market like Washington, DC, where Class A residential communities have high resident expectations, that mindset matters more than most employers admit up front.

Bozzuto also provides medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. If you're a technician who wants to grow your skills, earn credentials, and work in a well-regarded residential community, this is a reasonable place to do it.

job.title: Maintenance Technician ($2,500 Sign-On Bonus)
job.description:
It's a Tuesday morning, and there's a work order for a tripped breaker in 4B, a leaky shutoff valve in 2A, and a turn unit that needs to be punched out before Friday's move-in. That's the job. Not glamorous, but never boring.

Bozzuto is hiring a Maintenance Technician for their East Boston community, and they're offering a $2,500 sign-on bonus to make the conversation worth having. The salary sits in the $52,000 to $53,000 range, which is a reasonable starting point for a technician with beginner-to-intermediate skills who's genuinely motivated to grow.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

You're handling service ticket resolution across the usual disciplines: HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. You'll prep vacant units for move-in, which means turns, punch lists, and making sure nothing gets missed before a resident walks through the door. General building upkeep, grounds maintenance, and snow removal are also part of the picture. This is Boston, so that last one is not a footnote.

There's an on-call rotation, which is the honest part of every maintenance job that doesn't always get front-page treatment. You need reliable personal transportation and the availability to respond when something goes sideways after hours. If that's a dealbreaker, this role isn't the right fit. If you're used to it, you already know the tradeoff.

What You Bring

  • Some background in building maintenance or a trades education. Entry-level candidates with the right attitude are considered.
  • Working knowledge of HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair at a beginner-to-intermediate level
  • Ability to handle physical demands, including frequently moving 25 to 50 pounds
  • Basic computer skills for work order platforms and communication
  • Strong communication and a professional presentation
  • A genuine service orientation. Residents notice the difference.

One thing worth knowing: Bozzuto will pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification once you hit your 12-month anniversary. That's a real credential that follows you throughout your career in residential maintenance, and having it covered is not something every operator offers. It signals that this isn't just a warm body situation.

Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of PTO plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. The culture at Bozzuto consistently earns recognition, which in our industry usually means the internal operations are at least functional and people aren't running for the exits every six months.

If you're a technician looking to build your skills under a well-organized operator in a market where good maintenance talent is genuinely hard to find, this one is worth a closer look.

job.title: Maintenance Technician ($2,500 Sign-On Bonus)
job.description:
Maintenance work at a well-run residential property is really a bundle of distinct technical skills, and the best technicians treat each one as something to sharpen over time. This role with Bozzuto in Mineola, NY sits at that intersection: you're expected to handle HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair, but the company is also explicitly structured to help you build on what you bring in. That combination is worth paying attention to.

The Skills This Role Puts to Work

Day to day, you'll spend real time on service ticket resolution, which sounds simple until you're troubleshooting an appliance issue in one unit, jumping to a plumbing call in another, and managing a make-ready punch list for a vacant apartment before move-in. The ability to context-switch without losing quality is underrated in this work. Preventative maintenance is its own discipline. It requires you to think ahead rather than react, and technicians who get good at it dramatically reduce emergency call volume over time.

Turn-over work is another core piece of this role. Preparing vacant units for move-in means you need an eye for detail and a sense of what a resident notices on day one. Bozzuto sets a high bar on the aesthetic side of things. Grounds maintenance, snow removal, and general upkeep round out the physical scope. You should expect weekend availability and participation in an on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies. That's standard at any quality residential community, and it's worth being honest with yourself about before applying.

What You're Building Toward

Bozzuto will fund your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification after your first year. That's a meaningful credential. HVAC-certified technicians with solid all-around maintenance experience have a clear path toward senior technician roles, lead maintenance positions, and eventually Maintenance Supervisor or Facilities Manager roles. The skills you build here, especially in HVAC and resident communication, transfer directly up that chain.

  • Beginner to intermediate experience in HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Reliable transportation for commuting and on-call response
  • Physical ability to lift and exert force in the 25 to 50 pound range regularly
  • Comfort with basic software and digital work order systems
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • A service orientation that holds up under pressure

What Makes This Specific Opportunity Worth Considering

Bozzuto operates as a third-party management company with a reputation built heavily on resident experience. That culture shapes how maintenance teams operate. You're not just fixing things. You're a visible part of how residents feel about where they live. Technicians who internalize that tend to move faster at companies like this than those who treat it as a purely technical job.

The $2,500 sign-on bonus and a salary range of $55,000 to $60,000 are competitive for the Mineola market. The benefits package includes medical, dental, and vision, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For someone early in their maintenance career who wants structured growth and real credential support, the setup here is worth taking seriously.

job.title: Maintenance Technician ($3,000 Sign-On Bonus)
job.description:
Bozzuto operates across the full property management spectrum, from development through day-to-day operations, and their maintenance teams reflect that same attention to detail. This Maintenance Technician role at their Coram, NY community is built for someone who takes genuine pride in the craft, whether that's a clean HVAC repair or a perfectly turned unit ready for move-in day.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your days will pull from several disciplines at once. Service ticket resolution, preventative maintenance, and on-call emergency response form the core of the role. You'll work through HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and appliance repair at a beginner-to-intermediate level, which means you don't need to walk in as a master of every trade. What you do need is a solid foundation and the drive to keep sharpening it.

Turn work is a real part of this job. Preparing vacant units for incoming residents requires attention to detail and a methodical approach. A thorough punch list mindset matters here. Beyond the apartments themselves, you'll contribute to the overall appearance and safety of the community through grounds upkeep, snow removal, and general cleanliness.

This role includes weekend and evening availability, and participation in an on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies. That's a real commitment, and it's worth going in clear-eyed about it. Reliable personal transportation is required, both for getting to work and for responding when the on-call phone rings at 11pm.

Skills You Bring, Skills You'll Build

The role requires a working knowledge of building maintenance trades and the physical capacity to do the job (frequent lifting in the 25 to 50 pound range). Strong communication skills matter more than many maintenance candidates expect. Resident-facing service is part of every interaction, and how you show up in those moments shapes the resident experience as much as the repair itself.

What this role builds: Bozzuto offers a clear path toward EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification, fully covered by the company, after your first year. That credential carries real weight in the industry and opens doors to senior technician and lead maintenance roles down the line. Comfort with maintenance software and property management apps is also something you'll develop on the job, and that tech fluency transfers across any future role in the field.

  • Experience in building maintenance or a trades education background
  • Beginner to intermediate HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair proficiency
  • Availability for an on-call rotation and after-hours emergencies
  • Reliable personal transportation
  • Basic computer and software skills
  • Strong written and verbal communication
  • A service-oriented mindset and genuine interest in learning

Compensation and Growth

The salary range for this position is $54,000 to $57,000, with a $3,000 sign-on bonus and eligibility for additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. Bozzuto also maintains a range of employee resource groups and is committed to an inclusive workplace culture.

Candidates who stand out in this type of role combine trade competence with a genuine service orientation. The technical skills are teachable. The instinct to treat a resident's broken heat in January as genuinely urgent, not just a ticket to close, is what separates good technicians from great ones.

job.title: Maintenance Technician - $3,500 Sign-On Bonus
job.description:
Residential property maintenance sits at the intersection of technical skill and resident experience. When a resident submits a work order, the technician who shows up represents the entire community. Bozzuto understands this, and they're hiring a Maintenance Technician at their Quincy, MA property with a $3,500 sign-on bonus to fill that role.

What This Role Actually Involves

Day to day, you're working through a mix of service tickets and preventative tasks. That means HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair at a beginner-to-intermediate level. You're also handling turns: prepping vacant units for move-in through make-ready repairs and punch list items. Between that, you're keeping the property looking sharp through general upkeep, grounds maintenance, and snow removal when Boston winters demand it.

Honest acknowledgment here: this role includes an on-call rotation. You'll cover evenings, weekends, and after-hours emergencies. That's the nature of residential maintenance, and Bozzuto is upfront about it. Reliable personal transportation is a firm requirement, not just a preference, since responding to emergencies quickly matters.

The salary range runs from $52,530 to $55,000, plus the sign-on bonus and additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

Skills This Role Requires vs. Skills It Builds

What you need coming in:

  • Hands-on building maintenance experience or a trades education background
  • Working knowledge of HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair (beginner to intermediate is fine)
  • Basic computer proficiency for work order software and apps
  • Strong verbal and written communication, because you're interacting with residents directly
  • Physical ability to frequently move 25 to 50 pounds and consistently handle lighter loads throughout a shift
  • A professional appearance and a genuine service mindset

What this role builds over time is different. Technicians who work in well-managed communities like Bozzuto's tend to sharpen their diagnostic skills faster because the volume and variety of work orders is consistent. You'll develop comfort with property management software and resident communication in ways that translate directly to a lead technician or maintenance supervisor role. Strong performers here often move into Service Manager positions, where they're overseeing the full maintenance operation, managing vendor relationships, and contributing to capex planning.

The candidates who stand out aren't always the most technically advanced. What separates a good fit from a great one at a company like Bozzuto is the combination of technical competence and genuine care for residents. Showing up to a work order with both the right tools and the right attitude is what earns trust in a residential community, and that reputation compounds over time.

job.title: Maintenance Technician ($500 Sign-On Bonus)
job.description:
The Skills That Drive This Role

Maintenance work at a Bozzuto community in Issaquah isn't just about fixing things. It's about keeping a residential operation running cleanly enough that occupancy holds, residents renew, and the asset stays in shape. The skills you bring to this position touch every layer of that: HVAC diagnosis, plumbing, electrical troubleshooting, appliance repair, and the kind of general upkeep that keeps a property looking like it's worth what residents are paying for it.

You don't need to be a master of every trade on day one. Beginner to intermediate proficiency across those core systems is the baseline. What matters more is your instinct for working through a problem, your ability to prioritize a service ticket queue without letting things pile up, and the communication skills to close the loop with a resident so they actually feel taken care of. That last part is underrated. A technically perfect repair that ends with a resident feeling ignored still hurts the property.

What You'll Actually Be Doing

Your day will move between reactive and preventative work. Service tickets come in covering the usual range: HVAC performance issues, leaks, electrical complaints, appliances that stop working mid-lease. You'll work through those while also handling turns. Make-ready work on vacant units is a real part of this role, and how quickly and thoroughly you can punch out a turn directly affects how fast those units get back into the leasing pipeline.

Beyond the units themselves, you're contributing to grounds maintenance, general building upkeep, and snow removal when the Pacific Northwest demands it. The property's curb appeal and cleanliness fall partly in your hands.

On-call rotation is part of the deal. After-hours emergencies happen, and you'll need reliable personal transportation to respond. Weekends and evenings are in the mix. That's the honest reality of property maintenance, and it's worth knowing going in.

  • HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair at a beginner to intermediate level
  • Turn and make-ready work on vacant apartments
  • Preventative maintenance and general building upkeep
  • Snow removal and grounds care
  • On-call availability for after-hours emergencies
  • Ability to lift and carry 25 to 50 pounds frequently
  • Basic computer skills for work order systems and apps

What Bozzuto Brings to You

The $500 sign-on bonus is a straightforward starting point. The more meaningful long-term perk: after your 12-month anniversary, Bozzuto will cover the cost of your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification. For any technician looking to move toward HVAC specialization, that's real value. EPA 608 Universal is the credential that opens doors to more complex HVAC work and higher earning potential across the industry.

Compensation runs $52,000 to $58,240 annually, with additional bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. Bozzuto has a reputation as an operator that invests in its maintenance staff, and the certification support backs that up in a concrete way.

If you're a technician who takes service quality seriously and wants to build real credentials while doing the work, this is a position worth taking a close look at.

job.title: Maintenance Technician Hiring Event - Friday, May 8th (10a to 4p)
job.description:
It's a Friday morning. A work order just came in for a unit with no heat, another resident is waiting on a leaking faucet repair, and there's a turn on the third floor that needs to be ready for move-in by Monday. That's a pretty normal start to the day in this role, and it's exactly the kind of pace Bozzuto is hiring for right now in Washington, D.C.

Bozzuto is holding an in-person hiring event on Friday, May 8th from 10am to 4pm EST for Maintenance Technicians across their D.C. portfolio. The format is straightforward: you apply, a recruiter contacts you to confirm the location and schedule your interview slot, and qualified candidates can receive an offer within 48 business hours of the event. Bring your resume and dress professionally.

What the Job Actually Involves

This is a full-scope maintenance tech role. You're handling service ticket resolution across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. You're doing make-readies on vacant units, getting them punched out and move-in ready. You're also handling general upkeep of the building and grounds, including snow removal when D.C. winters demand it.

The on-call piece is real. This role includes a rotation for after-hours emergencies, which means your own reliable transportation isn't optional. Residents in a Bozzuto community expect a high service standard, and the tech fielding that 10pm call needs to show up ready. That's just the honest reality of property maintenance at this level.

The pay range is $50,000 to $55,000, with additional bonus opportunities. After your 12-month anniversary, Bozzuto will cover the cost of your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification, which is a real credential worth having and one that opens doors to senior tech and HVAC-focused roles down the line.

What They're Looking For

  • Some background in building maintenance or trades education. Beginner to intermediate skill level in HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair is acceptable.
  • Physical capability to frequently handle 25 to 50 pounds of force, and constant handling of 10 to 20 pounds.
  • Availability for on-call rotation and weekend or evening shifts when needed.
  • Reliable personal transportation.
  • Solid communication skills, both written and verbal, and basic comfort with software and apps used for work order management.
  • A genuine service mindset. Residents notice the difference between a tech who just closes tickets and one who actually takes care of people.

Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage; 20 days of paid time off plus holidays; a 401k with company match; and tuition reimbursement. Bozzuto has a real reputation in the mid-Atlantic market for investing in their maintenance staff, which is worth something when you're comparing offers.

If you're a tech with solid fundamentals who wants to work for a company that actually tracks your development, this event is worth a few hours of your Friday.

job.title: Maintenance Technician - Lease-Up
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

This is a lease-up role. That means you're not walking into a stabilized property with a known rhythm. You're coming in during the early stages of occupancy, which brings a specific kind of workload: incomplete punch lists, warranty items, first-time resident move-ins, and service requests stacking up faster than the occupancy curve climbs. Turns happen constantly during lease-up, and the expectation for speed and quality on make-readies is higher than at a stabilized asset. You need to be comfortable working in a building that's still finding its footing.

Day to day, you're resolving service tickets across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. You're prepping vacant units for move-in, handling general upkeep, and keeping grounds and common areas presentable. Snow removal is part of the job when the season calls for it. You'll rotate through on-call coverage for after-hours emergencies, including evenings and weekends. That's the honest part of this role that every posting mentions and every candidate should actually think through before applying.

One thing worth noting about the timing: Bozzuto will cover your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification after your 12-month anniversary. If you're still working toward that credential, this role gives you a clear path to get it paid for.

What You Bring

  • Hands-on experience in building or facilities maintenance, or formal trade education
  • Beginner to intermediate skills in HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Reliable personal transportation for commuting and on-call response
  • Physical capability to frequently move 25 to 50 pounds and handle constant light lifting
  • Basic comfort with software and mobile apps (work order platforms, resident communication tools)
  • Availability for rotating on-call coverage, evenings, and weekends
  • A service-first mindset. Residents notice attitude as much as they notice results.

Context Worth Knowing

Lease-up maintenance experience is genuinely valuable on a resume. Technicians who come up through a lease-up learn to triage fast, communicate with residents who have higher expectations (they just signed a lease on a brand-new or newly renovated unit), and handle volume without letting quality slip. Those skills transfer directly into senior tech or lead roles at stabilized properties. We've seen a lot of technicians use a lease-up assignment as the thing that accelerated their career, because the exposure is compressed and intense in a way that a quiet Class B property just isn't.

The Langhorne, PA position comes with a salary range of $53,000 to $55,000, bonus eligibility, medical/dental/vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. Bozzuto operates as a third-party management company with a recognizable brand in the mid-Atlantic and Northeast markets, which carries some weight if you're thinking about where this role could take you next.

job.title: Maintenance Technician - Marlin Cove
job.description:
Maintenance tech roles at well-run apartment communities tend to follow a predictable rhythm: service tickets in the morning, turns in the afternoon, and the occasional after-hours call that reminds you why reliable transportation is listed as a hard requirement. At Marlin Cove in Foster City, Bozzuto is looking for someone who can hold down that rhythm consistently and take real ownership of the physical condition of the property.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Day to day, you're resolving resident service requests across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. You'll handle make-ready work on vacant units, working through punch lists to get apartments back in rentable condition before new residents move in. Grounds upkeep, general cleanliness, and keeping the building looking sharp are part of the job too. That's not glamorous work, but it matters more than most people think for resident retention and the overall feel of the community.

The honest part: this role includes weekend availability, evening shifts, and participation in an on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies. If a water heater fails at 10pm, you're expected to respond. That's standard for this type of position, and it's worth factoring into your decision before you apply.

What You Bring

  • Some background in building maintenance or a trades education. Beginner to intermediate skill in HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair is the target range.
  • Physical ability to frequently move 25 to 50 pounds and handle the demands of hands-on maintenance work throughout a full shift.
  • Your own reliable transportation. This isn't optional given the on-call component.
  • Basic comfort with software, apps, and work order systems. Most properties run digital maintenance platforms now, and you'll be using one here.
  • Clear written and verbal communication. Resident interactions are part of the job, and how you handle them matters.

Growth and Compensation

The salary range is $54,110 to $56,000, with additional bonus opportunities on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. One specific perk worth noting: after your 12-month anniversary, Bozzuto will pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification. For someone still building out their HVAC credentials, that's a meaningful investment. The 608 opens doors to more complex refrigerant work and carries weight when you're looking at senior tech or lead tech roles down the road.

Strong candidates here aren't necessarily the ones with the longest resume. The ones who stand out are reliable, communicate clearly with residents even when the news isn't great, and approach preventative maintenance with the same attention they give emergency repairs. If you treat a routine service ticket the same way you treat an urgent one, you'll do well in this environment.

job.title: Maintenance Technician - Salem, OR
job.description:
It's a Tuesday afternoon and you've got three open service tickets, a vacant unit that needs to be turned before the weekend, and a resident who just flagged a slow drain on your way to the supply room. That's a normal day. If that kind of variety keeps you moving rather than stressed, this role is worth your attention.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Bozzuto is hiring a Maintenance Technician for their Salem, Oregon community. The core of the job covers what you'd expect from a well-run residential property: service ticket resolution, preventative maintenance, and make-ready work to get vacant units back into rent-ready condition. Beyond that, you'll handle HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repairs across the property, plus general upkeep including grounds maintenance and snow removal during Oregon winters.

The role does include an on-call rotation. After-hours emergencies are part of the job, and you'll need reliable personal transportation to respond when they come up. Bozzuto is upfront about this, so it's worth factoring into your decision.

One thing worth noting for candidates who are still building their technical foundation: Bozzuto will pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification after your 12-month anniversary. That's a meaningful credential for any technician looking to grow into HVAC-heavy work or eventually move into a lead tech or maintenance supervisor role.

What You Bring

  • Some background in building maintenance or a trades education (beginner to intermediate skill level is fine)
  • Working knowledge across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Physical ability to frequently lift and carry 25 to 50 pounds
  • Comfort with basic software and mobile apps used for work orders and communication
  • Reliable transportation and availability for on-call emergency response
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills and a professional presentation

The candidates who tend to stand out in this kind of role aren't necessarily the ones with the longest resume. They're the ones who treat residents like people, communicate clearly about timelines, and take ownership of a punch list without being managed through every item. Service orientation and technical skill compound each other here.

Compensation runs from roughly $50,300 to $52,700 annually, with bonus opportunities on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement for ongoing development.

For a technician who wants to grow, this is a solid place to build. The skills you sharpen here, particularly in resident communication and multi-trade troubleshooting, transfer directly into lead technician and maintenance supervisor positions down the road.

job.title: Multi-Site Assistant Maintenance Manager - Brentwood & New Carrollton, MD
job.description:
Two Properties, One Role

This is a multi-site position covering two Bozzuto communities: one in Brentwood and one in New Carrollton, MD, with work also extending into Northeast Washington, DC. You're not a single-property technician. You're coordinating maintenance operations across locations, which means your day involves more logistics, more coordination with site teams, and more ownership over the full scope of each building's physical condition.

Bozzuto manages a mix of mid-rise and garden-style residential communities, and their maintenance culture emphasizes asset preservation just as much as resident service. The phrase they use internally is "preserve and protect." That's the operating mindset here.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

You'll handle the full range of trades work: HVAC diagnostics and repairs, plumbing, electrical, and appliance service. You'll also manage the make-ready process, keeping turns moving on schedule so units are leased-ready when the leasing team needs them. Preventative maintenance programs are yours to own and execute consistently, not just schedule.

Beyond the technical work, you're a lead. You set the standard on how work gets done, how residents are treated, and how the property presents itself day to day. That includes grounds upkeep, snow removal, and general cleanliness. Safety code compliance and OSHA standards are non-negotiable on your watch.

Weekend availability is required. You'll also rotate into the emergency on-call schedule, which is standard for this level in residential maintenance.

What You Need

  • 5 or more years in the trades, facilities maintenance, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of HVAC systems, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and paint skills
  • Familiarity with complex mechanical systems (boilers, chillers, central plant, split systems) is a plus
  • Supervisory or lead experience preferred
  • Knowledge of OSHA, MSDS, and general risk management practices
  • Comfort with technology platforms and basic Microsoft Office proficiency
  • Ability to work across both the Brentwood and New Carrollton sites

The salary range for this role is $70,000 to $72,000, with additional bonus eligibility. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What Separates Strong Candidates

The multi-site piece is where most candidates either thrive or struggle. Strong applicants will have managed work orders and turn schedules without a supervisor prompting every step. They know how to prioritize when two properties need attention at the same time, and they communicate clearly enough that site teams stay aligned without constant check-ins. If you've only ever worked a single-property role, this is a real step up in complexity. Come prepared to speak to how you've managed competing priorities across locations or departments.

job.title: Multi-Site Leasing Consultant
job.description:
Multi-site leasing roles exist because a single property doesn't always generate enough traffic volume to keep a skilled sales professional fully challenged. Bozzuto has structured this position to span multiple communities in the Harrison, NJ area, which means you're building a broader view of how different assets perform, what drives traffic, and how to close across varying price points and resident profiles. That breadth is genuinely useful if you're thinking about where this career goes next.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Most of your day runs on follow-up and conversion. A prospect fills out a form or calls in, and your job is to turn that lead into a tour, and that tour into a signed lease. Bozzuto wants someone who can conduct personalized, informative tours that don't feel scripted. The social media piece is real here too. You'll be expected to create and post content on platforms like Instagram and Facebook to generate awareness and keep the communities top of mind for people who are still in research mode. It's not just liking posts; it's original content that actually reflects the character of the property.

The schedule runs on a weekend-heavy rhythm. Three weekends per month with two weekdays off is the structure. If you've worked leasing before, you know weekends are when serious prospects show up. If this is your first leasing role, adjust your expectations early and you'll be fine.

What You Bring and Where It Takes You

  • Strong written and verbal communication, you're comfortable on the phone, in person, and in a caption
  • A genuine interest in helping people find a place to live, not just closing for the sake of it
  • Comfort with CRM tools, property management software, and social platforms
  • A professional presentation and the kind of energy that puts a hesitant prospect at ease
  • Sales instincts, meaning you can read where someone is in their decision and respond to that, not just recite features

The salary range for this role sits between $51,500 and $55,000, with additional bonus opportunities tied to performance. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

Here's the career angle worth considering. Multi-site experience compounds faster than single-property experience. You see more traffic, more objection types, more closing scenarios, and you learn how different communities position themselves against each other. Leasing consultants who've worked across multiple assets tend to move into Assistant Manager or Leasing Manager roles with a stronger operational foundation than those who've only worked one door. At a company like Bozzuto, which manages a significant portfolio across the mid-Atlantic and Northeast, internal mobility is a real path, not just a recruiting line.

job.title: Multi-Site Maintenance Manager
job.description:
Picture this: it's 7 a.m. on a Tuesday, and you're walking two properties before your techs clock in. You're checking mechanical rooms, logging what needs attention, and mentally prioritizing the punch list for three turns scheduled to deliver by Friday. That's the rhythm of this role.

What This Role Actually Covers

Bozzuto is hiring a Multi-Site Maintenance Manager based in Santa Monica to lead maintenance operations across multiple residential communities. This isn't a wrench-turning position. It's a leadership role where your technical depth earns you credibility, but your ability to build and develop a team determines how well the properties actually run.

You'll own the full make-ready process, ensuring turns move efficiently so units can be leased without delay. Preventative maintenance programs, annual building systems inspections, capital improvement projects, and vendor management all fall under your scope. You're also responsible for keeping the operation compliant with applicable building, county, and safety codes, and running an accident-free shop. The on-call rotation is real. Emergency coverage is 24/7, and you'll need to be available when situations arise.

On the technical side, Bozzuto expects advanced proficiency in HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. Exposure to complex mechanical systems including central plant, boilers, chillers, and split systems is a genuine requirement here, not a nice-to-have. Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting round out what you're expected to handle or confidently supervise.

What You'll Need to Bring

  • 7 or more years in facilities or residential building maintenance, with a trades education background
  • At least 3 years of supervisory experience, including hiring, developing, and retaining maintenance staff
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of OSHA, MSDS, and risk management practices
  • Comfort with technology platforms, Microsoft Office, and property management apps
  • Physical capability to frequently handle 25 to 50 pounds of force, climb ladders, and work in varying weather conditions

The salary range runs from $82,000 to $100,000, with additional bonus opportunities. Bozzuto's benefits package includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What separates strong candidates from average ones in a multi-site role like this is the ability to triage across locations without letting any single property drift. The managers who thrive here build systems, not dependency. They train their techs to handle the routine so they can focus on the complex, the capital projects, and the vendor relationships that protect NOI long-term. If you've been running a single property and you're ready to scale your impact, this is a logical and meaningful step up.

job.title: Multi-Site Maintenance Technician ($2,500 Sign-On Bonus)
job.description:
It's Tuesday morning and you're pulling into Atlantic Station before most residents have had their coffee. There's a work order for a leaking fixture in one unit, a vacant apartment that needs punch list items cleared before a Friday move-in, and a heads-up from the overnight on-call log about a heating complaint. That's a pretty normal start to the day in this role.

What This Role Actually Involves

Bozzuto is hiring a Multi-Site Maintenance Technician to support two connected properties: Atlantic Station and Atlantic Station West in Stamford, CT. You'll split your time across both sites handling service ticket resolution, preventative maintenance, and the turn-over work that gets vacant units ready for incoming residents. The core technical work spans HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair at a beginner-to-intermediate level. You don't need to be a master of every trade, but you do need a genuine foundation and the drive to build on it.

Beyond the mechanical work, you're also part of how residents experience the community. Grounds upkeep, snow removal, common area cleanliness, and showing up professionally for every interaction all matter here. Weekend availability and participation in an on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies are part of the deal. That's worth knowing upfront. On-call is real, and reliable personal transportation is a hard requirement for responding when something comes up outside business hours.

The salary range for this position is $47,000 to $50,000, and there's a $2,500 sign-on bonus attached. Additional bonus opportunities exist beyond that.

What You Bring

  • Hands-on experience in building or residential maintenance, or a trades education background
  • Working knowledge of HVAC, plumbing, electrical systems, and appliance repair (beginner to intermediate)
  • Reliable personal transportation for commuting and on-call response
  • Physical capability to frequently lift and move 25 to 50 pounds
  • Comfort using software, apps, and basic computer programs for work order management
  • Strong communication skills and a professional, service-oriented approach

One thing that separates strong candidates in this kind of multi-site role: the ability to self-manage your time across two properties without needing someone to tell you where the priority is. When you're splitting days between Atlantic Station and Atlantic Station West, you'll regularly have to triage competing demands. Technicians who thrive here treat the work order queue like a living document, not a to-do list they'll get to eventually.

Bozzuto also offers a tangible growth incentive for this position. After your one-year anniversary, the company will cover the cost of your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification. That's a real credential that opens doors to higher-level HVAC work and carries weight across the industry. Combined with tuition reimbursement and a 401k with company match, the benefits package reflects a company that's thinking beyond just filling the role.

job.title: Overnight Concierge
job.description:
Luxury residential properties at the mid-rise and high-rise level live and die by the quality of their resident experience. The overnight shift is where that experience either holds or falls apart. Bozzuto's Overnight Concierge role at their Reston, VA community exists precisely because residents coming home at midnight deserve the same level of attention as those walking in at noon.

This is a Monday through Friday position, 11pm to 7am. That detail matters. It's a consistent schedule, which is genuinely rare in hospitality-adjacent roles, and it means you're owning the overnight hours for a community that expects a lot from its building staff. You're the face people see when they get home from a late flight or a long shift. You're handling package management (which gets serious during the holidays), coordinating freight elevator reservations, keeping front desk logs clean, and managing clubroom bookings. The administrative side is real and it requires someone who finds satisfaction in precision, not just in conversation.

What the Day-to-Day Actually Looks Like

Overnight concierge work has a rhythm that's different from daytime front desk roles. Traffic is lighter but the interactions are more personal. Residents who come home late tend to remember who greeted them by name, who already knew their dog's name, and who quietly solved a problem without making them feel like a burden. You're also expected to plan and execute resident events, manage the community's social media presence, and encourage satisfied residents to leave reviews. That's a broader scope than most overnight roles carry, and it's worth noting if you're comparing opportunities.

Bozzuto is looking for someone with at least two years in hospitality, retail, travel, or hotel environments. Strong written and verbal communication skills matter here, as does comfort with software and apps used for building operations. The ability to lift up to 25 pounds rounds out the physical requirements.

Where This Role Can Take You

Concierge experience at a company like Bozzuto is a real foundation. The skills you build here, reading residents, managing competing priorities calmly, keeping a community feeling cohesive, transfer directly into leasing, assistant management, and eventually community management. Many strong leasing consultants and assistant managers came up through concierge and front desk roles because they already understood how residents think and what they actually value. If you want to move into property operations long-term, this is a legitimate on-ramp, not a dead end.

  • 2+ years in hospitality, hotel, retail, or travel
  • Strong written and verbal communication
  • Comfort with software, apps, and computer programs
  • Detail-oriented with an instinct for anticipating needs
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds
  • Professional appearance and customer-first mindset

The salary for this position is $48,000 annually, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Package Handler (Sunday-Thursday, 11:30a - 7:30p)
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role is simpler to describe than it is to execute well. You're receiving, logging, and delivering packages to residents at a Bozzuto community in Roselle, NJ, working a Sunday through Thursday shift from 11:30am to 7:30pm. That schedule matters. You're covering the window when package volume peaks and residents are home expecting their deliveries.

The physical side is real. You'll spend most of your shift on your feet, continuously lifting and carrying packages that commonly weigh anywhere from 25 to 75 pounds. Package rooms don't organize themselves, and an audit trail that's even one day behind creates problems fast. Every package gets logged, tracked, and delivered within 24 hours of receipt. That's the standard.

The resident-facing piece is where this role separates itself from a warehouse job. You're often the only Bozzuto team member a resident sees that day. A quick, genuine exchange when handing off a package does more for resident retention than most people expect. The residents who feel known and cared for are the ones who renew.

What You Bring

  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Reliable personal transportation to get yourself to the property consistently
  • Physical stamina for continuous lifting, lowering, and carrying throughout your shift
  • Comfort working independently without constant supervision while still hitting daily deadlines
  • A calm, professional manner with residents, even when they're frustrated about a missing or delayed package

Context Worth Knowing

Package volume at apartment communities has grown dramatically over the past several years and shows no sign of slowing. Many Class A and B properties now treat package management as a dedicated operational function rather than a task handed off to whoever's available at the front desk. Filling that role well means understanding that you're part of the service infrastructure, not separate from it.

Skills you build here transfer directly into leasing and resident services roles. You develop a feel for resident communication, you learn how a well-run community handles daily operations, and you build a track record with a company that promotes from within. Bozzuto is a recognized name in the mid-Atlantic property management space, and starting on the operations side gives you a grounded view of how a community actually functions day to day.

Compensation for this position falls between $50,000 and $53,000 annually, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Part-Time Resident Experience Associate (Wednesday - Friday, 12pm - 8pm)
job.description:
What This Role Actually Is

Bozzuto runs some of the more polished communities in the mid-Atlantic and Southeast, and their leasing and resident experience teams reflect that. This part-time Associate position covers Wednesday through Friday, noon to eight, which means you're carrying the afternoon and evening shift when foot traffic picks up, packages pile in, and residents are coming home from work with questions, complaints, or both. It's a front-desk-forward role with a genuine leasing component layered in.

The Day-to-Day

You're the first face people see. That sounds simple, but at a Bozzuto community it carries real weight. You'll follow up with prospects, convert those conversations into tours, and hand warm leads off to the leasing team. Occupancy goals don't happen on their own, and this role contributes directly to them.

Resident retention is the other half of the job. You'll plan and run community events, keep the clubroom and package room organized, and manage front desk logistics with the kind of obsessive attention to detail that prevents small problems from becoming big ones. Social media falls in your lap too, including nudging satisfied residents toward leaving reviews. Beyond that, you're covering for teammates, anticipating needs before residents voice them, and generally keeping the community looking and running like it should.

A few things Bozzuto specifically looks for:

  • At least two years in hospitality, retail, hotel, or travel (customer-facing experience that's trained you to read a room)
  • Strong written and verbal communication
  • Comfort with software, apps, and property management platforms
  • A detail-oriented, anticipatory mindset
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds. Package volume during the holidays is real.

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

We've seen a lot of people apply to resident experience roles who treat them as glorified receptionist positions. The ones who thrive treat every prospect interaction as a leasing opportunity and every resident interaction as a retention play. Remembering names matters. Learning a resident's dog's name matters more than people expect. The evening shift specifically rewards candidates who stay sharp and personable at 7pm on a Friday when the rest of the office has been gone for hours.

This role pays $24 to $26 per hour. Bozzuto offers medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For a part-time schedule, that's a benefits package worth taking seriously. Career-wise, Resident Experience Associates at companies like Bozzuto often move into full-time leasing consultant roles or assistant manager tracks, especially once they have a few lease conversions and a retention event or two under their belt.

The Chapel Hill market is active, university-influenced, and competitive. Residents have options. The communities that win on retention are the ones where the front desk team genuinely knows who lives there.

job.title: Porter
job.description:
Porter roles at well-run multifamily communities are more physical than people expect and more important than they get credit for. The condition of your grounds and common areas is often the first thing a prospective resident notices during a tour, and it's usually the last thing a long-term resident mentions when they renew. Bozzuto understands that, which is part of why they call this position a Community Care Associate. It's not just a title change.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

This is a grounds and common area role at a Bozzuto community in Aberdeen, MD. Day to day, you're keeping the property looking sharp: trash pickup, weed pulling, raking, watering, sweeping hallways and curbs, and checking that hall lights are working. Weekly tasks include cleaning handrails and vacuuming the pool area. Monthly and seasonal work rounds it out: hosing out hallways, power washing stairwells twice a year, and snow removal when Maryland decides to cooperate.

You'll also support the maintenance team with various tasks and painting as needed. That's worth noting because it's a real opportunity to pick up skills. Porters who pay attention during those assist jobs tend to move into maintenance tech roles faster than people realize.

  • Daily trash pickup and grounds upkeep
  • Weekly hallway sweeping, light checks, and handrail cleaning
  • Monthly hallway hosing and pool area vacuuming
  • Semi-annual stairwell power washing
  • Snow removal support as needed
  • Clubhouse and common area cleaning
  • Flier and newsletter delivery by 3:00 p.m. when requested
  • Maintenance and painting assistance

What You Need to Bring

Previous cleaning experience in multifamily or commercial settings is preferred. You need reliable personal transportation to get to the property. Physically, this role involves lifting up to 50 pounds and regular pushing, pulling, and climbing. That's not a formality. You're on your feet and moving throughout the shift.

One thing that separates solid porters from average ones: attention to detail without being told twice. Checking bandit signs daily, catching a burned-out hall light before a resident complains, noticing that a stairwell drain is clogged before it becomes a maintenance ticket. That kind of proactive eye is what property managers remember when a maintenance tech position opens up.

The Bozzuto Side of Things

Bozzuto has a real reputation in the mid-Atlantic multifamily market. They manage and develop quality communities, and they tend to invest in their people. This position comes with medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. The salary range is $42,024 to $43,000, with additional bonus opportunities available. For a porter role with a company of this caliber, that's a fair package. The path forward is there if you want it.

job.title: Property Manager
job.description:
Property management at the senior level is a craft that blends financial discipline, team leadership, and resident experience into one daily practice. This Property Manager role at Bozzuto in Washington, DC puts all three of those skills to work simultaneously, and it rewards people who are genuinely strong across all of them, not just one or two.

Skills This Role Demands From Day One

You'll need a real command of P&L management. Bozzuto expects you to own the financial performance of the property, which means reading and interpreting operating statements, managing to budget, and producing accurate financial and operational reports for clients. If you've managed NOI targets and understand the relationship between occupancy, concessions, and net revenue, that foundation matters here.

On the leadership side, this isn't a role where you manage from a distance. Bozzuto is explicit that no task is below you. You're expected to step in, assist your team, and model the behaviors you want to see. That kind of hands-on leadership style is something you either already practice or you don't. It's hard to fake at this level.

Strong competitive market awareness is also a genuine requirement. Washington, DC is a dense, competitive rental market with constant new inventory coming online across neighborhoods like NoMa, Navy Yard, and Capitol Riverfront. Knowing how to read traffic patterns, adjust positioning, and advise on marketing strategy isn't optional. It's part of running the asset.

  • 5 or more years of experience in property management, hospitality, or retail leadership
  • Four-year degree or equivalent hands-on experience at a comparable level
  • Demonstrated track record managing revenue, budgets, and client relationships
  • Proficiency with Yardi or similar property management software
  • DC Property Manager license, or the willingness to obtain one within 90 days
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills

What This Role Builds

Managing a Bozzuto community gives you exposure to third-party client reporting, which is a skill set that transfers directly into regional or portfolio-level roles. You're not just running an asset for an owner-operator. You're delivering financial transparency and performance accountability to a client, and that experience reads differently on a resume than straight in-house management.

You'll also develop your team coaching ability in a structured way. Bozzuto invests in professional development, and as the property manager, you're expected to drive that growth for your direct reports. People who sharpen this skill here often move into multi-site or regional manager roles with a clearer sense of how to build teams, not just manage them.

What Distinguishes Strong Candidates Here

The schedule includes one weekend per month with two weekdays off in exchange. That's a reasonable trade, but candidates who struggle with weekend availability tend to find it more disruptive than expected. Strong applicants come in clear-eyed about it.

Beyond schedule fit, the candidates who stand out are those who can hold a financial conversation with a client and then turn around and motivate a leasing associate in the same afternoon. The range of what this role requires in a single day is wide. Bozzuto's culture leans into that range rather than narrowing it, so the people who thrive here tend to find that variety energizing rather than exhausting.

The salary range for this position runs from $97,000 to $107,000, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Property Manager
job.description:
Bozzuto has built a reputation in multifamily that carries real weight. The company designs, builds, manages, and maintains properties with a level of operational discipline that most operators talk about but don't actually practice. This Property Manager role in Miami's 33127 zip code puts you at the center of that standard, running a community where the bar for resident experience and financial performance is genuinely high.

The job requires you to bring financial fluency on day one. You'll own P&L management, produce accurate reports for ownership clients, and track the property's performance against budget with enough detail to explain variances and propose corrections. Yardi proficiency is preferred, and a working knowledge of accounting principles isn't optional. If you've managed a T-12, presented financials to a client, and felt comfortable doing it, that's the baseline this role expects.

Sales and marketing are equally central. You're responsible for occupancy, and that means you understand the competitive set, you know what concessions the market is running, and you partner with marketing to execute strategies that convert traffic into signed leases and retain residents through renewal. Miami's rental market is dense and competitive, with strong demand but also constant pressure from new lease-up communities. Knowing your submarket cold isn't a bonus skill here. It's the job.

Leadership is the third pillar. You'll develop your team, hold them to Bozzuto's standards, and step in wherever the work needs doing. One weekend per month you'll be on-site when prospective residents are actively searching, which is the right call operationally even if it requires planning around your schedule.

What this role requires vs. what it builds

You need to arrive with at least five years of experience in property management, hospitality, or retail, along with a demonstrated track record in client relationships and team leadership. A four-year degree or equivalent experience rounds out the baseline. A real estate license is either required or something you'll need to obtain within 90 days of hire depending on applicable state rules.

What the role builds is different. Managing a Bozzuto asset sharpens your ability to present financials to sophisticated ownership groups, which is a skill that transfers directly to regional and portfolio-level roles. You'll also develop instincts around client management that most on-site managers never get exposure to until much later in their careers. Strong Property Managers at companies like Bozzuto tend to move into Regional Manager positions with a cleaner track record than peers who came up at operators with less structured reporting expectations.

  • 5+ years in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • Proven P&L and budget management experience
  • Strong client and resident relationship track record
  • Yardi or comparable property management software proficiency
  • Basic knowledge of GAAP
  • Real estate license or willingness to obtain within 90 days
  • Outstanding written and verbal communication

The salary range for this position is $82,000 to $88,000, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Property Manager - Wynwood Haus
job.description:
Wynwood Haus sits in one of Miami's most design-forward, culturally dense neighborhoods. The residents who live there chose it deliberately. They want a community that matches the energy around it, and they'll notice immediately if it doesn't. That's the context for this Property Manager role at Bozzuto.

This is a full ownership position. You're responsible for the financial performance of the property, the resident experience, the team's development, and the client relationship. Bozzuto works on a third-party management model, which means your reporting to ownership isn't a formality. Accurate, timely financial and operational reports matter here. Clients expect them, and your credibility with ownership depends on delivering them consistently.

Day to day, you're splitting your attention between the numbers and the people. That means reviewing occupancy and traffic trends in the morning, walking units or reviewing a punch list with maintenance, handling a resident escalation before lunch, and then sitting down with your leasing team to talk through conversion rates and concessions strategy in the afternoon. No two days are identical. The role rewards people who can context-switch without losing their footing.

One weekend per month is part of the schedule. That's when prospective residents are actively looking, and Bozzuto expects the manager to be present for it. You'll get two weekdays off in exchange. It's a reasonable trade, but worth knowing upfront.

What separates strong candidates here isn't just P&L experience, though you do need to be comfortable with budget management and a working knowledge of accounting principles. What separates the best candidates is how they lead teams through the unglamorous parts of the job. Rolling up your sleeves, covering gaps, coaching someone through a difficult resident interaction rather than just handling it yourself. That's the culture Bozzuto describes, and it's the culture Wynwood Haus will need.

What Bozzuto is looking for

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least five years of relevant industry experience with a clear leadership track record
  • Demonstrated experience managing revenue, budgets, and P&L
  • Proficiency with Yardi or comparable property management software
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills across residents, vendors, and ownership
  • A Florida real estate license, or the willingness to obtain one within 90 days of hire

The salary range is $82,000 to $90,000, with bonus eligibility on top of base. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. Bozzuto has a genuine reputation for investing in career development, and the Property Manager role at a high-profile asset like Wynwood Haus is the kind of position that opens doors to regional or portfolio-level roles over time.

If you're someone who takes real ownership of a community, communicates clearly with clients, and genuinely enjoys developing the people around you, this role is worth a close look.

job.title: Resident Experience Associate
job.description:
Bozzuto builds and manages properties that people genuinely want to come home to. That standard starts at the front desk, not in a leasing office or a marketing deck. The Resident Experience Associate is the first face prospects see and the consistent presence residents rely on, and that visibility shapes occupancy, retention, and the overall feel of the community in ways that show up in the numbers.

This role runs a non-traditional schedule: Wednesday through Friday from 11am to 7pm, and Saturday and Sunday from 3pm to 11pm. That's not a detail buried in the fine print. It matters. You're covering the hours when residents are actually home, when packages pile up, when someone needs a freight elevator reserved for a move-in, and when a prospect who toured online finally walks through the door. Traffic doesn't stop at 5pm, and neither do you.

Day to day, you're converting inbound leads into scheduled tours, following up with prospects who went quiet, and making sure every interaction feels personal rather than transactional. You'll coordinate with the leasing team to keep tour flow organized, manage front desk logs and package rooms with real precision, and plan resident events that actually get people off their couches. The social media piece matters too: generating authentic content and nudging happy residents toward leaving reviews is a real driver of lease-up momentum and long-term reputation.

What separates a strong candidate here isn't just warmth, it's the combination of genuine people instincts and operational follow-through. Bozzuto's properties run tight. A community that looks impeccable at 11pm on a Saturday didn't get that way by accident. The best people in this role remember names, anticipate needs before they're voiced, and treat a cluttered package room like a problem that reflects on them personally.

What You Bring

  • At least two years in hospitality, retail, hotel, or a similarly customer-facing environment
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with software, apps, and digital tools
  • Willingness to obtain a leasing license within your first 90 days
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds (holiday package volume is real)
  • A detail-oriented mindset and a genuine instinct for anticipating what people need

This role is a natural entry point into property management for someone coming out of service industries. The skills you build here, lead conversion, resident relations, event programming, social engagement, transfer directly into leasing consultant and assistant manager roles over time. Bozzuto operates across multiple markets and property types, so internal mobility is a realistic path for people who perform.

Compensation sits between $43,000 and $46,000 annually, with benefits covering medical, dental, and vision, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Resident Experience Associate (Thurs-Mon, 3:30pm-12:00am) - $1,500 Sign-On Bonus
job.description:
Evening and weekend front desk coverage at a residential community isn't just about answering phones. It's the shift when residents come home tired, packages pile up, maintenance requests get urgent, and the leasing office is closed. Someone has to own that window, and do it well.

Bozzuto is hiring a Resident Experience Associate for their Lynbrook, NY community, working Thursday through Monday, 3:30pm to midnight. The $1,500 sign-on bonus reflects the real value this shift carries. You're not a backup. You're the face of the building during the hours most residents actually live in it.

What This Role Actually Requires

This is a hospitality-forward position, not a pure leasing role, though leasing is part of it. You'll be expected to obtain a leasing license within your first 90 days. Day to day, that means converting inbound leads into scheduled tours, following up with prospects, and contributing to occupancy alongside the core leasing team.

Beyond that, the job is relationship work. Residents expect you to know them. Their names, their preferences, their pets. That kind of familiarity doesn't happen by accident. It requires consistent attention and a genuine interest in people, not just a customer service script.

  • 2 or more years in hospitality, retail, hotel, or travel environments
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with software, apps, and digital tools
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds (package volume gets real during the holidays)
  • Availability Thursday through Monday, including weekends and evenings
  • A sharp, professional presentation

You'll also manage front desk logistics: package room organization, club room reservations, freight elevator scheduling, and keeping detailed logs. It sounds administrative, but done poorly it creates friction for every resident. Done well, it's invisible, and that's the point.

What This Role Builds

Strong candidates coming from hotel front desk, concierge, or restaurant management often discover that residential property management rewards the same instincts they already have, just applied over longer resident relationships rather than single transactions. The skills you develop here transfer directly into leasing consultant and assistant manager roles. You'll learn how occupancy goals connect to daily traffic, how resident retention ties to renewal rates, and how consistent community programming supports both.
