Bozzuto's platform also means you're not learning in isolation. The company manages a large portfolio of communities, which creates real internal mobility for people who perform. The salary for this role is $45,000, with additional bonus opportunities and a benefits package that includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

If you've built a career around making people feel taken care of, this role gives you a place to apply that craft inside a company that takes its resident experience standards seriously.

job.title: Roving Leasing Consultant
job.description:
The Skills This Role Puts to Work Every Day

Leasing is a sales role, and this one is honest about that. As a Roving Leasing Consultant with Bozzuto, you're moving across a portfolio of communities in southern Connecticut (Stamford, Norwalk, Darien) and doing the core work of converting interest into signed leases. That means two distinct skill sets run in parallel here: consultative sales and adaptability. You need to walk into a new property, understand its specific competitive position in that submarket, and deliver a tour that feels personal and informed, not scripted and recycled.

The roving structure is worth understanding before you apply. Unlike a site-based leasing role where you learn one community deeply, this position asks you to hold multiple properties in your head at once. Prospects ask detailed questions. Parking, commute, pet policies, local coffee shops. You learn to get up to speed quickly on each asset and communicate its value with genuine conviction. That's a real skill, and it's one that transfers directly to portfolio-level roles later in your career.

Social media content creation is listed as a requirement, not a bonus. Bozzuto expects you to generate awareness and engagement on platforms like Instagram and Facebook. If you've only consumed content, this role will push you to produce it consistently and with a distinct voice for each community's brand.

What You're Responsible For

  • Following up with all prospects and converting leads into scheduled tours across your assigned properties
  • Conducting personalized, neighborhood-aware tours that move prospects toward a leasing decision
  • Building resident loyalty through consistent, high-quality interactions that extend beyond the initial lease signing
  • Creating and posting social media content that reflects each community's identity
  • Working three weekends per month, with two weekdays off in exchange
  • Obtaining a Connecticut real estate license within 90 days of hire if you don't already hold one

What Strong Candidates Bring, and What This Role Builds

The candidates who stand out in this kind of role already have strong written and verbal communication skills, a genuine comfort with sales conversations, and enough tech fluency to move between leasing platforms and social tools without friction. A sharp, professional presence matters here because first impressions at a tour carry real weight.

What the role builds is equally valuable. Roving positions accelerate your ability to read a property quickly, adapt your pitch, and manage your own time across multiple sites without a single manager watching your schedule. Consultants who do well here tend to move into senior leasing, assistant manager, or lease-up specialist roles faster than peers who spent the same time at a single community. The breadth of exposure is a genuine career asset.

Bozzuto manages a significant portfolio of Class A communities across the Mid-Atlantic and Northeast, and the Stamford market sits in one of the more competitive suburban corridors in Connecticut. Renters in this area are often evaluating multiple high-quality options and commuting to New York. Knowing how to speak to that lifestyle directly, not just recite square footage and amenities, is what separates a good tour from a signed lease.

job.title: Roving Leasing Consultant
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

This isn't a single-property leasing role. As a Roving Leasing Consultant with Bozzuto, you're covering a portfolio of communities across Greater Boston, which means your week looks different depending on where traffic is strongest and where support is needed. One day you're conducting personalized tours at a community in Quincy, the next you're following up on leads for a different property entirely. You own the full leasing cycle: converting inquiries into tours, turning tours into signed leases, and making sure prospects feel genuinely welcomed at every touchpoint.

Weekend availability is real here. Three weekends per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. That's when prospective residents are actively searching, so that's when you're present. You'll also manage social media content across platforms like Instagram and Facebook, creating material that reflects each community's character rather than generic apartment marketing.

The roving structure is worth understanding before you apply. You're not bouncing around because the company can't staff properly. You're a specialist who brings consistent leasing energy and technique to properties that need it, whether during a lease-up push, a seasonal traffic spike, or a staffing gap. That's a different skill set than being planted at one address.

What You Bring

  • A genuine interest in people and the ability to read what a prospect actually needs from a conversation
  • Strong written and verbal communication, including the ability to craft social media content that doesn't feel templated
  • Comfort with software, apps, and leasing platforms (Bozzuto operates at a high-tech standard)
  • Sales instincts without the pressure-sales feel. Residents notice the difference.
  • Professional presentation and the adaptability to represent multiple communities credibly
  • Reliable transportation and flexibility to move across the Greater Boston portfolio

Where This Role Takes You

Roving roles build something that single-property experience can't: exposure to how different communities operate, what drives traffic at each, and how to close in varied environments. Consultants who come through roving positions often move into Senior Leasing Consultant roles or property-level Assistant Manager positions faster than their peers, because they've already seen how multiple sites handle occupancy challenges and resident retention. You're also building a portfolio of social content and leasing metrics across properties, which is exactly the kind of track record that makes a future promotion conversation easy to have.

Bozzuto manages a well-regarded portfolio in the Boston metro, and this role puts you inside that network from day one. The salary range sits between $51,500 and $53,000, with bonus potential on top of that, plus benefits covering medical, dental, and vision, 20 days of PTO, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement if you're thinking about long-term growth.

job.title: Roving Leasing Consultant
job.description:
Leasing is a skill set, not just a job title. The best consultants blend genuine curiosity about people with a disciplined sales process, and they do it across multiple properties without losing momentum or consistency. That's exactly what this Roving Leasing Consultant role at Bozzuto requires.

The Skills This Role Runs On

You'll spend a meaningful portion of your week converting inbound and outbound prospect contacts into scheduled tours, which means your written communication has to be just as sharp as your in-person presence. A compelling follow-up message is often what separates a signed lease from a lost lead. You also need a real comfort with social media, not just scrolling, but creating content on platforms like Instagram and Facebook that builds genuine interest in a community before a prospect ever steps through the door.

The "roving" part of this title matters. You're supporting a portfolio of properties across the Miami area, which means you adapt quickly. Different communities have different personalities, price points, and resident profiles. Showing one property in the morning and another in the afternoon requires you to shift your pitch, your knowledge base, and your energy. Inconsistency is the one thing you can't afford.

Sales aptitude is listed as a requirement, and it's worth taking seriously. This isn't a role where you wait for warm traffic to walk in and sell itself. You're actively working leads, following up, and guiding prospects toward a decision. The social component and the sales component aren't separate skills here. They reinforce each other.

What You'll Actually Be Doing

  • Following up with prospects and converting leads into community tours across properties in the Miami region
  • Conducting personalized, informative apartment tours that move prospects toward a leasing decision
  • Creating and managing social media content to generate awareness and interest in Bozzuto communities
  • Maintaining a high-quality resident experience that builds long-term brand loyalty
  • Working three weekends per month, with two weekday days off in exchange
  • Obtaining a Florida real estate license within 90 days of hire if you don't already hold one

The weekend schedule is worth acknowledging honestly. That's when most prospective residents are actively looking, so it's a real business need, not optional. The weekday flexibility is a real trade-off, not just a consolation. If you prefer a traditional Monday through Friday structure, this role isn't the right fit. If you don't mind working when traffic is highest and having quieter weekdays off, the rhythm works well.

What's Distinctive About Doing This at Bozzuto

Bozzuto operates across a wide range of communities with a reputation built on the resident experience side of property management, not just the transaction. For a leasing consultant, that context matters. You're not just closing leases. You're setting the tone for how a resident feels about where they live from day one. The properties you'll support aren't interchangeable boxes. They're meant to feel like distinct places, and your job is to communicate that authentically.

For someone early in a property management career, the roving structure is genuinely valuable. Exposure to multiple communities builds pattern recognition faster than staying in one place. You learn what makes a strong lease-up, what drives delinquency in one market segment versus another, and how to read traffic and adjust your approach. That experience transfers directly into roles like Leasing Manager or Assistant Community Manager when you're ready to step up.

The compensation range sits between $50,000 and $51,000 annually, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Roving Leasing Consultant
job.description:
Roving leasing roles exist because no single property in a portfolio always needs the same level of leasing support. Traffic spikes, staff vacations, new lease-ups, and seasonal demand shifts all create gaps. Bozzuto fills those gaps with consultants who can step into any community, learn it quickly, and convert prospects without missing a beat. That's the job in a nutshell.

This position covers Durham, Chapel Hill, and Raleigh. You're not planted at one address. You move across the Triangle market, which means you'll develop a working familiarity with multiple communities, different resident profiles, and varying price points. That breadth is genuinely useful for your career, even if it asks more of you day to day.

What You'll Actually Be Doing

Most of your time breaks into two categories: follow-up and tours. You're converting leads into scheduled visits, then turning those visits into signed leases. That sounds simple, but doing it well at multiple properties requires you to internalize each community's unique selling points quickly. You won't have the luxury of learning one floor plan set over six months. You'll need to get sharp fast, then shift when the next property needs you.

Weekends are part of the schedule. Three per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. That's when prospects are actively searching, and Bozzuto wants someone present and prepared to meet them. Social media content creation is also part of the role, specifically on platforms like Facebook and Instagram, so comfort in front of a camera or behind a caption matters here.

What This Role Requires vs. What It Builds

You need to bring a few things on day one:

  • Strong written and verbal communication, not polished corporate speak, but genuine conversational ability
  • Comfort with technology: leasing software, apps, and social platforms
  • A professional presentation and real sales instinct
  • A valid real estate license, or the willingness to obtain one within 90 days where required
  • The flexibility to travel across the Durham, Chapel Hill, and Raleigh markets regularly

What this role builds is harder to put on a list. Roving consultants develop pattern recognition faster than site-based leasing staff. When you've toured prospects at a Class A mid-rise in one neighborhood and a garden-style community in another, you start to see what objections are property-specific versus what's happening across the whole market. That kind of perspective is exactly what prepares someone to step into a Leasing Manager or Assistant Manager role. Operators notice consultants who can perform consistently across multiple assets.

The salary range for this position is $46,000 to $48,000, with additional bonus opportunities on top of base pay. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Roving Leasing Consultant ($2,000 Sign-On Bonus)
job.description:
Bozzuto is hiring a Roving Leasing Consultant to cover a portfolio of communities across Southern Connecticut, including Norwalk, Darien, and Stamford. The role comes with a $2,000 sign-on bonus and a base salary range of $53,000 to $57,000, plus additional bonus opportunities tied to performance.

This isn't a single-property leasing seat. You're moving between communities, which means your ability to read a prospect quickly, adapt your pitch to different unit types and price points, and close without the luxury of deep familiarity is what actually matters. On any given day you might be following up on digital leads at one property in the morning and conducting personalized tours at another community by the afternoon. Traffic converts when the follow-up is fast and the tour feels personal. That's the job.

Weekends are a core part of the schedule. Three weekends per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. If you've worked leasing before, you know weekends are when serious prospects show up. That's not a downside here; it's where the conversion happens.

One requirement worth flagging: this role calls for 1 to 2 years of experience with affordable housing compliance and documentation under 8-30g, which is Connecticut's affordable housing statute. If you've worked with income-qualified units, tenant eligibility files, or deed-restricted communities in CT, that background is directly relevant. Bozzuto also requires either an active Connecticut real estate license or a commitment to obtaining one within 90 days of hire.

Social media is part of the leasing toolkit here. Generating awareness and interest through platforms like Instagram and Facebook is an actual expectation, not a footnote.

  • 1 to 2 years of experience with 8-30g affordable housing compliance in Connecticut
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Active real estate license or ability to obtain one within 90 days
  • Comfort with social media content creation for leasing purposes
  • Sales aptitude and a track record of converting prospects to leases
  • Proficiency with leasing software, apps, and standard computer programs
  • Reliable transportation to travel between properties across the portfolio

The Southern CT corridor is a competitive rental market. Stamford in particular draws renters who are comparing Class A product and weighing commute times to New York. Strong consultants in this region know how to sell the neighborhood, not just the unit, and they close because they build genuine rapport fast. The roving structure accelerates that skill set considerably. Consultants who succeed in portfolio-based roles tend to develop a sharper instincts around prospect qualification and pipeline management than their single-property peers, which positions them well for future roles in leasing management or community management.

Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Roving Leasing Consultant - Alexandria, VA
job.description:
Leasing is a skill-heavy role that most job postings understate. This one leans on three in particular: reading people quickly, communicating across channels without losing your personality, and staying sharp on a rotating schedule across multiple properties. If any of those feel like natural strengths, keep reading.

What This Role Actually Requires

Bozzuto is hiring a Roving Leasing Consultant to work across a portfolio of apartment communities in Alexandria, VA. "Roving" means you're not anchored to one property. You move where the need is, which keeps the work varied but also demands that you get up to speed fast on different communities, different floor plans, and different resident profiles. You can't rely on routine. You have to be genuinely curious about each property and able to pitch it convincingly within a short window.

The core of the job is converting inbound leads into tours and tours into signed leases. That means prompt follow-up with prospects, personalized outreach, and tour experiences that go beyond walking someone through a unit. Strong leasing consultants ask questions and listen. They match the apartment to the person, not the other way around. You'll also manage social media content for the communities, which means Instagram and Facebook posts that actually reflect the neighborhood and the lifestyle, not generic stock-photo content.

On the schedule: you'll work three weekends per month, taking two weekdays off in exchange. Weekend traffic is when serious prospects show up, so this isn't negotiable. If you've worked leasing before, you already know this. If it's new information, it's worth sitting with before you apply.

Skills That Carry Weight Here

  • Confident, clear communication in person and in writing. Prospects will reach out by text, email, and phone. Your response quality matters.
  • Comfort with social media content creation, not just scrolling. You're generating awareness and interest, which requires some creativity and consistency.
  • Professional presentation. You represent the brand at the door before you say a word.
  • Adaptability across properties. Each community has its own identity, pricing, and amenity mix. You need to internalize those differences quickly.
  • Sales instinct without the pushy energy. Prospects respond to consultants who genuinely help them, not ones running through a script.

What's Distinctive About Bozzuto

Bozzuto has a real reputation in the mid-Atlantic market. They design, build, and manage their own communities, which means the product tends to be well-considered and the operations teams are integrated rather than siloed. For a leasing consultant, that matters because you're selling something the company actually believes in. You're not trying to paper over a bad product with a good pitch.

The roving structure is also worth noting as a career angle. Consultants who work across a portfolio see more of the business faster. You encounter different lease-up situations, different occupancy challenges, and different resident demographics in a compressed timeframe. That breadth tends to accelerate growth into senior leasing or assistant manager roles faster than staying in a single community for two years.

The salary range is $50,000 to $52,000, with bonus opportunities on top. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Roving Leasing Consultant - Sarasota County

Relationship-building sits at the center of this work. You're converting prospect inquiries into scheduled tours, then turning those tours into signed leases. Written communication matters too. You'll be creating and managing social media content across platforms like Instagram and Facebook, so a good eye for what resonates with an audience is genuinely useful here, not just a checkbox.

That means adapting quickly to different floor plans, different resident demographics, and different team cultures without missing a beat. During the week, you're managing your lead pipeline, following up on inquiries, posting content, and making sure the resident experience stays consistent even after the lease is signed. That last part matters more than people realize. Resident retention starts at move-in, not at renewal.

The salary range for this role runs from $46,000 to $50,000, with bonus opportunities on top of that. Bozzuto also offers health coverage, paid time off, 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement, which is worth noting if you're thinking about where this role can take you.

What's Distinctive About This Role at Bozzuto

Bozzuto operates across a range of community types and markets, and the people who do well there tend to be genuinely curious about residents as individuals, not just as applicants to process. The roving structure also gives you broader exposure than a single-site leasing role would. If you're building toward a leasing manager or assistant manager position, working across multiple properties accelerates your understanding of how different communities operate, what drives occupancy, and how to adapt your approach to different audiences. That breadth is hard to get when you're stationed at one address.

If you're someone who gets energized by human connection, can hold a conversation with a retiree looking to downsize and a young professional looking for their first apartment in the same afternoon, and you want to grow in property management rather than just pass through it, this role is worth a serious look.

job.title: Roving Maintenance Technician - Northern Virginia ($2,500 Sign-On Bonus)
job.description:
What This Role Actually Is

A roving maintenance technician position works differently from a site-based role. You're not tied to one community. Instead, you move across a portfolio of Bozzuto properties in Northern Virginia, filling coverage gaps, supporting turns, and handling service tickets wherever the need is greatest that day. It requires more self-direction than a typical maintenance tech job, and it exposes you to a wider variety of building systems and management styles faster than staying put at a single asset would.

Bozzuto operates a managed portfolio that spans residential communities across the Mid-Atlantic, and their Northern Virginia footprint includes well-maintained communities where resident experience is taken seriously. This is a company that tracks service quality carefully. Your work will be visible.

What the Day-to-Day Looks Like

The core of this role is service ticket resolution across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. You'll also prep vacant units for move-in, handling the turn process from punch list items through final make-ready inspection. Some days you're doing preventative maintenance and grounds upkeep. Some days you're responding to after-hours emergencies on call rotation. The roving nature means you need reliable personal transportation and the flexibility to be somewhere new without much notice.

  • Beginner to intermediate proficiency in HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Physical capability to frequently move 25 to 50 pounds
  • Availability for an on-call rotation including evenings and weekends
  • Reliable personal vehicle for travel between Northern Virginia properties
  • Basic computer skills for work order software and communication
  • Strong verbal and written communication with residents and site teams

The $2,500 sign-on bonus reflects real demand for this coverage role. Bozzuto will also pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification after your first year, which is a meaningful credential that expands your HVAC scope of work and your earning potential.

Where This Role Takes You

Roving tech experience builds something that site-based roles don't always develop: adaptability. You learn to read a property quickly, work with teams you didn't train alongside, and prioritize without a supervisor walking you through it. Those skills translate directly into lead maintenance, maintenance supervisor, and eventually facilities or property operations roles.

Technicians who do well in roving positions tend to develop a broader mechanical vocabulary than their peers. When you've worked on fifteen different unit types across multiple communities, diagnosing unfamiliar equipment stops being a problem. That range is exactly what companies look for when promoting into senior maintenance or portfolio-level roles. If you're early in your trades career and want to compress your learning curve, this structure does that faster than most.

The salary range sits at $50,000 to $51,000, with additional bonus opportunities beyond the sign-on. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement for continued development.

job.title: Roving Maintenance Technician - Washington, DC
job.description:
Roving maintenance roles are genuinely different from site-based positions, and not every technician thrives in them. You're not anchored to one property. You move between communities across the Washington, DC market, which means your days shift based on where coverage is needed, what tickets are piling up, and which buildings are running short-handed.

What You'll Actually Be Doing

The work itself covers the full range of residential maintenance: HVAC troubleshooting, plumbing repairs, electrical work, appliance fixes, and turn work on vacant units to get them ready for move-in. You'll also handle general upkeep, grounds maintenance, snow removal, and the kind of daily walkthroughs that keep a building looking sharp and safe.

Because this is a roving position, you need your own reliable transportation. You'll be traveling between Bozzuto-managed communities rather than reporting to a single address each morning. That also means you're joining an on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies. If a pipe bursts at 11pm or an HVAC unit fails on a July weekend, you're one of the people who responds. That's the honest part of this job that doesn't always show up in the headline.

What Bozzuto Is Looking For

  • Hands-on experience in building or residential maintenance, or a background in the trades
  • Beginner to intermediate skills across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Reliable personal transportation and availability for on-call response
  • Ability to handle physical demands, including regularly lifting and moving 25 to 50 pounds
  • Comfort with basic computer programs and property management apps
  • Strong communication and a professional appearance, since you're interacting with residents directly

The salary range sits between $52,000 and $55,000 annually, with additional bonus opportunities on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

One Thing Worth Knowing

After your first year with Bozzuto, they'll cover the cost of your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification. For technicians who are still building out their credentials, that's a real benefit. EPA 608 Universal is the ceiling-level refrigerant certification, and having it on your resume opens doors to higher-paying HVAC-specific roles down the line. It signals that Bozzuto isn't just filling a slot here. They're investing in where you go next.

The roving structure also builds something a site-based role often doesn't: exposure to multiple property types, different maintenance teams, and varying building systems. Technicians who work roving positions tend to develop faster because the variety forces adaptability. If you eventually want to move into a lead tech or maintenance supervisor role, this kind of cross-portfolio experience is hard to replicate.

job.title: Roving Overnight Concierge

What Makes Someone Stand Out

  • 2 or more years in hospitality, hotel, retail, or travel , somewhere you learned how to read people quickly and respond with composure
  • Strong written and verbal communication , you're writing emails, logging incidents, and talking to residents in the same shift
  • Comfort with software, apps, and property management tools

  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds (package volume gets real during the holidays)

The candidates who do well in this role long-term tend to be the ones who take ownership without being told to. They notice when something's off in the lobby before a resident mentions it. They follow up. The salary for this position ranges from $43,000 to $44,720, with bonus opportunities included. Bozzuto also offers medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

For someone early in a property management career, the concierge role is a strong entry point. It builds resident relations instincts and operational awareness that translate directly into leasing, assistant manager, and community manager roles down the line.

job.title: Sales & Marketing Associate
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role lives at the intersection of sales and hospitality. Your day starts with lead follow-up: responding to inquiries, qualifying prospects, and scheduling tours. Not just confirming appointments, but genuinely warming people up before they walk through the door. When they arrive, you're conducting personalized tours that connect the community's features to what that specific person actually needs in a home.

Closing a lease here isn't a hard sell. It's the result of a conversation that felt real. You'll track your pipeline, follow up with prospects who didn't lease on the first visit, and work to understand what's holding them back. Between tours, you're building the community's presence on social platforms like Instagram and Facebook, creating content that reflects the neighborhood and lifestyle, not just floor plans and pricing.

The schedule is honest about one thing: weekends matter in leasing. That's when most people are actually out looking. You'll work three weekends per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. If you've worked retail or hospitality, this rhythm will feel familiar.

What You Bring

  • A customer-first instinct that shows up consistently, not just when the traffic is high
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, because you're selling through email threads and in-person tours equally
  • Comfort with social media content creation, specifically on Instagram and Facebook
  • Sales aptitude and the ability to read a conversation and adjust your approach
  • Proficiency with software, apps, and property management platforms (or a quick learning curve)
  • A real estate license, or the willingness to obtain one within 90 days of hire where required
  • A polished, professional presentation that reflects the community you represent

Context Worth Knowing

Bozzuto operates across property management, development, and construction, which means this leasing role sits inside a larger organization with real internal mobility. Sales and Marketing Associates who build a strong track record here often move into senior leasing roles, assistant manager positions, or community manager tracks. The skills you develop, specifically converting leads, managing a prospect pipeline, and building resident relationships, transfer directly into any asset class or market you work in later.

What separates strong candidates from average ones in this role isn't just closing ability. It's consistency between visits. The prospects who don't lease on the first tour are often the ones who come back if the follow-up felt genuine rather than scripted. That instinct for follow-through, combined with the ability to create social content that actually reflects a community's personality, is what Bozzuto is looking for at this level.

Compensation for this position ranges from $54,000 to $59,500 annually, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Sales & Marketing Associate - Summer 2026 Start
job.description:
Bozzuto has built a reputation as one of the more respected names in multifamily management, particularly across the mid-Atlantic. This Sales and Marketing Associate role sits at the front line of that reputation. You're the person a prospective resident meets first, and in most cases, the reason they sign or don't sign.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

This is a leasing-focused role with a real marketing dimension. You're not just showing apartments. You're managing a lead pipeline, following up with prospects who came through the website or a third-party ILS, and turning those conversations into tours. Then you're turning tours into leases. The social media piece is genuine, not an afterthought. Bozzuto expects you to create content for platforms like Instagram and Facebook that reflects the community's personality, not just repost corporate graphics.

The schedule is worth understanding before you apply. Three weekends per month is standard in leasing because that's when traffic peaks. You'll get two days off during the week to compensate, but if your social life runs on Saturdays, this is worth thinking through honestly.

The role has a summer 2026 start, so Bozzuto is hiring ahead of their leasing season. That timing matters. You'd be onboarding before peak traffic hits, which actually gives you a better runway to learn the community before things get busy.

What You Bring

  • Strong written and verbal communication. Leasing conversations happen in person, by phone, and over email. All three matter.
  • Comfort with social media content creation, specifically Instagram and Facebook, not just personal use but audience-aware posting
  • A professional presentation and genuine ease with people you've just met
  • Basic tech fluency across software, apps, and property management tools
  • A real estate license, or the willingness to get one within 90 days of starting (where state law requires it)
  • A customer-first mindset that comes naturally, not just as a performance

Team Fit and Where This Can Go

What separates strong candidates from average ones here isn't charm. It's follow-through. The leads that convert are often the ones where someone called back twice, sent a thoughtful email, and remembered what the prospect said about their commute. Bozzuto tracks that kind of detail in how they evaluate performance.

This role is also a genuine entry point into a property management career. Leasing associates who develop strong closing skills and learn to read traffic patterns and occupancy trends often move into assistant manager or community manager roles within two to three years. The marketing component here, specifically social media content and brand consistency, also builds skills that translate directly into regional marketing coordinator positions if that direction appeals to you more than operations.

Compensation is between $48,000 and $52,000 annually, with bonus potential on top of base. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. The Alexandria location puts you in a competitive multifamily market where Bozzuto operates several communities, which means internal mobility is realistic if you perform well.

job.title: Sales & Marketing Associate - Summer 2026 Start
job.description:
Bozzuto's leasing roles are worth paying attention to. The company manages a substantial portfolio of Class A communities and has built a reputation for resident experience that goes well beyond the basics. This Sales and Marketing Associate position, based in Chicago and slated for a summer 2026 start, sits right at the intersection of leasing performance and brand stewardship.

What This Role Actually Involves

The core of the job is converting interest into signed leases. That means following up with prospects, providing genuinely useful information about the community and surrounding neighborhood, and conducting tours that feel personal rather than scripted. You're not reading from a brochure. You're reading the person in front of you and adjusting accordingly.

Beyond tours and traffic, you'll also carry some responsibility for social media content. Instagram and Facebook presence matters for lease-up momentum and for keeping the community top of mind with people who are still in research mode. It's a real part of the job, not an afterthought.

Weekend availability is required. Three weekends per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. That's the standard trade-off in leasing, and it's worth knowing upfront rather than being surprised by it later.

  • Follow up with all inbound leads and guide prospects through the decision process
  • Lead personalized apartment tours with the goal of closing new leases
  • Maintain a consistent, high-quality resident experience that builds long-term retention
  • Create and manage social media content across platforms
  • Obtain a real estate license within 90 days of hire where required by state law

What You Should Bring

Strong written and verbal communication skills are genuinely important here, not just listed as filler. You'll be writing follow-up emails, responding to inquiries, and producing social content, all while conducting in-person tours and handling objections in real time. The ability to shift registers between a quick Instagram caption and a thoughtful prospect email is a real skill this role develops.

Sales aptitude matters, but what separates strong candidates from average ones in a role like this is curiosity about people. The best leasing consultants ask better questions than their peers. They find out what's actually driving a prospect's search and match the community's strengths to that specific need. That's harder to train than product knowledge.

Where This Role Can Take You

Leasing associate roles at well-managed companies like Bozzuto are a legitimate entry point into property management careers. The skills you build here, closing, relationship management, social marketing, objection handling, transfer directly into senior leasing roles, Assistant Manager positions, and eventually Community Manager tracks. Companies that invest in training and promote from within tend to be the ones where this path accelerates fastest. Bozzuto has a tuition reimbursement program and internal development resources, which signals they're thinking about retention past year one.

The salary range for this position is $48,000 to $54,500, with bonus eligibility on top of base. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, and a 401(k) with a company match.

job.title: Sales & Marketing Associate - Summer 2026 Start
job.description:
Bozzuto runs some of the most design-forward, resident-focused communities in the country, and their leasing roles reflect that standard. This Sales and Marketing Associate position in Charlotte is a Summer 2026 start, which makes it worth thinking about now if you're building toward a long-term career in property management or multifamily marketing.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

This isn't a role where you sit at a desk and wait for walk-ins. You're working the full leasing cycle: following up with prospects, converting inquiries into tours, and closing leases through genuine conversations rather than high-pressure scripts. Bozzuto's communities tend to attract residents who care about quality, so your ability to speak knowledgeably about the neighborhood, the building, and the lifestyle matters as much as your closing instincts.

Social media is a real part of the job here, not an afterthought. You'll be expected to generate content on platforms like Instagram and Facebook that actually builds awareness and interest in the community. If you've done this well before, in any context, that experience is directly transferable.

If you haven't worked a leasing schedule before, it's worth knowing upfront: weekends are your prime time, and the best leasing consultants plan around that rather than resist it.

  • Convert inbound leads into scheduled tours through thoughtful, timely follow-up

  • Obtain a real estate license within 90 days of hire if required in your market

What Strong Candidates Bring

The posted qualifications are fairly standard: communication skills, a professional appearance, comfort with software, social media fluency. What actually separates strong candidates at a company like Bozzuto is something harder to list. It's the ability to read a prospect quickly and adjust your tour accordingly. It's writing a follow-up email that doesn't sound like a template. It's understanding that every interaction either builds or erodes brand loyalty, and treating it that way consistently.

Sales aptitude matters, but not the pushy kind. Bozzuto's culture leans toward hospitality, so candidates who come from retail, hotel, or food service backgrounds often transition well into this type of role. The instinct to make someone feel genuinely welcome is the foundation everything else gets built on.

Where This Role Leads

A leasing associate position at a company like Bozzuto is a real starting point, not a placeholder. Consultants who perform well here typically move into senior leasing roles, then into assistant manager positions where they start touching occupancy strategy, delinquency management, and budgeting. The marketing component of this specific role, especially the social media work, is increasingly valuable as properties compete harder for qualified traffic. That's a skill set that compounds across every future role you take in multifamily. The salary range listed is $45,000 to $50,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that.

job.title: Sales & Marketing Associate - Summer 2026 Start
job.description:
Some roles in property management are primarily operational. This one is primarily conversational. The Sales and Marketing Associate position at Bozzuto rewards people who can read a prospect in the first two minutes of a phone call, shift their pitch accordingly, and still follow up three days later with something worth reading. If that sounds like your natural rhythm, keep going.

What This Role Actually Demands

The core skill here is lead conversion. You're turning inquiries into tours, and tours into signed leases. That requires more than friendliness. It requires discipline around follow-up, an instinct for what information actually moves a prospect closer to a decision, and the ability to present the same community in genuinely different ways depending on who's standing in front of you.

Social media is a real part of the job, not a checkbox. Bozzuto wants someone who can generate organic interest through platforms like Instagram and Facebook, which means creating content that actually connects with people who are apartment shopping. Generic posts don't move the needle. You'll need a feel for what looks authentic versus what looks like a property management company trying too hard.

Weekend availability is built into the schedule. Three weekends per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. That trade-off works well for people who prefer lighter midweek schedules, but it's worth being honest with yourself about whether weekend work fits your life before you apply.

Skills That Travel

This kind of leasing role builds a foundation that transfers broadly. Strong performers here tend to move into leasing manager or property manager roles, where the same conversion skills apply at a higher level of complexity. The social media and content creation experience is increasingly valuable as properties compete harder for online attention. And the customer service orientation, specifically the ability to handle objections without getting defensive, is something every senior ops role in this industry needs.

What Bozzuto Brings to the Table

Bozzuto manages a portfolio that skews toward well-located, amenity-rich communities. The Columbia, MD location puts you in a market with a mix of established residents and consistent apartment demand, given its proximity to both Baltimore and the DC corridor. That means traffic tends to be real, not manufactured, which makes the leasing work more satisfying and the performance metrics more honest.

The position carries a salary range of $48,000 to $52,000, with bonus opportunities on top of that.   • Strong written and verbal communication skills

  • Sales aptitude and a customer-first approach
  • Proficiency with standard software and property management apps
  • Professional presentation and follow-through habits
  • Real estate license, or willingness to obtain within 90 days

This role starts in summer 2026, which gives candidates with current commitments time to plan accordingly. The strongest applicants won't just have friendly personalities. They'll have a track record of turning conversations into outcomes, whether that's in hospitality, retail, or a previous leasing role.

job.title: Sales & Marketing Associate - Summer 2026 Start
job.description:
Leasing is a skill-heavy job that most postings undersell. At its core, it asks you to read people quickly, communicate clearly under pressure, and close conversations without being pushy. Bozzuto's Sales and Marketing Associate role at their Nashville community leans into all three of those skills from day one.

What This Role Actually Uses

Your strongest asset in this position will be your ability to hold a real conversation. Not a scripted pitch. Prospects walk in with different priorities, different timelines, and different levels of skepticism. The ones who sign a lease are usually the ones who felt genuinely heard during the tour, not just walked through a floorplan. If you're good at reading a room and adjusting your approach on the fly, that's the foundation this role is built on.

Social media is the other major skill set in play here. Bozzuto expects you to generate awareness and excitement for the community through platforms like Instagram and Facebook. That means creating content that actually performs, not just hitting post on a stock photo. If you've built an audience before, managed a brand account, or have a genuine eye for what makes content worth stopping for, that transfers directly.

A real estate license is required (or you'll need to obtain one within 90 days of hire, where applicable). Worth knowing before you apply.

The Honest Day-to-Day

Weekends are part of the job. Three of them per month, with two weekday days off in exchange. That's not buried fine print; it's just how leasing works. Prospects look for apartments when they're not at work, which means you're working when they are. If that schedule genuinely doesn't fit your life, this role won't be a good fit either.

The day-to-day rhythm involves following up with leads, converting that interest into tours, conducting those tours in a way that feels personal rather than transactional, and then continuing to build relationships with current residents so the experience holds up after move-in. The follow-up piece matters more than people expect. A lot of leases are won or lost in the days between the first inquiry and the tour confirmation.

What Sets Strong Candidates Apart

The range of people who apply for leasing roles is wide. What separates the ones who thrive at a company like Bozzuto is consistency. It's easy to be engaging on a good day with a motivated prospect. It's harder to bring that same energy to the fifth follow-up call of the morning, or to a tour with someone who's already half-committed to a competitor property. The candidates who do well here are the ones who treat every interaction like it counts, because in leasing, it usually does.

  • Customer-first mentality, not just customer-friendly phrasing
  • Strong written and verbal communication
  • Comfort with CRM tools, apps, and leasing software
  • Social media fluency with an instinct for engaging content
  • Professional presentation and sales instincts

The salary range sits between $45,760 and $49,920, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. The Summer 2026 start date gives you time to plan accordingly.

Bozzuto operates at a high standard across their portfolio. Working here builds habits that carry into any leasing or property management role you take on afterward, including the discipline of follow-through and the ability to represent a brand without losing your own voice in the process.

job.title: Sales & Marketing Associate - Summer 2026 Start
job.description:
Bozzuto operates across the full property management spectrum, from design and construction through leasing and long-term management. At the community level, the leasing team is where occupancy gets made or lost, and this Sales and Marketing Associate role sits right at that front line.

What the Role Actually Involves

Your core job is converting traffic into leases. That starts with lead follow-up, turning inquiries into scheduled tours, and then conducting those tours in a way that feels personal rather than scripted. Every prospect who walks through the door is a real conversion opportunity, and your ability to read what matters to them determines whether they sign or keep shopping.

Beyond the tour itself, you're also responsible for keeping the community visible online. Social media content creation is part of the job, specifically building awareness and excitement on platforms like Instagram and Facebook. Think of it as lead generation you control directly.

That's standard for leasing roles because that's when prospective residents actually come out to look. If you need a strict Monday through Friday schedule, this isn't the right fit.

  • Strong written and verbal communication, not just friendly but actually persuasive

  • Sales instincts and a professional presentation

Where This Role Can Take You

Strong leasing consultants at well-run companies don't stay in leasing forever unless they want to. The traffic-to-lease data you generate, the resident experience you own, and the marketing skills you build here translate directly into Assistant Manager and then Property Manager roles. At a company like Bozzuto that operates across multiple asset classes and markets, internal mobility is a real path. The people who move up fastest are the ones who treat occupancy like a metric to be managed, not just a number that happens.

job.title: Senior Assistant Property Manager
job.description:
Bozzuto is hiring a Senior Assistant Property Manager (the title they use internally is Assistant General Manager) for a community in Fairfax, VA. The salary range is $65,000 to $70,000, plus bonus eligibility.

This is a genuine second-in-command role. When the General Manager is out, you're running the property. That means handling delinquency, coaching leasing staff, keeping the community presentation sharp, and making sure resident issues get resolved without waiting for someone above you to step in. You're not just supporting the GM. You're backing up the entire operation.

Day to day, you're splitting time between the leasing office and the floor. One hour you're conducting a personalized community tour for a prospect. The next you're reviewing outstanding balances, posting payments in Yardi, and following up on past-due accounts. You're also the person who notices when something looks off on the exterior walk and gets it addressed before a resident does. The role rewards people who are genuinely comfortable doing both the relational work and the administrative work without treating one as secondary to the other.

Weekend availability is part of the job. Expect one to two weekends per month, with weekday time off in exchange. That's standard for leasing-focused roles at this level, and Bozzuto is upfront about it.

Where this role tends to lead: Senior APMs at well-run companies like Bozzuto often move into General Manager or Property Manager roles within two to three years. If you're building toward running your own asset, this is the right step. You'll get real exposure to financial reporting, team leadership, and occupancy management, which are exactly the skills that translate directly into a GM seat.

What They're Looking For

  • Four-year degree or equivalent hands-on experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least two years working in one of those industries with real exposure to rent collection, budgets, and accounts receivable
  • Proficiency with Yardi or similar property management software
  • Comfort using social media (Facebook, Instagram) for community engagement and content
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • A sales mindset. You need to close leases, not just give tours

The candidates who stand out here aren't just organized. They read people well. A prospect who walks in skeptical needs to leave excited. A resident who's frustrated needs to leave heard. That interpersonal range, combined with operational discipline, is what separates a strong candidate from someone who can do the job but won't grow in it.

Bozzuto offers medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of PTO plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Senior Leasing Consultant
job.description:

What You'll Actually Be Doing

Bozzuto is looking for a Senior Leasing Consultant to run point on the full leasing cycle at one of their communities. The core of the job is converting leads into tours and tours into signed leases. That means timely follow-up, knowing the community and the surrounding neighborhood well enough to answer real questions, and running tours that feel personalized rather than scripted. Beyond the floor work, you'll have a hand in social media, specifically creating content on platforms like Instagram and Facebook to drive awareness and keep the community visible to people who haven't toured yet.

Resident experience is part of the job too. Bozzuto puts real weight on the quality of interactions at every touchpoint, so "stellar" isn't just a word they throw in the posting. What Makes Someone Good at This

  • A customer-focused mindset that comes across as genuine, not transactional

  • Sales instincts and professional presentation
  • Fluency with software, apps, and leasing platforms

The "Senior" designation matters here. Bozzuto isn't looking for someone still figuring out how to handle an objection or close a tour. The candidates who do well in this kind of role have enough reps behind them that they can read a prospect quickly, adjust their pitch, and know when to push and when to back off. Social media content creation is a real differentiator in this market. A lot of leasing consultants can post. Fewer can actually build a presence that generates leads.

Compensation and Growth

The salary range is $54,000 to $59,000, with bonus opportunities on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of PTO plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

On the career side, a Senior Leasing Consultant role at a company like Bozzuto is a reasonable path toward an Assistant Manager or Leasing Manager position. Sunnyvale is a competitive submarket within the Bay Area, and time spent leasing here gives you genuine experience handling a sophisticated renter pool.

If you want a role where the work is visible, the feedback is immediate, and the company has real infrastructure behind it, this one's worth a closer look.
