job.title: Senior Leasing Consultant ($1,500 Sign on Bonus)
job.description:
Leasing is the revenue engine of any apartment community. Every tour that doesn't convert, every lead that goes cold, every prospect who picks a competitor down the street represents real impact on occupancy and NOI. Senior Leasing Consultants exist because communities need someone who doesn't just show apartments but actively moves people from interest to signed lease. Bozzuto is hiring for that role at one of their Nashville properties.

What This Role Actually Requires

You're going to own the front end of the leasing process. That means following up with every prospect who has expressed interest, turning digital inquiries and phone calls into scheduled tours, and then conducting those tours in a way that feels personal rather than scripted. The goal isn't to show a floor plan. It's to help someone picture their life in that apartment.

Social media is a real part of the job here, not an afterthought. Bozzuto expects you to create and post content on platforms like Instagram and Facebook that generates genuine interest in the community. If you've only ever scrolled, this role will push you. If you've built an audience or run content for a property before, you'll hit the ground running.

Weekend availability is required. Three weekends per month, with compensating days off during the week. That schedule reflects reality: most prospects tour on Saturdays and Sundays, and the communities that staff accordingly win the traffic battle.

The salary range sits between $53,560 and $55,000, and there's a $1,500 sign-on bonus plus additional bonus opportunities. Bozzuto also offers medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of PTO plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What This Role Builds

Strong candidates for this role already have at least one year of leasing or sales experience and know how to handle objections without losing warmth. They understand that a prospect who says "I need to think about it" is often a prospect who didn't feel a genuine connection during the tour. That's a skill you refine here.

Beyond conversion technique, this role develops something that transfers everywhere in property management: the ability to read what a resident actually needs versus what they say they need. That skill carries directly into assistant manager and property manager roles, where resident retention and renewal strategy depend on the same emotional intelligence you use on a leasing tour.

  • At least one year of leasing or sales experience (strongly preferred)
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort creating content for Instagram and Facebook
  • Proficiency with software, apps, and property management platforms
  • Professional presentation and natural sales instincts
  • Availability to work three weekends per month

Nashville's rental market has remained competitive, with strong in-migration continuing to drive demand across several submarkets. A senior leasing role at a Bozzuto community here means you're working with real traffic, which means real opportunities to sharpen your craft quickly.

job.title: Senior Maintenance Technician
job.description:
Bozzuto operates some of the more well-regarded residential communities in the country, and their Seattle property reflects that standard. This Senior Maintenance Technician role sits one level above a standard tech position, meaning you're not just turning wrenches. You're the person on-site who others look to when a problem gets complicated.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your day centers on mechanical, electrical, plumbing, irrigation, and security systems. You're troubleshooting control systems, adjusting settings, and making sure the physical asset stays in the condition residents expect from a Bozzuto community. When preventative maintenance inspections flag deficiencies, you're the one who responds fast and resolves them before they become bigger cost items.

You'll also help lead the community's Preventative Maintenance Program alongside other techs. That means coordinating, coaching, and setting the pace. Not managing in a formal sense, but setting the standard others follow. Resident-facing work is part of the job too. Every maintenance request gets a response within 24 hours, and follow-through with residents, vendors, and the property management team has to be consistent and professional.

On-call rotation is real here. Evenings and weekends are part of the deal, and you'll need your own reliable transportation to respond to after-hours emergencies. That's a genuine expectation, not fine print.

What You Need to Bring

  • 2 to 5 years of maintenance technician experience, preferably in residential property management
  • HVAC and EPA certification strongly preferred
  • Hands-on expertise in HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and appliance repair
  • Ability to physically exert 50 to 100 pounds of force occasionally through pushing, pulling, and climbing
  • Solid written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with maintenance software, apps, and digital work order systems
  • Reliable personal transportation for on-call coverage

Where This Role Can Take You

Senior tech roles at companies like Bozzuto are a natural stepping stone toward Maintenance Supervisor or Chief Engineer positions. The skills you build here, specifically systems-level troubleshooting and preventative maintenance program leadership, translate directly to overseeing a full maintenance team at a larger asset or a portfolio of properties. The candidates who move up fastest aren't necessarily the most technically gifted. They're the ones who communicate clearly, document their work, and treat residents the way they'd want to be treated themselves.

The pay range for this position is $28 to $31 per hour, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Senior Maintenance Technician
job.description:
Bozzuto manages some of the most carefully maintained residential communities in the country, and this Senior Maintenance Technician role at their Redmond, WA property reflects that standard. This isn't an entry-level ticket-clearing position. You're the person the rest of the maintenance team looks to when a control system behaves strangely at 7am or a plumbing issue threatens to escalate into a unit turn nobody planned for.

The day-to-day centers on mechanical, electrical, plumbing, irrigation, and security systems. You're diagnosing problems, adjusting control systems, and staying ahead of deficiencies before residents notice them. Preventative maintenance isn't just a checklist here. It's a program, and you're helping lead it alongside other technicians who need your experience to sharpen their own. When a PM inspection flags something, you're not waiting on it.

Response time matters. Every maintenance request gets addressed within 24 hours, and follow-through with residents, vendors, and the property management team is expected to be professional and consistent. Residents at a Bozzuto community have high expectations, and the maintenance team is a direct part of whether those expectations get met or missed.

The on-call component is real. Rotational after-hours and weekend coverage is part of this role, and you'll need reliable personal transportation to get to the property when something goes sideways overnight. If that kind of unpredictability doesn't fit your life right now, this one probably isn't the right fit.

What the role requires

  • 2 to 5 years of maintenance technician experience, ideally in residential property management
  • Hands-on expertise in HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and/or appliance repair
  • EPA certification strongly preferred; HVAC certification a real plus
  • Ability to exert significant physical force regularly, including pushing, pulling, and climbing
  • Comfort with maintenance software, apps, and digital work order systems
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills

The salary range sits between $56,000 and $60,000, with bonus opportunities on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

One thing worth knowing if you're considering the move: Senior Maintenance Technicians at well-run companies like Bozzuto frequently progress into Maintenance Supervisor or Chief Engineer roles. The exposure you get managing preventative maintenance programs and mentoring junior techs builds exactly the operational credibility those next-level positions require. Redmond's competitive rental market means the property stays occupied and active, so you won't be short on experience.

job.title: Senior Maintenance Technician
job.description:
What This Role Actually Is

Senior Maintenance Technician at a Bozzuto property in Oakland means you're functioning as the technical backbone of the community. You're not just completing work orders. You're the person other techs come to when the HVAC behaves strangely, when a plumbing issue escalates, or when a control system needs someone who actually understands it. Bozzuto operates across the mid-rise and high-rise tier, so the mechanical systems you'll work on tend to be more complex than a typical garden-style community.

The Day-to-Day

Your scope covers the full range: mechanical, electrical, plumbing, irrigation, and security systems. You'll troubleshoot and adjust control systems, not just swap out parts. Preventative maintenance inspections are part of the rhythm here, and when deficiencies turn up, you're expected to move quickly. Resident service requests get a response within 24 hours. That's a real standard, not a suggestion.

You'll work alongside other maintenance technicians and serve as the senior resource on the team. That means some informal mentoring comes with the territory. On-call rotation is real, including nights and weekends. Oakland properties don't stop needing attention after business hours.

  • 2 to 5 years of residential maintenance experience, ideally in multifamily property management
  • Solid working knowledge of HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and appliance repair
  • EPA certification strongly preferred; HVAC certification a plus
  • Comfortable with property management software and maintenance apps
  • Reliable personal transportation for on-call response
  • Ability to handle physical demands including lifting and carrying up to 100 pounds occasionally

Building code compliance and fair housing awareness matter here. Bozzuto takes both seriously at the company level, and this role is expected to stay current on local, state, and federal standards.

Where This Role Can Take You

Senior tech is one of the clearest stepping stones in multifamily maintenance. The skills you sharpen here, particularly systems-level troubleshooting and the ability to lead a make-ready process or manage a punch list under pressure, translate directly into a Maintenance Supervisor or Lead Tech role. Bozzuto promotes from within with some consistency, and the company's investment in tuition reimbursement and career development programs is genuine infrastructure for that growth, not just a talking point.

What separates strong candidates from average ones at this level isn't just technical range. It's the combination of technical depth and communication. Residents notice when someone explains what was wrong and what was fixed. Property managers notice when a senior tech closes the loop without being asked. That combination is what gets you considered for the next level.

Compensation for this role runs between $56,000 and $65,000 annually, with bonus eligibility, medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Senior Property Manager
job.description:
Bozzuto has built a reputation in multifamily that carries real weight. Their communities tend to skew toward Class A and lease-up situations where resident experience isn't a talking point, it's the actual operating model. This Senior Property Manager role in Miami sits inside that framework, and it's worth understanding what that means before you apply.

The position is a General Manager role in practice. You're responsible for the full picture: occupancy, NOI, team development, client reporting, and the kind of resident satisfaction scores that ownership groups actually read. You'll be coaching leasing and maintenance staff, running performance reviews, and delivering financial and operational reports that clearly explain variances and trends. Bozzuto works with owners and investors, so your communication has to hold up in those conversations, not just internally.

One thing worth naming: weekend availability is part of this role. One weekend per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. That's a real trade-off, and the best candidates for this kind of GM seat have already made peace with the fact that multifamily doesn't pause on Saturdays when traffic picks up.

What separates strong candidates from average ones here isn't budget experience alone. It's the combination of financial fluency and genuine people instinct. Bozzuto's operating model puts a lot of emphasis on how residents feel in the community, which means a GM who can read a T-12 but can't build trust with a team or a difficult resident is going to struggle. The role asks you to coach in the moment, stay hands-on, and treat no task as beneath you. That's a culture signal worth taking seriously.

What You'll Bring

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience, with 5 or more years in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • Demonstrated history of building and retaining high-performing teams
  • Experience managing a budget and P&L, with working knowledge of GAAP
  • Proficiency in Yardi
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills suitable for client and ownership-level audiences
  • Active real estate license, or willingness to obtain one within 90 days of hire

From a career trajectory standpoint, this role is a legitimate stepping stone toward a Regional Manager or Director of Operations seat. GMs who perform well at companies like Bozzuto build a track record with institutional ownership groups, which opens doors that fee management or smaller operators simply can't. The Miami market adds another layer: it's competitive, heavily renter-driven, and gives you exposure to a resident demographic that sharpens your sales and retention instincts fast. The compensation range is $90,000 to $103,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that, plus benefits that include medical, dental, vision, 20 days of PTO, 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Senior Property Manager
job.description:
It's 9 a.m. on a Tuesday and you're already three things at once: reviewing last month's variance report before a client call, coaching a leasing consultant through a tough objection on the floor, and mentally flagging a maintenance trend that's starting to show up in resident reviews. That's the job.

What This Role Actually Covers

Bozzuto is hiring a Senior Property Manager (titled General Manager internally) for a residential community in Washington, DC. This is a full ownership role. You're responsible for the property's financial performance, team development, client relationships, and resident experience.

On the financial side, you'll manage to budget, explain variances clearly in ownership reports, and keep NOI on track. You're expected to know your T-12, understand where the numbers are trending, and communicate that story to clients and investors without hand-holding.

On the people side, you're building and coaching a team. That means real-time feedback, structured performance reviews, and development plans that actually move people forward. Bozzuto expects its GMs to be in the work alongside their teams. No job is beneath you here.

You'll also own the competitive positioning of the community. That includes staying sharp on what comparable properties are doing, working with marketing to drive traffic, and making sure your team can articulate why a prospective resident should choose this community over the one down the street.

One weekend per month is required, with two weekdays off in exchange. Prospective residents shop on weekends, and Bozzuto expects leadership present when it counts.

What You Need to Bring

  • At least 5 years in property management, hospitality, or retail, with demonstrated team leadership experience
  • A four-year degree or equivalent hands-on experience
  • Proven ability to manage a P&L and explain financial performance to ownership groups
  • Proficiency in Yardi and standard property management software
  • Working knowledge of GAAP basics
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills

The salary range for this position is $130,000 to $147,500, plus bonus eligibility. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What separates strong candidates from average ones here isn't just the financial acumen. It's the ability to hold a team accountable while keeping morale intact. DC is a competitive, high-expectation rental market with residents who have options and know it. The GMs who do well at Bozzuto tend to be the ones who treat resident retention as seriously as lease-up, and who build teams that don't fall apart when they're not in the room.

job.title: Senior Property Manager
job.description:
It's a Saturday morning. Foot traffic is picking up, your leasing team is running tours, and you're pulling weekend duty, reviewing last night's review responses before jumping in to help close a prospect who's been on the fence for two weeks. That's the rhythm of this role. Not every day looks like that, but many do.

Bozzuto is hiring a Senior Property Manager (General Manager) for a community in Atlanta's 30309 zip code, which puts you squarely in Midtown. That's a competitive submarket with strong Class A demand, high renter expectations, and a steady pipeline of new supply that keeps occupancy and retention conversations real. Residents here have options, and they know it. Your job is to make sure they keep choosing yours.

What This Role Actually Involves

You'll own the full operational picture: financial performance against an established budget, team coaching and development, client reporting, and the day-to-day culture of the property. Bozzuto's model is hospitality-forward, so resident satisfaction scores and community reviews carry real weight alongside your NOI. You'll work directly with ownership groups and investors, delivering accurate financial and operational reports that explain variances clearly, not just present numbers.

Marketing is part of the job too. You'll partner with Bozzuto's consumer marketing team to position the community effectively, and you'll be expected to know the competitive set well enough to coach your team on how to differentiate during every tour and follow-up call.

One weekend shift per month is required, with two weekdays off in exchange. It's worth knowing that upfront.

What You Need to Bring

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least five years of relevant industry experience, with a real track record of building and retaining strong teams
  • Experience managing a budget or P&L, with solid working knowledge of GAAP basics
  • Proficiency in Yardi and standard industry tools
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, especially when presenting to clients or ownership
  • A Georgia real estate license, or the ability to obtain one within 90 days of hire
  • Experience managing relationships with owners, investors, and vendors

What separates strong candidates here isn't just operational competence. It's the combination of financial fluency and genuine people instinct. Bozzuto's culture puts a premium on how you show up for your team and residents, not just what the numbers say at month-end. If you're the type who sees coaching a leasing consultant through a tough objection as part of your job, not a distraction from it, that mindset fits here.

The salary range for this position is $101,908 to $105,000, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401(k) with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Sr. Maintenance Technician - $2,500 Sign-On Bonus
job.description:
Bozzuto is offering a $2,500 sign-on bonus for a Senior Maintenance Technician at their Harrison, NJ community. If you've put in the hours diagnosing HVAC systems, tracing electrical faults, and closing out work orders before the next shift starts, this role is built around that skill set.

The day-to-day here isn't just reactive work orders. You're taking point on mechanical, electrical, plumbing, irrigation, and security systems, making control adjustments, and catching deficiencies before they become resident complaints or costly repairs. That distinction matters. A strong senior tech thinks in terms of the property's physical condition over time, not just today's punch list. You'll work alongside other technicians to keep the preventative maintenance program on track, which means your organizational habits directly affect how the asset holds up month to month.

Response time is a real KPI in this role. Maintenance requests need a response within 24 hours, and resident follow-up should be professional and timely every time. At a Bozzuto community, the standard of service is high because resident experience ties directly to renewals and occupancy. A tech who communicates clearly with residents and the property management team closes the loop faster and keeps satisfaction scores where they need to be.

One thing worth acknowledging: this role includes on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies, nights, and weekends. That's the reality of residential maintenance at this level. You'll need reliable personal transportation to respond when the call comes in. If that's already part of how you operate, it won't be a surprise.

What Bozzuto is Looking For

  • 1 to 3 years of experience as a maintenance technician, preferably in residential property management
  • HVAC and EPA certification strongly preferred
  • Working knowledge across HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and appliance repair
  • Ability to handle physical demands: occasional exertion up to 100 pounds, frequent lifting and climbing
  • Comfortable with maintenance software and digital work order systems
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Reliable personal transportation for on-call response

The salary range for this position is $55,000 to $57,000, plus eligibility for additional bonus opportunities on top of the sign-on. Bozzuto's benefits package includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

For technicians thinking about where this role leads: senior tech experience at a well-run operator like Bozzuto is a direct path toward a Maintenance Supervisor or Service Manager position. You build the diagnostic credibility, the vendor relationships, and the systems knowledge that property managers rely on when they're building a maintenance team from scratch or turning around a struggling asset. That combination of hard skills and operational awareness is what separates technicians who stay senior techs from those who move up.

job.title: Sr. Maintenance Technician - Seaport
job.description:
What This Role Actually Involves

Senior Maintenance Technician positions at mid-to-large residential communities aren't for people who want a predictable 9-to-5. This one at Bozzuto's Seaport property in Boston puts you at the front of the technical work: mechanical systems, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, irrigation, and security. You're not just completing tickets. You're the person other technicians look to when a problem gets complicated.

Bozzuto manages and maintains residential communities across multiple markets, and their Seaport property sits in one of Boston's most developed and competitive submarkets. Residents here expect quick, professional service. That expectation lands on you.

The Day-to-Day

A typical week mixes scheduled preventative maintenance work with the unpredictable. You might start Tuesday running through PM inspections on HVAC units, find a control system that needs adjustment, and spend the afternoon troubleshooting before a resident move-in. Thursday could look completely different. That variability is real, and it's worth knowing before you apply.

  • Lead troubleshooting on mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and building control systems
  • Maintain the physical asset with a focus on tasks requiring advanced technical skill
  • Drive the community's Preventative Maintenance Program alongside other technicians
  • Respond to all maintenance requests within 24 hours and follow up with residents and vendors professionally
  • Stay current on applicable building codes, safety standards, and fair housing requirements
  • Participate in on-call rotation, including evenings and weekends for after-hours emergencies

The on-call component is real. Rotational after-hours coverage means some weekends and nights belong to this job. If that's a dealbreaker, it's better to know now.

What Bozzuto Is Looking For

Two to five years of residential maintenance experience is the baseline. HVAC certification (EPA 608) is strongly preferred, and expertise in at least a few of the core trades (HVAC, electrical, plumbing, appliance repair) is expected. You'll need reliable personal transportation for emergency response.

The candidates who do well in senior tech roles tend to combine solid trade skills with genuine communication ability. Residents don't just want a fix. They want to know what happened, what you did, and whether it'll happen again. Written and verbal communication matters here, as does comfort with software and maintenance management apps.

Physical demands are significant. The role requires occasionally moving 50 to 100 pounds, frequently handling 25 to 50 pounds, and constant physical activity through climbing, pushing, and pulling. This is hands-on work throughout the shift.

Compensation and Benefits

The posted salary for this role is $60,000, with eligibility for additional bonus opportunities. Bozzuto's benefits package includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For technicians who want to grow toward a lead or maintenance supervisor role, Bozzuto's size and structure create a real path to get there.

job.title: Weekend Evening Concierge
job.description:
It's Saturday at 12:00 PM. You walk in, take over the front desk, and within the first hour you've signed for six packages, helped a prospect find parking, and remembered to ask a longtime resident how her dog's vet visit went. That's the rhythm of this role.

What You'll Be Doing

This is a weekend-only position: Saturday and Sunday, noon to 9 PM. You're the face of the community during those hours. Prospects come in off the street. Residents come home from their day. Everyone gets the same attentive, personalized experience.

The work splits across a few areas. You handle front desk operations: package management, freight elevator scheduling, club room reservations, and keeping logs current and organized. You solve problems as they come up, which means staying calm when something goes sideways and knowing when to escalate. You also plan and execute resident events that actually bring people together, not just put food on a table.

On the social side, you generate content and engagement to keep the community visible online. You also encourage satisfied residents to share their experience through reviews. Both matter for the property's reputation.

What This Role Requires

  • At least 2 years in hospitality, retail, hotel, or a similar customer-facing environment
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with software, apps, and standard computer programs
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds (package volume gets real around the holidays)
  • A professional appearance and genuine attention to detail
  • A customer-first mindset that doesn't require reminding

The pay range is $22 to $25 per hour, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What separates strong candidates here is memory and warmth. Anyone can manage a package log. The people who thrive in concierge roles at luxury residential properties are the ones who remember names, pick up on preferences, and make residents feel genuinely recognized every time they walk through the door. San Francisco residents at this price point have high expectations and plenty of alternatives. Consistent, personalized service is what builds the kind of retention that shows up in renewal numbers.

This position is with Bozzuto, a well-regarded residential operator with properties across major U.S. markets. The role is based in San Francisco at the 94103 zip code.

job.title: Weekend Evening Concierge (4pm - 12am)
job.description:
Bozzuto builds and manages residential communities with a reputation for high-touch service, and their concierge roles sit right at the center of that identity. This isn't a gatekeeping position. It's a relationship role, and the person who fills it sets the tone for how residents experience coming home every single day.

What You're Actually Doing on Shift

The shift runs 4pm to midnight on weekends, which means you're covering the hours when residents are most present and most likely to need something. You'll greet people by name, manage package intake and retrieval, handle front desk logistics like club room reservations and freight elevator scheduling, and field whatever comes through the door with composure. Some nights that's a smooth, social few hours. Others involve a frustrated resident, a delayed vendor, and a missing package all at once. You handle it without losing your warmth.

Beyond the desk, you'll help plan and execute resident events that actually bring people together, not just obligatory gatherings. You'll also contribute to the community's social media presence and encourage satisfied residents to leave reviews online. That last piece matters more than it sounds: at a Bozzuto property, online reputation directly reflects the quality of the on-site team.

What You Bring

  • At least two years in hospitality, retail, hotel, or a similar customer-facing environment
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with software, apps, and property management or front desk platforms
  • A professional appearance and genuine attention to detail
  • Physical ability to lift up to 25 pounds (package volume gets real around the holidays)
  • A customer-first instinct that doesn't require reminding

The hourly range for this role sits between $21.21 and $23.00, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

Who Thrives Here

The candidates who stand out in concierge roles at high-quality multifamily properties are the ones who remember details without being prompted. You know which resident has a dog named Biscuit. You notice when someone looks like they've had a rough day. You don't wait to be asked before solving a problem. That kind of attentiveness is harder to train than most skills on a resume, and it's exactly what a Bozzuto community depends on during evening and weekend hours when the broader management team isn't on site.

This role also builds a practical foundation for growth. Concierge experience at a well-run property gives you direct exposure to resident relations, community programming, and front-of-house operations. Those are transferable skills if you eventually move toward leasing, assistant management, or hospitality management roles. Bozzuto's size and internal development programs make that path realistic, not theoretical.

job.title: Bilingual Resident Services Specialist - Sandy Springs, GA
job.description:
Cortland runs one of the larger vertically integrated multifamily platforms in the country, and their centralized contact center model means this role looks a little different from a typical on-site leasing consultant position. You're not walking apartments or collecting keys. You're the voice residents hear when something needs to get resolved, and you're handling that across multiple properties at once.

What This Role Actually Involves

As a Bilingual Resident Services Specialist based in Sandy Springs, you'll work inside Cortland's contact center, fielding inbound calls, emails, and chat messages from residents across the portfolio. The bilingual piece matters here. You'll be communicating in both English and Spanish, which means your ability to de-escalate a tense situation or explain a lease detail clearly doesn't get to stop at one language.

The volume is real. You'll manage multiple conversations simultaneously, toggle between platforms like RealPage, OneSite, and Funnel, and stay organized while priorities shift. Some of what lands in your queue will be simple. Some of it won't. Difficult residents, time-sensitive maintenance concerns, and sensitive account questions are part of the daily mix, not the exception.

  • Handle resident inquiries across phone, email, and chat with accuracy and professionalism
  • De-escalate complaints and document interactions within CRM tools
  • Navigate multiple property management platforms and adapt as systems change
  • Maintain Fair Housing compliance on every interaction, without exception
  • Work collaboratively within the contact center team toward shared service goals
  • Maintain availability for weekend and extended hours as business needs require

What Cortland Is Looking For

Two or more years of customer service experience is required, preferably in a high-volume contact center or multifamily setting. A high school diploma or equivalent is the minimum education requirement. You'll need solid proficiency in Microsoft Office and comfort with property management software platforms. Strong time management isn't a soft skill here. It's a daily operational requirement.

What separates strong candidates from average ones in this kind of role is emotional steadiness. It's easy to be pleasant when the call is easy. The candidates who do well here stay calm and solution-oriented when a resident is frustrated, when systems are slow, and when the queue isn't shrinking. Bilingual fluency in Spanish is a core requirement, not a bonus.

The Bigger Picture

Contact center roles at large operators like Cortland tend to build a skill set that translates well across the industry. You'll develop fluency with enterprise-level property management platforms, a strong understanding of resident retention, and experience handling compliance-sensitive conversations at scale. Those skills carry real weight if you later move toward an on-site community manager role or a regional support function. This isn't a dead-end position. It's a clear entry point into a company that promotes from within and has the portfolio size to back that up.

job.title: Bilingual Resident Services Specialist - Sandy Springs, GA
job.description:
It's 10:30 on a Tuesday morning. You have a resident on hold who's frustrated about a maintenance delay, two chat windows open with prospective residents asking about availability, and an email queue that grew by six messages while you were handling the last call. That's a normal Tuesday in this role.

What This Job Actually Is

Cortland, one of the largest multifamily owners and operators in the country, runs a centralized contact center model that serves residents across its portfolio from a single hub. This bilingual Resident Services Specialist position, based in Sandy Springs, GA, sits at the center of that model. You're the voice residents hear when something goes wrong, when they have a question about their lease, or when they just need someone to follow through on a promise a previous rep made.

The bilingual piece matters here. You'll serve Spanish-speaking residents who deserve the same quality of communication and resolution that every other resident gets, and your fluency is what makes that possible. Not a translation service. An actual conversation.

Day to day, you're handling inbound calls, emails, and chat inquiries across multiple properties simultaneously. You'll work inside platforms like RealPage, OneSite, and Funnel, so comfort with property management software matters. De-escalation is a real part of this job, not an occasional exception. Some residents are patient. Some aren't. You'll need to hold your professionalism either way, while keeping Fair Housing compliance intact in every interaction.

What Cortland Is Looking For

  • Bilingual fluency in English and Spanish, spoken and written
  • At least two years of customer service experience, preferably in a high-volume contact center or multifamily housing setting
  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Demonstrated ability to manage confidential information with discretion
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office and familiarity with CRM and property management platforms
  • Strong organizational skills and the ability to prioritize accurately when everything feels urgent
  • Availability to work weekends and extended hours when the business needs it

What separates the candidates who thrive here from those who burn out quickly is usually one thing: the ability to reset between calls. You'll take a hard conversation, close it, document it accurately, and pick up the next one without carrying the frustration forward. That emotional reset is a skill. If you have it, this role is manageable and genuinely rewarding. If you don't, the volume will wear you down fast.

Cortland's vertically integrated structure means this isn't a third-party management shop passing resident complaints up a chain of vendors. Decisions move faster, and your resolutions tend to carry more weight. That's a meaningful difference for someone who actually wants to close tickets rather than just log them.

job.title: Centralized Painter - Arlington, VA
job.description:
Multifamily operators with large portfolios don't leave painting to chance. When you're turning dozens of units a month, inconsistent paint quality slows make-readies, delays move-ins, and chips away at NOI. Cortland, one of the top 10 multifamily owners and operators in the country, runs a centralized paint operation in Arlington, VA to keep that from happening. This role sits inside that system.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your baseline is one full apartment paint or two touch-up units per day. That's the floor, not the ceiling. You'll handle spray equipment, roller and brush work, and the prep that makes all of it hold up. Small drywall repairs and texture matching are part of the job. You're not waiting for a separate crew to patch before you paint. You handle it.

The paint shop is your responsibility too. That means opening and closing procedures, equipment maintenance, and keeping supplies stocked. Spray equipment that's poorly maintained becomes a scheduling problem fast. You follow the maintenance protocols as written and flag anything that threatens the timeline.

You'll work closely with the Service Manager. When you see a process that could run tighter, you say so. That kind of input is expected here, not just tolerated.

What Cortland Needs From You

  • At least 2 years of interior residential painting experience. Custom home background is a plus.
  • Solid spray equipment skills, including prep and the ability to troubleshoot or repair equipment.
  • Drywall repair capability, including texture matching. Larger project experience is a bonus.
  • Comfort working on ladders and scaffolding.
  • Working knowledge of OSHA and MSDS regulations.
  • Valid driver's license with current insurance.
  • Ability to work independently with minimal oversight.
  • Flexibility for weekend shifts when needed.
  • Maintenance management software experience is helpful. Willingness to learn is required.
  • High school diploma or equivalent.

Strong candidates in this type of role tend to separate themselves through consistency, not speed. Turning a unit fast means nothing if the paint is pulling away from trim or the texture match is off. The painters who stand out here are the ones who bring the same level of attention to unit 14 on a Friday afternoon as they did to unit 1 on Monday morning.

This is a drug-free workplace. Cortland participates in E-Verify.

job.title: Centralized Painter - Boise, Idaho
job.description:
Paint is one of the most visible parts of a make-ready, and in a centralized model it's also one of the most measurable. When a company pulls painting out of individual properties and puts it in the hands of a dedicated team, the goal is consistency and throughput. Cortland operates that way in Boise, and they're adding a Centralized Painter to keep units moving.

The production expectation here is concrete: at least one full apartment paint per day, or two touch-up units. That's not unreasonable for someone with solid spray experience, but it does mean you need to be efficient from the moment you open the paint shop. Prep work, equipment maintenance, opening and closing tasks, keeping the shop stocked and clean. These aren't afterthoughts; they're part of the job description because they directly affect your ability to hit daily numbers.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Most days you're moving between units, running spray equipment, doing roller and brush work where spraying isn't practical, and handling small drywall repairs including texture matching. That last part matters more than people expect. A painter who can't match texture leaves units looking patched, which either delays approval or comes back as a punch list item. The ability to do small drywall work in-house keeps turns moving without waiting on a separate trade.

You'll coordinate closely with the Service Manager on scheduling and flag anything that threatens your timeline. In a centralized setup, one delayed unit can affect the whole day's plan, so communication isn't optional. Working knowledge of maintenance management software is a plus, and if you don't have it, you'll need to pick it up.

What Cortland Is Looking For

  • At least two years of interior residential painting experience; custom home background is a bonus
  • Proficiency with spray equipment, rollers, and brushes, along with proper spray prep
  • Working knowledge of OSHA and MSDS regulations and elevated surface safety
  • Ability to complete small drywall repairs and texture matching
  • Comfort working on ladders or scaffolding
  • Valid driver's license with current insurance
  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Availability including weekends as needed

The candidates who tend to stand out in centralized painting roles aren't just fast. They're consistent. Speed without quality creates rework, and rework destroys the efficiency that the centralized model is built around. If you take pride in a clean cut-in and a properly maintained sprayer, that matters here more than it would in a general maintenance tech role.

Cortland is a top-10 multifamily owner and operator nationally, which means there's infrastructure behind this position. Processes, standards, and resources that smaller operators don't have. For a painter who wants to do the work right without constantly improvising around missing supplies or broken equipment, that's genuinely worth something.

job.title: Centralized Painter - Boise, Idaho
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

This is a production-focused painting role supporting apartment turns across Cortland's Boise portfolio. The expectation is clear: complete at least one full unit paint per day, or two touch-up paints. That pace is the baseline, not the goal.

Beyond rolling and brushing, you'll run spray equipment regularly. That means proper setup, daily maintenance of the sprayer, and following equipment protocols to keep gear in working condition. Spray prep, masking, and cleanup are all part of the job. So is knowing when something is wrong and flagging it before it slows the team down.

You'll also handle small drywall repairs and texture matching before paint goes on. Not every turn is a clean canvas. Nail pops, scuffs, minor holes. You fix them and move on. Larger drywall work is a plus, but the core expectation is solid patch-and-paint execution at volume.

The paint shop is yours to maintain. That means stocked, clean, organized, and ready at open and close each day. You'll work directly with the Service Manager and flag anything that affects your schedule or the quality of completed units.

What You Bring

  • At least 2 years of interior residential painting experience. Custom home background is a plus.
  • Solid proficiency with rollers, brushes, and spray equipment. You're not learning the basics here.
  • Ability to complete small drywall repairs and match existing texture.
  • Working knowledge of OSHA requirements, MSDS regulations, and elevated work safety (ladders, scaffolding).
  • Valid driver's license with current vehicle insurance.
  • Comfortable working with minimal supervision and communicating issues clearly in writing or verbally.
  • Flexibility to work weekends as needed.
  • Familiarity with maintenance management software is a plus. Willingness to learn is required.

Why This Role Is Worth Understanding

Centralized painter roles in multifamily are different from general maintenance painter tasks. You're not floating between work orders. You own the turn pipeline from a paint standpoint, which means your throughput directly affects how fast units get back on the market. Slow paint equals delayed move-ins. Strong painters in this type of centralized model often become go-to resources for training newer team members or stepping into lead painter roles as portfolios grow.

Cortland operates as a vertically integrated company, meaning decisions about operations, standards, and processes tend to move faster than at third-party management firms. If you spot a better way to run the shop, there's a real channel to surface that feedback. The Service Manager relationship here is collaborative, not just supervisory.

Boise's multifamily market has seen consistent development activity, which means Cortland's local portfolio is likely active. Turns keep coming. The work is steady, the standards are high, and the expectation is that you show up and perform at pace every day.

job.title: Centralized Painter - Denver, CO
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

Cortland runs a centralized paint operation across its Denver portfolio, which means your work directly feeds unit turn speed. A slow paint shop is a delayed make-ready. A delayed make-ready is a vacant day. Vacant days kill NOI. That's the operational chain you're sitting inside.

Your baseline expectation is one full apartment home paint per day, or two touch-up paints. That's the minimum. You'll handle spray equipment, roller and brush work, and the prep that makes all of it possible. Small drywall repairs and texture matching are part of the job, not exceptions to it. You'll also own the paint shop itself: opening and closing tasks, equipment maintenance, inventory levels, and keeping the space clean and organized to Cortland's standards.

When something affects your schedule or threatens a completion date, you flag it. You don't wait for the Service Manager to notice. That communication habit is what separates a good painter from a reliable one on a centralized team where multiple units depend on a single shop's output.

What You Bring

  • At least 2 years of interior residential painting experience. Custom home work is a genuine plus because it sharpens attention to finish quality.
  • Real proficiency with spray equipment, including maintenance and troubleshooting. Knowing how to fix the sprayer matters as much as knowing how to run it.
  • Comfort with small drywall repairs and texture matching. Larger project capability is a bonus.
  • Working knowledge of OSHA standards, MSDS regulations, and elevated safety procedures (ladders, scaffolding).
  • A valid driver's license with current vehicle insurance.
  • Ability to work independently without needing daily direction, including weekend availability when needed.
  • Willingness to learn maintenance management software if you're not already familiar with it.

Pay for this role runs $19 to $21 per hour depending on experience.

The Operational Reality

Centralized painter roles are relatively uncommon in multifamily. Most operators assign painting to individual maintenance teams at each property, which means inconsistent quality and slower turns. Cortland's centralized model is designed to solve both problems. It also means your output is measurable in a way that individual property painting rarely is. You're not invisible here. Your daily unit count, your equipment uptime, and your shop organization are all visible to leadership.

Strong candidates come in knowing that pace and quality have to coexist. The painters who struggle in this kind of role are the ones who can do beautiful work slowly or fast work sloppily. This job requires both, consistently, across multiple units in a week. If you've got that combination and you want a role where your craft actually connects to something larger than a single building's occupancy, this is a good fit.

job.title: Centralized Painter - Greater Fort Worth, TX

Multifamily operators at scale run into a simple problem: turns pile up faster than a general maintenance team can paint them. Cortland's centralized painter model solves that by dedicating a specialist to painting output across properties in the Greater Fort Worth area, keeping make-ready timelines tight and quality consistent.

Your day is built around production. The baseline expectation is one full apartment paint per day or two touch-up units. That's a real number, not a soft goal. You'll work out of a dedicated paint shop, which you're responsible for keeping stocked, clean, and organized to Cortland's standards. Opening and closing that shop is part of the daily routine.

Beyond rolling and cutting, you'll handle small drywall repairs and texture matching before you ever pick up a brush. Spray equipment is a core part of this role. You need to know how to set it up, maintain it, and troubleshoot it. If something breaks or a schedule slips, you're expected to flag it to the Service Manager promptly, not quietly fall behind.

This isn't a solo-in-the-corner role. You'll collaborate with the service team on process improvements and speak up when you see a better way to run things.

What Cortland Needs From You

  • At least 2 years of interior residential painting experience. Custom home painting is a bonus.

Strong candidates in this type of centralized role separate themselves by consistency, not just craft. Cortland is running a portfolio operation, which means your output directly affects unit availability and occupancy across multiple properties. Painters who communicate proactively, maintain their equipment without being told, and take quality seriously on unit 8 the same way they did on unit 1 are the ones who thrive here. The ability to work with minimal supervision isn't a soft preference. It's essential.

Cortland is a top-10 multifamily owner and operator in the U.S., founded in 2005, with vertically integrated operations that include in-house design, construction, and property management. Fort Worth sits inside one of the most active multifamily markets in the country right now, which means this role isn't going to slow down anytime soon.

job.title: Centralized Painter - Greater North Dallas, TX
job.description:
Large multifamily operators with sizable portfolios don't rely solely on property-level maintenance teams to handle every turn. Centralized trade roles exist precisely to bring consistency and speed to one of the most repetitive, time-sensitive tasks in apartment operations: getting a unit paint-ready between residents. Cortland has built that model out across the Greater North Dallas market, and they're looking for a skilled painter to work within it.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

This is a production-oriented role, not a one-off custom job environment. You're expected to complete at least one full apartment paint per day, or two touch-ups, depending on what the schedule calls for. That pace requires someone who's efficient with spray equipment, knows how to prep correctly the first time, and doesn't need someone watching over their shoulder. You'll also handle small drywall repairs, including texture matching, which separates solid candidates from those who can only roll paint.

Maintaining the paint shop itself is part of the job. That means opening and closing tasks, keeping equipment in tight condition, and making sure supplies stay stocked. If you've ever worked somewhere that let sprayers go uncleaned or let inventory run dry mid-week, you know how fast a turn schedule falls apart. Cortland runs a structured shop, and they expect you to help keep it that way.

You'll report to a Service Manager and collaborate on process improvements when opportunities come up. This isn't a role where you clock in, paint, and clock out without thinking. They want someone who notices inefficiencies and speaks up about them.

What You Bring to This Role

  • At least two years of interior residential painting experience. Custom home background is a bonus.
  • Proficiency with spray equipment, rollers, and brushes, including proper spray prep and the ability to troubleshoot or repair trade equipment
  • Comfort working on ladders and scaffolding
  • Working knowledge of OSHA requirements and MSDS regulations related to paint and coatings
  • Ability to complete small drywall repairs with texture matching. Larger project experience is a plus.
  • Valid driver's license and current vehicle insurance
  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Familiarity with maintenance management software, or willingness to learn it
  • Availability for varied shifts, including weekends when needed

The North Dallas submarket has seen significant multifamily development over the past several years, with Cortland holding a notable presence across garden-style and mid-rise communities throughout the area. A centralized painter here isn't bouncing between one or two properties. You're likely covering multiple sites, which means your time management and ability to work independently directly affect how quickly units get turned and how fast they're back on the market generating revenue.

One honest note: production painting in a multifamily setting is physically demanding and schedule-driven. It suits people who find satisfaction in visible, measurable daily output and who take quality control personally. If you've spent time in custom residential work and want more structure and consistency in your day, this kind of centralized role tends to be a natural fit.

What You'll Actually Do

This is a production role. Cortland runs a centralized paint operation across its Greater Washington DC portfolio, which means you're moving between communities to keep make-ready timelines on track. Beyond rolling and spraying units, you'll handle small drywall repairs and texture matching, run paint prep, and maintain spray equipment to near-new condition. The paint shop itself is your responsibility to open, close, and keep stocked and clean. You're also expected to flag problems early. If something is slowing you down or affecting the schedule, the Service Manager needs to know before it becomes a missed turn date, not after.

Cortland has documented protocols for spray equipment maintenance. You'll follow them. If you see a better way to do something, you're expected to say so. They want input on process improvements, not just compliance.

What You Bring

  • At least two years of interior residential painting experience. Custom home work is a genuine plus because it sharpens your eye for detail and finish quality.
  • Proficiency with roller, brush, and spray application, including proper prep work before spraying

  • Working knowledge of spray equipment maintenance and the ability to troubleshoot or repair trade equipment if needed
  • Comfort working on ladders and scaffolding

  • A valid driver's license and current vehicle insurance
  • High school diploma or equivalent
