job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role lives and dies on follow-up. You're the first real conversation a prospective resident has with Cortland Cotswold, and your job is to turn that conversation into a signed lease. That means working a sales pipeline with real discipline: logging every interaction in the CRM, hitting conversion targets, and knowing when to push and when to listen. Consultative selling matters here. You're not reciting floor plans. You're uncovering what someone actually needs in a home and connecting that to what the community offers.

Beyond closing leases, you'll manage the community's marketing presence and social channels, plan resident events, and build the kind of relationships that generate referrals. Cortland operates at a premium brand standard, so the expectation is consistent: every touchpoint, from the first tour to a maintenance follow-up conversation, should feel intentional.

You'll track your own numbers. Lead activity, conversion rates, pipeline health. Good leasing consultants at companies like Cortland know their metrics the way a sales rep knows their quota. That self-awareness is what separates people who hit goals from people who wonder why they didn't.

What You Bring

  • At least six months of sales, leasing, or customer-facing experience where you were held to measurable results
  • Genuine comfort with goal-oriented work. You want to know the number, and you want to beat it
  • CRM fluency or a quick willingness to develop it. Sloppy records cost deals
  • Strong rapport-building instincts paired with the ability to handle objections without getting rattled
  • Coachability. Cortland invests in its leasing teams, and they expect you to use that investment
  • A polished, professional presence that fits a premium multifamily brand

Where This Role Can Take You

Leasing consultant roles at well-capitalized, vertically integrated operators like Cortland are genuinely good career entry points. The skills you build here compound. Pipeline management, objection handling, CRM discipline, and resident experience all transfer directly into assistant manager and then property manager roles. Cortland's in-house structure means there are paths into training, regional operations, and even development-adjacent work if you perform and stay curious.

Charlotte's multifamily market has remained active, with continued lease-up activity and strong renter demand in neighborhoods like Cotswold. That means you'll get real volume. Real traffic. The kind of repetition that actually builds a leasing skillset fast, rather than sitting in a slow-lease community waiting for the phone to ring.

If you're early in a property management career and want a company with enough structure to learn from but enough autonomy to grow, this is a reasonable place to start building that foundation.

job.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant - Cortland Legacy
job.description:
Leasing is a skill set that gets underestimated. The best consultants in this industry aren't order-takers waiting for walk-ins. They're running a sales pipeline, managing follow-up cadences, reading objections in real time, and closing with enough confidence that the prospect feels good about the decision. That's the kind of leasing this role at Cortland Legacy in Frisco, TX is built around.

The Skills This Role Puts to Work Every Day

Consultative selling is the core of it. You're not reciting floor plans. You're asking the right questions, figuring out what actually matters to a prospect, and building a case for why this community fits their life. That takes active listening, which sounds simple until you're on your fourth tour of the day and someone mentions in passing that they work nights. Good leasing consultants catch that. Average ones don't.

CRM discipline matters here too. Cortland expects pipeline hygiene: accurate records, timely follow-up, conversion metrics you can speak to. If you've been in a leasing role where leads fell into a black hole after the first call, this will feel like a different standard. It is.

Beyond the sales mechanics, you'll carry the community's social media presence and help plan resident events. Those aren't afterthoughts. In a Class A market like Frisco, where new product keeps coming online, retention is as important as traffic. The leasing consultant who builds genuine rapport with residents is the one who keeps renewals healthy.

What the Job Actually Looks Like

  • Driving occupancy and hitting leasing KPIs through proactive outreach and pipeline management, not just reactive walk-in traffic
  • Running tours with a consultative approach: uncovering needs, handling objections, closing with confidence
  • Keeping CRM records current and using lead activity data to prioritize follow-up
  • Delivering a concierge-level experience from first contact through move-in
  • Managing outreach marketing and maintaining the community's social media presence
  • Planning and executing resident engagement events that build loyalty and generate referrals
  • Taking coaching seriously and adjusting your approach based on market conditions and leadership feedback

What Makes This Role Worth Considering

Frisco is one of the more competitive submarkets in the DFW metro. Rent growth has moderated after a few aggressive years, which means leasing teams are working harder for every close. Concessions are back in parts of the market. That context matters because it means the skills you build here, negotiating value in a tighter environment, are genuinely transferable. A consultant who can perform when the market isn't doing all the work for them is a consultant who advances.

Cortland operates as a vertically integrated platform, which means decisions about the product, the renovations, the resident experience aren't outsourced to a third party. For a leasing consultant, that usually translates to cleaner communication and more consistency between what you're selling and what residents actually get. That alignment makes the job easier to do with integrity.

Six months of sales or customer-facing experience is the floor here. What separates strong candidates isn't tenure. It's coachability, follow-through, and genuine comfort being held to a number. If you like knowing exactly what "good" looks like, this role has that clarity.

job.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant - Cortland National Landing
job.description:
A prospect walks in on a Saturday afternoon, having toured three other communities that morning. They're tired, a little skeptical, and not sure any of them felt right. What happens in the next 45 minutes at Cortland National Landing is entirely up to you.

That's the core of this role. Cortland is one of the top 10 multifamily owners and operators in the country, and at National Landing in Arlington, VA, they're looking for a Leasing and Sales Consultant who treats occupancy as a number worth owning. Not just showing units. Closing leases, managing a pipeline, and building the kind of resident experience that drives renewals and referrals long after move-in day.

What This Role Actually Involves

You're responsible for moving prospects through the funnel from first contact to signed lease. That means prompt follow-up, disciplined CRM hygiene, and consultative conversations that uncover what a prospect actually needs, not just what they say they're looking for. Conversion metrics matter here. Traffic-to-lease ratios, follow-up response times, pipeline health. You'll track them, report on them, and be expected to improve them.

Beyond the sales side, you'll manage the community's marketing presence and social channels, plan resident events, and serve as the face of the brand in every interaction. Cortland positions itself as a premium product, so the experience you deliver needs to match that standard consistently, not just on busy Saturdays.

  • Drive occupancy by hitting leasing goals and conversion KPIs
  • Manage your prospect pipeline with timely follow-up and accurate CRM records
  • Use consultative selling to match residents to the right home and close with confidence
  • Handle objections professionally and turn hesitation into decisions
  • Coordinate resident engagement programs and community events
  • Maintain and grow the community's social media presence
  • Adapt your approach based on market shifts and leadership feedback

What Cortland Is Looking For

Six or more months of sales or customer-facing experience is preferred, but the attitude matters more than the resume length. Coachable, organized, and genuinely motivated by hitting a goal. You should be comfortable being held to numbers, because you will be. CRM experience helps. A polished, professional presence is expected given the brand standard Cortland maintains.

What separates strong candidates in this role from average ones is pipeline discipline. Anyone can give a good tour. The consultants who consistently hit their numbers are the ones who follow up the same day, who remember details from a prospect's first call three weeks later, and who treat every lead as worth closing. Arlington's rental market is competitive and educated. Prospects here often have multiple Class A options within a short radius. The leasing consultant who wins isn't always the one with the nicest property. It's the one who made the experience feel effortless and personal.

This role is a real launching pad within multifamily. Strong performers at the leasing consultant level who develop their sales metrics and operational awareness tend to move into assistant manager or property manager roles within two to three years, particularly within a vertically integrated platform like Cortland where internal mobility is part of how they retain talent.

job.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant - Cortland NoDa
job.description:
It's 10 a.m. on a Saturday. Three prospects have tours scheduled, two didn't show, and your phone has four unread follow-up reminders from yesterday. That's a normal morning in this role, and the people who thrive here don't wait for traffic to come to them.

What This Role Actually Is

Cortland is hiring a Leasing and Sales Consultant for their NoDa community in Charlotte. NoDa is one of Charlotte's more established urban neighborhoods, with a renter base that tends to be younger, arts-oriented, and specific about what they want in a home. You're not just showing units. You're selling a lifestyle to people who have done their research and have options.

Day to day, you're managing a sales pipeline. That means following up with leads promptly, keeping your CRM current, tracking conversion metrics, and closing leases. Cortland measures performance against real KPIs, including occupancy targets and conversion rates, so you'll know exactly where you stand at any given point in the month.

Beyond the numbers, you're the face of the community. You guide prospects through tours, handle objections without getting rattled, and build the kind of rapport that turns a signed lease into a long-term resident who refers friends. You'll also support social media presence and help plan resident events that keep the community feeling like more than just an apartment complex.

What Cortland Wants to See

  • At least six months of sales, customer service, or performance-driven experience
  • Comfort being held accountable to goals and conversion targets
  • Ability to learn and consistently use CRM tools for lead tracking
  • Strong follow-up discipline and time management habits
  • A polished, professional presence that fits a Class A brand
  • Openness to coaching and willingness to adjust your approach based on feedback

Cortland is a vertically integrated operator with in-house design, construction, and management, founded in 2005 and currently ranked among the top 10 multifamily owners and operators in the country. That scale matters for your career. Leasing consultants at large institutional operators like Cortland often move into assistant manager or leasing manager roles faster than those at smaller shops, because the training infrastructure and internal mobility are actually there.

One honest note: this role rewards consistency more than bursts of effort. The consultants who separate themselves aren't always the most charismatic. They're the ones who follow up on day two when everyone else forgot, who keep their pipeline organized when traffic slows, and who treat a slow Tuesday the same way they treat a busy Saturday.

job.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant - Cortland Pentagon City
job.description:
Cortland Pentagon City sits in one of the most competitive multifamily corridors in the DC metro area. Renters here have options. Lots of them. Your job as a Leasing and Sales Consultant is to make sure they choose this community, and then feel good about that choice long after move-in day.

What You'll Actually Do

Most of your day lives in the gap between inquiry and signed lease. You're fielding leads, qualifying prospects, scheduling and conducting tours, and following up with the kind of consistency that turns a "maybe" into a move-in. Cortland uses CRM tools to track pipeline activity, so you'll log every interaction and keep your lead data clean. That's not busywork. It's how you see where your conversion is breaking down and fix it before it costs you occupancy.

Beyond the sales motion, you're also the face of the community. You'll handle objections from prospects who are comparing you against competing communities up and down the corridor. You'll plan and support resident events that build retention. You'll manage social media presence and contribute to local marketing efforts. The role touches nearly every part of the resident lifecycle, from first click to lease renewal conversation.

Cortland is a vertically integrated operator with in-house design, construction, and management. That structure means decisions move faster than at a typical third-party management shop, and expectations around performance are specific and measurable. You'll be held to leasing goals, conversion targets, and KPIs that leadership tracks closely. That accountability is real.

What You Bring

  • At least 6 months of sales, leasing, or customer-facing experience where hitting a target actually mattered
  • Comfort with consultative selling. You listen first, then present solutions that fit what the prospect actually needs
  • Discipline around follow-up. Leads go cold fast in a high-traffic market, and consistent outreach is what separates good closers from average ones
  • Experience with or willingness to learn CRM platforms for lead tracking and pipeline management
  • Coachability. Cortland has defined processes, and they expect you to absorb feedback and apply it
  • A polished, professional presence that fits a Class A brand standard

A Few Things Worth Knowing

Pentagon City is a high-demand submarket with strong traffic, but that also means residents know what premium living looks like. They'll compare you on amenities, responsiveness, and the overall feel of the experience from the moment they walk in. The leasing consultants who perform well here aren't just friendly. They're organized, they're persistent, and they treat every tour like it matters, because it does.

This role is a real entry point into multifamily operations. Consultants who learn the sales side well, and who start understanding the connection between occupancy, concessions, and NOI, tend to grow into assistant manager or leasing manager roles within a couple of years. Cortland's scale gives you a portfolio to grow into if you perform.

If you want a role where your results are visible and your effort translates directly into outcomes, this one delivers that. Just go in knowing the pace is real, the goals are tracked, and the team around you is expected to show up ready.

job.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant-Cortland Portofino Place
job.description:
A prospect walks in on a Saturday afternoon, fresh from touring three other communities nearby. They're polite but guarded, and they've heard every pitch before. What you do in the next twenty minutes determines whether Cortland Portofino Place earns a new resident or loses one to the competition down the street. That moment is the core of this role.

What This Role Is Actually About

Cortland is one of the largest multifamily owners and operators in the country, and this position sits at the front line of occupancy and revenue for Portofino Place in West Palm Beach. You're not just processing applications. You're managing a sales pipeline: generating traffic, following up with leads through the CRM, tracking conversion metrics, and closing leases with consistency. The consultative piece matters here. You'll need to read what a prospect actually wants, not just what they say they want, and match that to what the community genuinely offers.

Beyond the sales cycle, you'll contribute to the resident experience after move-in. That means planning engagement events, maintaining a community social media presence, and building relationships that generate referrals. Cortland positions its communities as premium product, so the standard for presentation and interaction is high throughout.

What You Bring vs. What You'll Build

This role requires strong people skills, discipline around follow-up, comfort with goals and accountability, and the ability to take coaching without getting defensive. Six or more months of sales or customer-facing experience is preferred. CRM familiarity helps, though the specific platform can be learned on the job.

What the role builds is equally worth naming. You'll develop a concrete understanding of how leasing velocity, concessions, and traffic patterns connect to a property's NOI. You'll get repetition on objection handling, pipeline management, and performance reporting. Those skills transfer directly into a future as a Leasing Manager, Assistant Property Manager, or even a regional training role for operators who want people who understand the sales side of the business.

  • 6+ months of sales, customer service, or performance-driven experience preferred
  • Comfortable being measured against leasing goals and conversion targets
  • Organized with follow-up and time management across multiple active leads
  • Able to learn and maintain accurate CRM records consistently
  • Polished, approachable, and aligned with a premium brand standard
  • Coachable and willing to adjust approach based on feedback and market conditions

West Palm Beach is a competitive submarket with consistent demand and a growing number of newer Class A deliveries. Strong candidates here won't just rely on inbound traffic. They'll treat every lead like a managed relationship, not a single interaction, because in a market with options, the follow-up is often what actually closes the lease.

Cortland is a vertically integrated firm with in-house design, construction, and operations, which means decisions move faster than they do at third-party managed properties. For someone who wants to grow, that structure creates more visibility and more opportunity to be noticed early.

job.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant - Cortland Rosslyn
job.description:
Lease-up pressure and traffic-to-lease conversion are two of the most watched metrics at any Class A community. At Cortland Rosslyn, this role exists to own both. You're the first real touchpoint a prospect has with the property, and your ability to run a disciplined sales process from initial inquiry to signed lease directly moves the needle on occupancy and revenue.

Cortland is a vertically integrated operator ranked among the top 10 multifamily owners in the country. That structure matters here because it means decisions get made faster, resources are closer to the community level, and there's less friction between what you need and what you get.

What This Role Actually Looks Like Day to Day

Your pipeline is your responsibility. That means working your CRM consistently, not just logging notes after tours but actively managing follow-up sequences, tracking where each prospect is in the process, and closing with intent. A strong leasing consultant at a property like this knows their conversion rate without having to look it up.

Tours are only part of the job. You're also handling objections, building genuine rapport with people who are often making a significant financial decision under time pressure, and representing a premium brand at every interaction. Rosslyn is a competitive submarket. Prospects are comparing you to well-capitalized neighbors, so knowing your product cold and communicating its value clearly matters more than a polished script.

Beyond leasing, you'll support resident engagement programming and help maintain the community's social media presence. These aren't afterthoughts. Referral traffic and renewal rates both connect back to how well residents feel integrated into the community you're helping build.

What Cortland Is Looking For

  • At least 6 months of sales, customer service, or performance-driven experience
  • Comfort with goal-oriented environments where your numbers are visible and tracked
  • Confidence on tour and on follow-up, without being pushy
  • Organized enough to manage a live pipeline across multiple prospects simultaneously
  • Coachable. This team gives feedback and expects you to implement it.
  • CRM experience or a genuine willingness to get fluent in one quickly
  • Presentation that matches the brand standard of a premium community

The candidates who stand out here aren't necessarily the ones with the most apartment leasing experience. They're the ones who treat follow-up as a non-negotiable, ask good discovery questions instead of launching into a pitch, and care about their close rate the way a sales professional should. If you've been undersupervised and underdeveloped in a previous leasing role, Cortland's coaching culture tends to accelerate people fast.

This role is a legitimate launchpad. Leasing consultants at operators of this scale who hit their KPIs consistently move into assistant manager and property manager tracks with real speed. The metrics you build fluency with here, occupancy, traffic, conversion, pipeline velocity, are the same ones you'll manage from the other side of the desk when you step into a management role.

job.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant- Cortland Satori - Pinellas Park, FL
job.description:
Consultative selling skills sit at the center of this role. Not order-taking, not showing apartments on autopilot. Cortland Satori in Pinellas Park is looking for someone who can read a prospect, ask the right questions, and match what they're hearing to a specific home and lifestyle. That's the core skill this position exercises every single day.

What This Role Actually Demands

Your pipeline management habits will matter as much as your personality. Strong candidates keep their CRM current, follow up on a schedule, and treat every lead as a real opportunity until it's clearly closed or lost. The leasing side is genuinely sales-driven here. Cortland tracks conversion metrics, monitors lead activity, and holds consultants to KPIs. If you've thrived in a goal-oriented environment before, that muscle translates directly.

Beyond closing leases, you'll also manage the community's social media presence, plan resident events, and build the kind of relationships that generate referrals. That combination of outbound sales and community-building is what separates this from a typical leasing gig. You're the face of the property from the first inquiry email all the way through move-in and beyond.

  • Drive occupancy and revenue by hitting and exceeding leasing goals and conversion targets
  • Maintain accurate CRM records and manage your lead pipeline with consistent follow-up
  • Use consultative techniques to uncover what prospects actually need and present real solutions
  • Handle objections with confidence and turn hesitations into signed leases
  • Represent the community as an ambassador at every touchpoint, including digital channels
  • Plan and execute resident engagement programs that build loyalty and reduce turnover
  • Respond to coaching and adjust your approach based on market shifts and leadership feedback

Skills That Travel Well

The Pinellas Park rental market sits in a competitive corridor between St. Pete and Clearwater, where renter expectations skew toward lifestyle amenities and responsive management. Learning to sell effectively in this environment builds instincts that transfer to any Class A lease-up or stabilized community. Consultants who sharpen their CRM discipline, conversion tracking, and objection-handling here typically move into senior leasing, assistant manager, or even property manager roles faster than those who treat leasing as a purely transactional job.

Who Fits Here

Six or more months of sales or customer-facing experience is preferred, but the mindset matters more than the exact background. Cortland is explicit about wanting someone coachable. That means you're not defensive about feedback, you implement adjustments quickly, and you stay accountable to your numbers even when traffic is slow. A polished, approachable presence is also part of the job. Cortland positions its communities as premium experiences, and the consultant is the first proof point of that promise.

Cortland is a vertically integrated operator, which means design, construction, and management decisions run through one organization. For a leasing consultant, that translates into a better-resourced property and a company that actually controls the product it's asking you to sell. That's a meaningful difference when you're standing in front of a prospect making a case for premium living.

job.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant - Cortland Scottsdale Mountain
job.description:
Cortland Scottsdale Mountain is a premium multifamily community, and the Leasing and Sales Consultant role here is genuinely a sales position. Not a greeter. Not an order-taker. If you're the kind of person who tracks your own conversion rate and feels the sting of a lost lease, you'll fit the culture well.

The job centers on driving occupancy and hitting leasing KPIs through active pipeline management, consistent follow-up, and consultative selling. You're uncovering what a prospect actually needs, not just walking them through a floor plan and hoping they sign. That means keeping your CRM clean, following up when you said you would, and closing with confidence rather than waiting for residents to come back to you. Scottsdale's rental market draws a mix of relocating professionals, retirees downsizing from single-family homes, and seasonal residents, so your ability to read different buyers and adjust your pitch matters more than any script.

The resident experience side of this role is real work, not just a talking point. You'll plan and execute resident events, maintain the community's social media presence, and handle objections in ways that keep the relationship intact even when you can't solve a problem immediately. The best consultants here understand that a strong referral pipeline reduces dependence on paid traffic, and they build toward that deliberately.

One honest note about the dynamics: leasing roles at premium communities carry brand expectations that can feel demanding. Presentation standards, tone, and follow-up timeliness are watched closely. If you respond well to coaching and can implement feedback without taking it personally, that's a genuine advantage here. If you need a lot of runway before adjusting your approach, this probably isn't the right fit.

This role also builds skills that carry well into assistant management and eventually community management. The consultative sales experience, CRM discipline, and resident engagement work all translate directly into what an effective property manager does daily. Cortland is a vertically integrated operator with in-house construction, design, and management, so internal mobility is a real option for people who perform.

What Cortland is Looking For

  • At least 6 months of sales, customer-facing, or performance-driven experience
  • Comfortable being measured against goals and motivated by hitting them
  • Confident communicator who builds rapport quickly and handles objections without deflating
  • Organized follow-up habits and working knowledge of CRM platforms
  • Coachable, consistent, and able to maintain a polished presence that reflects a premium brand

Cortland was founded in 2005 and is recognized as one of the top 10 multifamily owners and operators in the country. They're an equal opportunity employer and participate in E-Verify.

job.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant - Cortland University City
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role is the front line of occupancy at Cortland University City, a Class A community in Charlotte's University City submarket. Your day centers on converting traffic into signed leases. That means working a sales pipeline with real discipline: timely follow-up, CRM entries that actually reflect where each prospect stands, and closing conversations that feel like consultations, not pitches. You'll use consultative techniques to understand what a prospect genuinely needs and then connect those needs to what the community offers.

Beyond the leasing office, you're also the face of the resident experience. You'll plan and run resident events, manage the community's social media presence, and handle objections from prospects without getting rattled. Some days the work is high-energy and social. Other days it's methodical: auditing your pipeline, reviewing conversion metrics, tightening up your follow-up cadence. Both matter equally here.

What You Bring

  • At least six months of experience in sales, customer service, or any performance-tracked role where results were measurable
  • Comfort working toward leasing goals and KPIs, and being held accountable to them consistently
  • Experience with or willingness to learn a CRM system for tracking leads and prospect interactions
  • Strong follow-through. Prospects fall through cracks because of poor follow-up more often than poor product.
  • A coachable mindset. Cortland is a large, vertically integrated operator with established best practices, and they expect you to implement feedback quickly.
  • Professional presentation that reflects a premium brand at every touchpoint

Context Worth Knowing

Cortland is one of the ten largest multifamily owners and operators in the country. That scale matters for this role in a specific way: you're not working for a small fee-management shop where systems are improvised. Processes here are documented, conversion benchmarks exist, and performance is tracked closely. That structure is an asset if you're someone who wants clear feedback on where you stand and a defined path to improve.

What separates a strong leasing consultant at a community like this from an average one isn't charm. It's pipeline discipline. Plenty of people can give a good tour. The consultants who consistently hit occupancy goals are the ones who follow up on day two, not day eight, and who keep their CRM current enough to actually use it as a tool rather than a chore. If you build those habits here, they transfer directly to leasing manager and assistant manager roles where you're overseeing someone else's pipeline and coaching for the same behaviors.

Charlotte's University City corridor continues to see apartment demand driven by proximity to UNC Charlotte and healthcare employment. For a leasing consultant, that means a predictable seasonal traffic pattern with strong late-summer volume. Learning to work that cycle well, including building referral relationships during peak periods that pay off in slower months, is one of the more transferable skills you'll develop in this position.

job.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant - Cortland Vesta

Leasing consultants at Cortland aren't just showing floor plans and handing out applications. Occupancy doesn't manage itself. You'll own the full leasing cycle: generating and nurturing leads, running tours, handling objections, and closing leases. CRM discipline matters here. That approach tends to produce better close rates and stronger resident retention downstream.

Beyond leasing, you'll support community marketing and social media presence and help plan resident events that keep occupancy sticky. Renewals start with the experience you create before someone even signs their first lease.

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

Cortland operates as a vertically integrated platform with in-house construction, design, and management. That means the product is consistent and the brand standard is specific. Organized because pipeline management falls apart without disciplined follow-up. Coachable because Cortland tracks KPIs closely and will give you direct feedback on what's working and what isn't.

job.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant - Cortland Whitehall
job.description:

This role is the front line of revenue at Cortland Whitehall. You're responsible for driving occupancy through active sales, not just answering questions and handing out applications. That means working a real pipeline: following up on leads, tracking conversion metrics in the CRM, handling objections, and closing leases with intention. You'll use consultative selling to figure out what a prospect actually needs, then connect those needs to what the community offers. Generic pitches don't work here.

Beyond the sales mechanics, you're also the face of the resident experience. You'll guide people through the leasing process from first tour to signed lease, manage social media presence, plan resident events, and build the kind of relationships that generate referrals. Cortland operates as a vertically integrated multifamily company, which means the brand standards are specific and consistent. You're expected to represent that consistently, not just when it's convenient.

A typical day mixes inbound traffic with outbound follow-up. You might give two tours in the morning, spend time in the afternoon working your follow-up queue, respond to online leads, update CRM records, and coordinate an upcoming resident event. It's a rhythm that rewards discipline and organization, not just personality.

What You Bring

  • At least 6 months of experience in a sales, customer-facing, or performance-measured role
  • Comfort being held to specific targets, including conversion rates and leasing goals
  • Genuine ability to build rapport quickly with people from different backgrounds
  • Willingness to take coaching and apply it without ego
  • Organized follow-up habits and solid time management, especially when juggling multiple prospects
  • Familiarity with or ability to learn CRM platforms for tracking leads and activity
  • A polished, professional presence that fits a Class A community environment

Where This Role Can Take You

Leasing consultant is one of the best entry points in multifamily because the skills compound quickly. You're learning sales pipeline management, objection handling, resident relations, and performance accountability all at once. From here, the natural trajectory runs toward leasing manager, assistant community manager, and eventually community manager. The people who accelerate fastest are usually the ones who treat the CRM seriously from day one and ask good questions about what's driving their conversion numbers, not just whether they hit their weekly goal.

Charlotte's multifamily market has stayed competitive, with strong renter demand and continued lease-up activity across the metro. Working at a recognized top-10 operator gives you a resume credential that travels well if you decide to move into a different market or a different operator down the road.

job.title: Maintenance Technician
job.description:
Cortland runs a tight operation. One of the top 10 multifamily owners in the country, vertically integrated from design through construction through day-to-day management, they don't hand off the hard stuff to third parties. That structure means their maintenance teams carry real responsibility, and the properties reflect it.

This Maintenance Technician role is based in Atlanta and sits at the center of resident satisfaction and asset preservation. You're not just turning wrenches. On any given day you might be diagnosing an A/C issue in one unit, walking a punch list on a make-ready, checking that surveillance cameras and access controls are functioning, and closing out work orders before the shift ends. The pace is real. Residents notice response time, and so does management.

The technical scope covers the full range: electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliances, flooring, stairs, railings, gas fixtures. You'll also be responsible for flagging and correcting unsafe conditions immediately, which takes judgment, not just technical skill. Preventive maintenance scheduling is part of the role too, keeping equipment from becoming a capital problem down the road. Inventory management matters here as well. Running out of a common part mid-work-order costs time and trust.

On-call rotation is part of the deal. Resident and property emergencies don't keep business hours, and Cortland expects their technicians to be available when it counts. That's the honest reality of this work.

What separates a strong candidate from an average one here isn't just the EPA certification, it's the ability to triage accurately. A tech who can walk into a problem, correctly identify the root cause on the first visit, and close the work order clean keeps NOI healthy. Repeat visits, deferred fixes, and incomplete work orders chip away at resident satisfaction scores and create downstream costs that show up in the T-12. Cortland tracks performance closely, and the best technicians treat their work order log the way a leasing team treats occupancy.

Cortland does offer in-house training through certified proctors for EPA, CPO, and CPR, which is genuinely useful if you're looking to build credentials while you work. There's also an individual growth plan structure, so the path toward lead tech or maintenance supervisor isn't invisible.

What you'll bring

  • EPA certification (required)
  • 1 to 3 years of experience in facilities services or multifamily maintenance
  • Working knowledge of electrical, plumbing, HVAC, and general apartment systems
  • Ability to identify and correct unsafe conditions quickly and accurately
  • Comfort with on-call scheduling and emergency response
  • Organized approach to inventory and work order management

Cortland is an equal opportunity employer and participates in E-Verify. This is a drug-free workplace.

job.title: Maintenance Technician
job.description:
Maintenance Technician at a Top-10 Multifamily Operator

Cortland runs a vertically integrated operation across a large national portfolio, which means their maintenance teams aren't just fixing things. They're protecting real assets, keeping residents satisfied, and feeding data back into a system that tracks performance seriously. This role at their Winter Garden community puts you at the center of that daily operation.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your days will cover the full range of residential maintenance. Electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliances, flooring, railings, and everything in between. You'll walk units, diagnose problems, and either resolve them directly or route the work to the right person with a clear timeline. That last part matters more than people expect. Knowing when to fix something yourself and when to coordinate is what keeps work orders from stacking up and residents from getting frustrated.

You'll also carry responsibility for safety conditions across the property. Open hazards, broken fixtures, failed lighting, malfunctioning access points or cameras. These aren't things that wait for a convenient moment, and Cortland expects them handled promptly. Preventive maintenance scheduling is part of the job too, so you're not just reactive. You're keeping equipment healthy before it fails.

On-call shifts are part of this role. That's honest, and worth knowing upfront. Emergency coverage for both residents and the property is expected, and if that's something you've done before, you already know it comes with the territory in multifamily maintenance.

  • EPA certification required
  • 1 to 3 years of experience in facilities or multifamily maintenance
  • Hands-on experience with HVAC, plumbing, and electrical systems
  • Comfortable with resident-facing communication and service
  • Ability to identify and correct unsafe property conditions quickly

Training, Growth, and What Cortland Offers

One thing Cortland does differently is keep certification training in-house. They have CPO, EPA, and CPR certified proctors on staff, so if you need to fill gaps in your credentials, that support exists internally. There's also online training you can work through at your own pace, and an individual growth plan built around where you actually want to go professionally.

Compensation includes monthly commission on top of base pay, plus full benefits. The commission structure tied to maintenance performance is more common at larger institutional operators like Cortland than at smaller fee-management shops, and it's a meaningful difference in total earnings potential.

Technicians who build strong diagnostic skills and a track record of low delinquency in work order completion tend to move into lead tech or maintenance supervisor roles within a few years at companies this size. The portfolio depth Cortland carries means there's room to grow without switching employers.

Winter Garden sits in the western Orlando metro, an area that's seen consistent apartment demand driven by population growth and ongoing development.
