job.description:
Multi-site leasing roles exist because a single consultant covering multiple communities has to be sharper with time, more disciplined with follow-up, and quicker to read a prospect than someone working one property. Cortland is hiring a Leasing and Sales Consultant to cover Cortland Benbrook in Fort Worth, TX, and the expectation is clear: you own occupancy.

What This Job Actually Is

This is a sales role. Cortland frames it that way openly, which is honest. You're managing a pipeline, hitting conversion targets, and closing leases. That means prompt follow-up, accurate CRM entries, and knowing where every prospect stands at any given moment. You're not waiting for walk-ins. You're working leads.

The resident experience side is real too. You'll guide people through the leasing process, handle objections without losing your composure, and plan community events that keep residents connected. You'll also manage the community's social media presence and marketing programs. It's a wider scope than a standard leasing consultant position.

Cortland is a vertically integrated operator, which means design, construction, and management all sit under one roof. For you, that translates to a consistent product standard and less explaining away deferred maintenance or vendor delays. The communities reflect a premium positioning, and your presentation needs to match that.

Who Fits This Role

  • At least 6 months of sales, customer-facing, or performance-driven experience
  • Comfortable being measured by goals, conversion rates, and KPIs
  • Organized enough to manage follow-up across multiple leads without things slipping
  • Confident on the phone and in person, with the ability to build rapport quickly
  • Willing to take coaching and adjust your approach based on feedback
  • Familiar with or able to learn CRM systems without much hand-holding

What separates strong candidates here isn't just closing ability. It's pipeline discipline. The consultants who struggle in multi-site leasing roles tend to be reactive, good at helping people who show up but inconsistent with the leads who went quiet after a tour. If you have a system for follow-up and you stick to it, that matters more than raw charisma.

Cortland is one of the top 10 multifamily owners and operators in the country. For someone early in a property management career, that's worth noting. Leasing consultants who perform at operators this size tend to have a clear path toward assistant manager and property manager roles, because the infrastructure for internal promotion is actually in place.

job.title: Multi-Site Leasing & Sales Consultant-Cortland North Brookhaven
job.description:
Leasing is a sales job. That's easy to say and harder to accept, but Cortland builds its leasing teams around people who genuinely embrace that reality. This multi-site Leasing and Sales Consultant role at Cortland North Brookhaven in Atlanta asks you to bring a specific combination of skills to work every day: consultative selling, relationship management, CRM discipline, and the kind of personal polish that holds up when a prospect walks through the door unannounced.

The Skills This Role Runs On

Consultative selling sits at the center of this position. You're not reciting floor plan dimensions. You're asking questions, listening for what actually matters to someone, and then connecting that back to what Cortland offers. That requires patience and genuine curiosity about people, not just a scripted pitch. Pipeline management matters equally here. You'll track leads in a CRM, follow up consistently, and monitor your own conversion metrics. If you're the kind of person who lets leads go cold because you got busy, this role will feel like a grind fast.

The multi-site component adds a layer that single-property leasing roles don't have. You're splitting your focus across communities, which means time management isn't just a soft skill on a resume. It's the thing that determines whether your traffic actually converts or slips through because no one followed up on day three.

What You're Responsible For

  • Hitting occupancy and revenue KPIs across multiple Cortland communities in the Atlanta market
  • Managing your sales pipeline with real follow-up cadence, not just logging notes after the fact
  • Guiding prospects through the leasing process in a way that feels personal, not transactional
  • Handling objections directly and honestly, without deflecting
  • Supporting social media presence and resident engagement programs that reinforce community identity
  • Keeping CRM records current and accurate so the team has visibility into lead activity and pipeline health

Resident events and community programming are also part of the job. That might sound secondary to leasing, but retention is revenue. A resident who feels connected to their community renews. One who doesn't starts browsing.

What Sets Strong Candidates Apart Here

Cortland operates as a vertically integrated company, meaning design, construction, and management all happen in-house. That gives their leasing teams a real product story to tell, not just a third-party pitch. Strong candidates here know how to use that context. They can speak to the brand with specificity rather than generic claims about "premium living."

You don't need multifamily experience specifically. Six months in any customer-facing, goal-driven environment translates well if you're coachable and competitive. What separates the people who thrive in Cortland leasing roles from those who wash out early is usually two things: willingness to accept feedback quickly and the discipline to follow up when it would be easier not to. Atlanta is a competitive Class A market. Traffic doesn't automatically convert. The follow-up does.

If you've been in retail sales, hospitality, or a service role where you were held to measurable targets and had to manage your own book of relationships, that background fits here more than you might expect.

job.title: Multi-Site Leasing & Sales Consultant - Cortland North Plano
job.description:
Cortland runs one of the largest apartment portfolios in the country, and this multi-site leasing role sits right at the front edge of that operation. You'd be splitting time between two North Plano communities, which means you'll sharpen your ability to context-switch fast, read two distinct resident profiles, and keep your pipeline organized across properties simultaneously. That's a real skill, and it transfers well.

The core of this job is sales. Not passive, wait-for-walk-ins leasing, but active pipeline management with follow-up cadences, conversion tracking, and CRM discipline. You'll use consultative techniques to figure out what a prospect actually needs, then connect those needs to the living experience Cortland delivers. Closing a lease here means you earned it.

Beyond the numbers, you're also the face of the brand at both communities. That means planning resident events, maintaining social media presence, and handling objections in a way that keeps the relationship intact even when the answer isn't what someone wanted to hear. The people who do this role well don't just hit occupancy goals. They build the kind of resident loyalty that shows up in renewals and referrals.

What This Role Builds

If you come in with some customer-facing or sales experience, this role will push you toward a more structured, data-informed version of that skill set. You'll get comfortable reading your own conversion metrics, adjusting your approach based on traffic patterns and market conditions, and taking coaching without getting defensive about it. Those habits matter at every level of property management, from leasing to assistant manager to community manager and beyond. Multi-site exposure early in a leasing career is genuinely uncommon, and it tends to accelerate how quickly people get promoted.

What separates strong candidates here isn't just charisma. It's follow-through. The leasing consultants who struggle are usually the ones who can hold a great tour but let leads go cold because the CRM felt like extra work. The ones who advance treat follow-up as the job, not the afterthought.

Qualifications Cortland is looking for:

  • At least 6 months of sales, customer service, or performance-driven experience
  • Comfort being measured against goals and KPIs on a regular basis
  • Organized follow-up habits and willingness to keep CRM records current
  • Coachable mindset with the ability to adjust your approach based on feedback
  • Polished, professional presence that fits a premium brand environment
  • Ability to work across two communities without letting either slip

If you want a leasing role where the expectation is that you sell, not just show, and where the company has the infrastructure to actually support your growth, this is a legitimate opportunity to build something real in multifamily.

job.title: National Leasing and Sales Consultant
job.description:
Most leasing roles put you in one community, working the same traffic funnel week after week. This one is built differently. Cortland's National Leasing and Sales Consultant position exists because even well-run portfolios hit moments where a single property needs a closer, not a caretaker. Lease-ups that stall, acquisitions that need immediate occupancy momentum, underperforming assets dragging on NOI. That's where this role lands.

Cortland is one of the top 10 multifamily owners and operators in the country, founded in 2005 and vertically integrated across investment, development, and operations. This isn't a floating leasing role in the traditional sense. It's a revenue deployment function. You're sent in with a specific mission, a short timeline, and the expectation that you'll move the needle before you leave.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

On any given assignment, you might be stabilizing a newly acquired community in Phoenix, accelerating lease-up velocity at a new development in Charlotte, or covering a critical staffing gap in Miami during peak season. You'll convert qualified traffic into signed leases, run consultative tours, manage your pipeline with discipline, and work through objections without hesitation. The role requires up to 80% travel, including extended assignments of eight weeks or more on short notice. If that sounds disruptive, this probably isn't your role. If it sounds like the kind of variety that keeps you sharp, read on.

When you're not on assignment, you'll pull performance data, identify where on-site teams are losing deals, and work with Cortland's Learning and Development team to sharpen their execution. You're not just closing leases. You're raising the floor for every community you touch.

What Cortland Is Looking For

  • At least three years of top-tier performance in a high-velocity, short-cycle sales environment: multifamily leasing, hospitality, luxury retail, timeshare, or similar fields
  • Documented history of exceeding conversion metrics, quota, and KPIs, not just meeting them
  • Real experience in lease-ups, property turnarounds, or regional floating roles supporting multiple markets
  • Comfort operating autonomously in unfamiliar environments with minimal ramp-up time
  • Strong CRM discipline and pipeline management habits
  • Willingness and ability to travel extensively, including on short notice

What separates strong candidates here isn't just a high close rate. It's the ability to walk into a community you've never seen, read the local competitive set quickly, identify where the leasing process is breaking down, and produce results before the assignment ends. That requires pattern recognition built from real exposure to diverse markets and asset types. Candidates who've worked only one community or one market for their entire career tend to struggle with the adaptation speed this role demands.

Cortland operates across Atlanta, Dallas, Charlotte, Orlando, Miami, Phoenix, and Denver, among other markets. Each of those metros has its own concession norms, traffic seasonality, and competitive dynamics. The ability to absorb that context fast, and still close, is the core skill this role is built around.

job.title: Part Time Front Door Waste Technician - Cortland Galleria
job.description:
It's 8 p.m. on a Tuesday, and residents along your assigned route have set their trash bags outside their doors. Your job is to move through the community quietly and efficiently, collect every bag, and leave each doorstep clean before the night is over. That's the core of this role.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Cortland is hiring a part-time Front Door Waste Technician at Cortland Galleria in Farmers Branch, Texas. This is a valet trash position supporting a multifamily apartment community. You'll walk the property on scheduled service nights, collect waste bags from resident doorsteps, transfer them to a designated vehicle or trailer, and dispose of everything into onsite compactors or dumpsters. The route doesn't stop at front doors. You'll also clear waste from dog stations, pool areas, mail kiosks, and common spaces, and pick up loose trash along sidewalks and parking lots.

Logging your work matters here. Cortland uses a mobile app to track route start and end times and to document conditions during your shift, so a working smartphone with a data plan is a genuine requirement, not a formality. You'll also be expected to photograph any issues or spills you encounter.

Physical demands are real. Expect to climb stairs repeatedly, carry bags weighing up to 50 pounds, and work in outdoor conditions that Farmers Branch summers will test. You'll need a valid Class E driver's license to operate the vehicle between stops or properties as needed.

What Cortland Is Looking For

  • High school diploma or equivalent preferred
  • At least one year of experience in waste services or a hands-on service environment
  • Working knowledge of EPA and OSHA safety standards
  • Proficiency in English; bilingual candidates are a plus
  • Ability to interact with residents and staff in a professional, courteous manner
  • Comfort reading and applying written equipment instructions
  • Availability for day, evening, and weekend shifts as scheduled
  • Valid Class E driver's license and a smartphone capable of running the tracking app

One thing that separates strong candidates in this role: consistency. Residents notice when their doorstep isn't serviced, and a missed bag or a mess left near the compactor creates real friction between the property team and the people who live there. If you take the route seriously and treat it like a service commitment rather than just a pickup job, you'll fit well here.

Cortland is a vertically integrated multifamily owner and operator ranked among the top 10 in the country. The company has built its reputation on in-house operations and a resident-first approach, and that philosophy extends to how they staff and support roles at every level of the property.

job.title: Part-Time Leasing & Sales Consultant-Cortland Benbrook
job.description:
Cortland's leasing teams don't just fill units. They're accountable to conversion rates, pipeline velocity, and occupancy targets that feed directly into property-level NOI. At Cortland Benbrook in Fort Worth, this part-time Leasing and Sales Consultant role is built for someone who treats every prospect interaction as a real sales opportunity, not just a tour.

The day-to-day breaks down simply: you're managing your own lead pipeline, following up with prospects who haven't signed, and closing leases with enough consistency to hit KPIs. Cortland uses CRM tools to track every touchpoint, so your pipeline health is visible and your conversion metrics are on record. That's not a pressure tactic. It's just how performance-driven leasing works at a top-10 national operator. If you're someone who likes seeing your numbers and beating them, that structure is motivating rather than stressful.

The consultative piece matters here. Cortland's portfolio sits in a competitive Class A and B space where residents have options. Knowing how to uncover what a prospect actually needs, then connecting that to what the community offers, is what separates a strong leasing consultant from someone who just recites floor plans and square footage. Objection handling is real. You'll encounter prospects who toured two other communities the same afternoon, and your follow-up discipline is often what closes the deal days later.

Beyond leasing, you'll have a hand in resident experience: events, social media presence, and the kind of day-to-day engagement that turns a satisfied resident into a referral source. Renewals start with the first impression, and this role has influence over both ends of that equation.

What Cortland is looking for

  • At least 6 months of sales, customer service, or performance-tracked work experience
  • Comfort being measured against goals and held accountable to conversion targets
  • Organized follow-up habits and attention to CRM accuracy
  • A coachable mindset. Cortland invests in development and expects you to apply feedback quickly
  • Professional presentation that fits a premium brand environment
  • Willingness to learn leasing software and CRM platforms if not already familiar

For candidates newer to property management, this kind of role builds a skill set that transfers well into full-time leasing, assistant manager work, or eventually community management. The combination of sales accountability, resident relations, and operational familiarity with a vertically integrated company like Cortland is a solid foundation. Cortland handles design, construction, and operations in-house, which means you're working inside a machine that moves faster and with more data than most third-party managed communities. That's worth understanding before you step in.

job.title: Residential Concierge - 3700M
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

The front desk at 3700M is a busy place. You're the first face residents see when they walk in, the person vendors check in with, and the one who fields questions that don't have a clear owner. On a typical shift, that might mean issuing a replacement access card in the morning, coordinating a work order with the maintenance team by midday, and restocking the amenity spaces before the afternoon rush. No two days are identical.

Cortland runs a resident-centric operation, which means the concierge role carries real weight. You'll manage the front desk and lobby, keep shared spaces clean and organized, handle package and mail coordination, and support the broader onsite team including leasing, management, and maintenance. You'll also help execute resident events and community programs, which requires some logistical thinking beyond just showing up with a smile. Access credentials, parking passes, visitor logs, amenity reservations, daily opening and closing procedures: all of it runs through you.

This isn't a role where you wait to be told what to do. The best concierges notice things before residents have to ask. A lobby that's slightly off, a supply that's running low, a vendor who hasn't checked in. You catch it first.

What You Bring

  • At least 3 years in a customer-facing role, ideally in residential, hospitality, or a professional front-desk setting
  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Comfort with Microsoft Office and basic office technology
  • Familiarity with mail and package handling through USPS, FedEx, and UPS
  • Strong organizational skills and the kind of attention to detail that keeps operations from slipping
  • Ability to lift and move items up to 15 to 20 pounds
  • Genuine interpersonal skills, not just a polished surface but actual follow-through

Context Worth Knowing

Cortland is one of the larger vertically integrated multifamily operators in the country, founded in 2005 and consistently ranked among the top 10 multifamily owners in the US. That structure, in-house design, construction, and operations under one roof, means the onsite teams tend to have more internal resources than you'd find at a third-party managed property. For a concierge, that translates to clearer processes and better coordination with maintenance and management, though it also means expectations for professionalism and consistency are high.

One honest note: this role is the face of the property. When something goes wrong, whether it's a noise complaint, a package that's gone missing, or a resident who's had a bad day, you're often the first person they come to. Handling that well, staying calm and solution-oriented without overpromising, is genuinely one of the harder parts of the job. If that kind of pressure appeals to you rather than drains you, this position fits.

For candidates thinking about career trajectory, concierge experience at a Class A community like this one builds a foundation that transfers directly into leasing, resident relations, or community management roles. The skills you develop here, conflict resolution, operational coordination, resident retention instincts, are the same ones that matter at every level of property management.

job.title: Resident Services Specialist, Bilingual - Sandy Springs, GA
job.description:
Bilingual communication skills sit at the center of this role. Not as a checkbox, but as the actual mechanism through which residents get help, concerns get resolved, and Cortland's reputation holds up across thousands of interactions. If you're fluent in English and Spanish and you genuinely enjoy talking to people through complicated moments, this is a job where that combination matters every single day.

What This Role Actually Requires

Cortland runs a centralized Contact Center model, which means you're not sitting at a leasing office desk. You're handling resident calls, emails, and chat inquiries across multiple properties simultaneously, often for residents you've never met in person. The skill set this demands is specific: you need to hold multiple conversations in your head at once, switch between platforms quickly, and still make each resident feel like their issue is the only one on your screen.

De-escalation is a real part of this job. Residents call when things go wrong. Maintenance hasn't shown up, a charge looks wrong, a neighbor is loud at 2am. Your patience and tone in those moments directly affect whether someone renews or gives notice. That's not a soft skill. It's a retention driver.

You'll also handle documentation: reviewing applications, verifying information, keeping records accurate inside systems like RealPage, OneSite, and Funnel. Fair Housing compliance isn't a formality here. It applies to every interaction, and Cortland takes it seriously.

Skills That Match This Role

  • 2 or more years in a high-volume customer service or contact center environment, with multifamily experience strongly preferred
  • Bilingual fluency in English and Spanish (written and spoken)
  • Comfort working inside CRM tools and property management platforms, including RealPage and OneSite
  • Strong organizational habits when managing competing priorities under pressure
  • Ability to handle confidential resident information with discretion
  • Flexibility for weekend and extended hours as business needs shift
  • High school diploma or equivalent required

What Makes Cortland a Different Kind of Operator

Cortland is vertically integrated, which means design, construction, and operations all sit under the same roof. For a Resident Services Specialist, that structure matters because the people you're coordinating with internally actually have ownership over outcomes. You're not relaying messages to a third-party vendor with no accountability. You're working inside a company that controls more of the process than most multifamily operators do.

One honest note: centralized contact center work requires discipline that's different from on-site leasing. You don't have the benefit of reading body language or walking a resident to a unit. Everything you accomplish happens through words and tone, at speed, across a screen. The people who thrive here tend to be organized under pressure, genuinely curious about solving problems, and comfortable sitting in ambiguity when a resident's situation doesn't fit a neat category. That's the real bar for this role.

job.title: Screening & Applications Specialist - Sandy Springs, GA
job.description:
Most leasing roles ask you to hand off an applicant after the tour. This one picks up right where that handoff ends. As a Screening and Applications Specialist at Cortland, you own the applicant relationship from submitted application all the way through move-in day. That's a narrower slice of the leasing process than a traditional Leasing Consultant handles, but it runs deeper.

Cortland is one of the ten largest multifamily owners and operators in the country, and they run a vertically integrated operation out of Atlanta. This role sits at the Sandy Springs location and functions as a centralized processing hub for applications across communities. You're not chasing leads or conducting tours. You're the person who keeps the back half of the leasing funnel moving cleanly.

Day-to-day, that means reviewing applications in OneSite, verifying income documentation and rental history, generating accurate leases before move-in dates, and following up on pre-move-in requirements like renters insurance, utility transfers, and co-signer agreements. When an application doesn't meet criteria, you're the one issuing adverse action letters correctly. You're also maintaining CRM data, running daily reports for on-site teams, and tracking key dates so nothing slips. It's detailed, compliance-adjacent work that requires someone who genuinely likes process.

Here's what separates strong candidates in this specific role: the ability to communicate the same decision clearly to a frustrated applicant, a leasing agent, and a community manager, each of whom needs to hear it differently. You won't always be delivering good news. The people who thrive here stay calm, stay accurate, and don't let urgency push them into shortcuts with documentation or fair housing requirements.

What Cortland is Looking for

  • At least two years in an organized, collaborative role. Multifamily or real estate experience is preferred but not required.
  • A bachelor's degree or equivalent business background.
  • Working knowledge of lease and application procedures, rental criteria, and applicant privacy standards.
  • Strong written and verbal communication across all levels of an organization.
  • Comfort with CRM systems and property management software, specifically OneSite.
  • Discretion with confidential applicant information.
  • Flexibility for weekend or extended hours when needed.
  • Bilingual ability is a genuine plus here given applicant diversity.

From a career trajectory standpoint, this role builds skills that translate directly into leasing management, compliance coordination, or operations roles at a larger scale. Because you're working across multiple communities rather than one, you develop a portfolio-wide perspective early. People who handle application processing well tend to become the go-to resource when a company needs to standardize leasing workflows during a lease-up or portfolio expansion. That's a useful reputation to build at a firm the size of Cortland.

job.title: Screening & Applications Specialist - Sandy Springs, GA
job.description:
Cortland is one of the largest multifamily owners and operators in the country, and this role sits at the center of how residents actually get into their homes. The Screening and Applications Specialist owns the applicant experience from submitted application to move-in day. That's a narrow window with a lot of moving parts.

The work is detail-heavy. You'll review income documentation, rental history, photo ID, employment verification, and credit and criminal background results. You'll generate leases, coordinate signatures, confirm renter's insurance and utility setup, and send adverse action letters when applications don't clear. Every step has a compliance dimension, and you're expected to follow established criteria consistently, not improvise.

You'll also manage reporting. Daily updates to communities, funnel and CRM maintenance, lease data input, final account statement close-outs. This isn't a job where you handle one thing at a time. Multiple applications are moving simultaneously, each at a different stage, and your job is to make sure nothing falls through.

The resident-facing side matters too. Applicants are often anxious about the process. They want answers quickly. You're the point of contact, which means you're fielding questions, driving pre-move-in conversations, and keeping things moving without pushing people away. Good written communication isn't optional here. Neither is discretion. You're handling sensitive personal and financial information every day.

What makes a strong candidate here

The applicants who stand out for this role aren't just organized. They understand that inconsistency in the screening process creates fair housing exposure. Cortland operates at scale across a large portfolio, so the stakes for applying criteria unevenly are real. Candidates who've worked in multifamily leasing or property management, specifically with OneSite or a similar platform, will ramp up faster. Bilingual ability is a genuine plus given the applicant pool in the Atlanta metro.

  • 2 or more years in an organized, high-volume administrative or customer-facing role
  • Familiarity with application and lease procedures, rental criteria, and fair housing basics
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills across different audiences
  • Comfortable with CRM tools, reporting, and data entry with a low tolerance for errors
  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent business background preferred
  • Flexibility to work weekends or extended hours when needed

This role is based in Sandy Springs, GA. Cortland is a drug-free workplace and participates in E-Verify.

job.title: Screening & Applications Specialist - Sandy Springs, GA
job.description:
What This Role Actually Does

This is not a leasing agent position. You won't be showing units or closing tours. What you will do is own the application process from submission to move-in, which means you're the person applicants hear from most during one of the more stressful stretches of renting an apartment. Every day looks roughly the same in structure but different in detail: reviewing incoming applications, verifying income documentation, pulling credit and criminal reports, generating accurate leases, and chasing down missing items before a move-in date slips.

You'll work inside OneSite to load and manage documentation, maintain the CRM for task tracking and key dates, and produce daily reports for the communities you support. When an application gets denied, you're drafting and sending the adverse action letter. When a move-in is two days out and the applicant still hasn't confirmed renter's insurance or updated utilities, you're the one driving that conversation to a close. The administrative side is real and constant.

Cortland is a top-10 multifamily owner and operator in the U.S., vertically integrated with in-house design, construction, and management. That scale means this role serves multiple communities, not just one property. You'll coordinate with on-site leasing teams and corporate operations simultaneously, so your communication has to be clear and your documentation has to be accurate the first time.

What You Bring to This Role

  • At least two years in a role that demanded tight organization, scheduling, and cross-functional collaboration. Multifamily or real estate experience is preferred but not required if you've done similar work in a high-volume, customer-facing environment.
  • A working knowledge of lease and application procedures, rental criteria, applicant consent requirements, and privacy standards around applicant data.
  • Strong written communication. You'll tailor messages to applicants, on-site teams, and corporate stakeholders, and the tone needs to shift accordingly.
  • Comfort with confidential information. Income documents, credit reports, and criminal history are part of your daily workflow.
  • A bachelor's degree or relevant business education.
  • Flexibility to work weekends or extended hours when the leasing calendar demands it.
  • Bilingual ability is a plus, particularly helpful in applicant-facing communication.

What This Role Builds

Specialists who do this job well develop a detailed understanding of the full lease-up cycle that most on-site leasing agents never get. You'll see how documentation errors create move-in delays, how delinquency sometimes traces back to incomplete screening at the front end, and how process consistency across a portfolio protects both the resident and the investor. Those are transferable skills. From here, the natural path moves toward leasing management, compliance coordination, or operations roles within a larger portfolio. At a company the size of Cortland, those next steps are genuinely available to people who perform.

One honest note: this role carries real responsibility around applicant decisions that affect people's housing. Getting comfortable with that weight, while still moving quickly and accurately, is what separates someone who's good at this from someone who's great at it.

job.title: Senior Leasing Hub Associate - Sandy Springs, GA
job.description:
This is a centralized, hub-based leasing role. You're not sitting at a property front desk greeting walk-ins. You're working remotely or from a central office, handling a high volume of inbound and outbound leads across multiple Cortland communities in the market.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your day runs on lead volume. Expect to manage 40 to 50 leads daily through RealPage's OneSite platform, working calls, emails, and texts from prospects who are at various stages of their apartment search. Your job is to qualify those leads, match each prospect to the right Cortland community based on their needs, and hand off warm, appointment-ready prospects to the onsite leasing teams.

You need to know Cortland's communities well. Pricing, floor plans, amenities, and local positioning. Prospects will ask detailed questions, and you can't afford to stumble. Cortland provides training and site visits to build that knowledge, but you have to put in the work to retain it.

This is a sales role. Outbound activity is a real expectation, not an afterthought. If you're more comfortable waiting for inbound traffic than initiating contact, this isn't the right fit.

What You Bring

  • At least two years in customer service, with a clear sales component
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, bilingual candidates welcome
  • Comfort with OneSite (RealPage); familiarity with L2L is a plus
  • Fast, accurate typing and solid computer skills overall
  • Ability to sustain energy and quality across a high volume of daily contacts

Where This Role Can Take You

Hub leasing roles are relatively new in multifamily, and associates who excel here build a skill set that translates well. You'll develop deep knowledge of how leads move through a leasing funnel, how to handle objections at scale, and how to coordinate across multiple properties simultaneously. That cross-community exposure is something most traditional leasing consultants don't get. Strong performers here often move into senior leasing, leasing manager, or training roles within larger operators like Cortland.

Cortland is a top-10 multifamily owner and operator in the US, vertically integrated with in-house design and construction. The organization is large enough to offer real career movement, and this role puts you in front of the leasing function at the portfolio level from day one.

job.title: Senior Maintenance Technician - Cortland on Blake
job.description:
Senior maintenance roles at well-capitalized multifamily operators tend to get underestimated as career moves. They shouldn't. A Senior Technician position at a company like Cortland, which operates vertically integrated across design, construction, and property management, puts you in contact with systems-level thinking that most technicians at smaller operators never see. If Service Manager is on your radar, this is the role that gets you there.

Cortland on Blake is looking for a Senior Maintenance Technician with an EPA certification and somewhere between three and five years of hands-on experience in multifamily or facilities work. The day-to-day covers the full range: electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliances, flooring, gas fixtures, and the kind of safety corrections (broken gates, open hazards, lighting failures) that can't wait. You'll also take point on preventive maintenance scheduling and inventory management, so work orders don't stall because a part isn't on hand.

The step up from a standard tech role here is real. You're expected to mentor junior technicians, cover Service Manager responsibilities when they're out, and interact directly with residents to diagnose and resolve issues. That combination of technical depth and people management is exactly what separates a Senior Tech from someone who's just been around longer. If you're already doing this informally at your current property, this role formalizes it and gives you credit for it.

On-call is part of the job. That's worth naming plainly. Emergency response for residents and the property comes with the territory, and the candidates who thrive in this role are the ones who treat it as a trade-off they've consciously accepted, not a surprise.

What Cortland Brings to the Table

  • Pay range of $27 to $30 per hour, plus monthly commission
  • Full benefits: medical, dental, vision, life, disability, and 401(k)
  • 20% rent discount on Cortland communities (subject to occupancy thresholds)
  • In-house proctors for CPO, EPA, and CPR certifications if you need them
  • An individual growth plan built around your specific career goals

That last point matters more than it sounds. Cortland's scale means there are actual rungs above this role, not just a vague promise of advancement. Senior Techs who build strong relationships with their Service Managers and demonstrate leadership on the floor tend to move into Service Manager roles within a few years. From there, the path toward facilities director or regional maintenance roles opens up. The skills you build here, particularly preventive maintenance planning and team coordination, carry directly into those positions.

Denver's multifamily market remains competitive, and Class A operators are raising the bar on resident experience. A technician who can handle complex systems and communicate well with residents is genuinely hard to find. If that description fits you, this role is a reasonable next step.

job.title: Senior Maintenance Technician-Summermill at Falls River
job.description:
Most maintenance teams at multifamily communities run on a two-tier structure: technicians who turn units and handle work orders, and a Service Manager who owns the big picture. This role sits in between. It's the person who keeps operations moving when the manager is out, trains the junior techs, and personally handles the calls that require real diagnostic skill.

What the Job Actually Involves

Summermill at Falls River is a Cortland-owned community in Raleigh, and this Senior Tech position carries genuine weight. Your EPA certification and three to five years of multifamily or facilities experience are the baseline. The daily work spans electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliances, flooring, and general safety corrections. You're not just executing work orders. You're diagnosing problems, prioritizing the unsafe ones first (open holes, broken gates, lighting failures), and making sure the right work gets assigned and completed on time.

You'll also step into the Service Manager's seat when they're unavailable. That means resident communication, scheduling, and accountability for the team's output. It's not a stretch assignment. It's a regular expectation.

Preventive maintenance is part of the rhythm here too. Security systems, mechanical equipment, and scheduled inspections all fall under your scope. You'll manage parts inventory so work orders don't stall waiting on supplies. On-call rotation is real. Emergencies don't wait for business hours.

What You Bring

  • EPA 608 certification (required)
  • 3 to 5 years of experience in multifamily maintenance or facilities services
  • Hands-on competency across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Comfort leading or mentoring junior technicians
  • Ability to step up operationally when the Service Manager is absent
  • Willingness to work on-call shifts as part of a rotating schedule

Cortland provides in-house training with certified proctors for CPO, EPA, and CPR credentials, plus online coursework at your own pace. There's also a structured growth plan tied to individual goals, which matters if you're thinking about a Service Manager role down the road. That's the natural next step from this position.

Compensation includes monthly commission on top of base pay, a full benefits package (medical, dental, vision, life, disability, 401k), and a 20% rent discount on Cortland-owned communities subject to occupancy thresholds.

One honest note on this type of role: strong Senior Techs are the ones who treat resident interactions as seriously as the technical work. Raleigh's rental market is competitive, and resident retention at the community level often comes down to how quickly and professionally maintenance issues get resolved. If you're good with people and good with tools, that combination carries real value here.

job.title: Senior Service Manager
job.description:
Most maintenance leadership roles at the senior level split time between turning wrenches and managing people. At a company like Cortland, which operates a vertically integrated portfolio and holds itself to institutional performance standards, the Senior Service Manager role leans hard into both. You're not just the most experienced technician on site. You're running a team, owning the make-ready pipeline, and partnering directly with the Community Manager to keep the asset performing.

What This Role Actually Covers

Day to day, you'll oversee and inspect the work of two or more full-time Service Technicians, which means you're accountable for quality, speed, and consistency across all maintenance output. Service requests need to close within 24 hours when possible. Make-ready status on vacant units needs to stay current and accurate. The punch list doesn't manage itself, and neither does the on-call rotation.

On the technical side, you'll diagnose and resolve issues across electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliances, flooring, and more. You're also the person who keeps safety compliance tight: MSDS sheets, HAZCOM records, pool logs, refrigerant logs, lead-based paint documentation, and OSHA requirements all fall in your lane. Utility meter cutoffs and sewer clean-outs need to be mapped and maintained. Inventory of parts and materials is yours to manage so the team isn't waiting on a supply run to close a ticket.

The people management side is real here. You'll hire, train, and develop your technicians, handle performance management when needed, and think about succession. Cortland emphasizes internal growth, so how you develop your team reflects directly on your own leadership track record.

What You'll Need

  • 5 to 6 or more years in carpentry, plumbing, and/or electrical repair; multifamily experience preferred
  • Current EPA, CPO, and HVAC certifications
  • Valid Class E driver's license
  • High school diploma or equivalent; technical degree or college coursework is a plus
  • Working knowledge of ADA, Fair Housing, OSHA, and EPA regulations
  • Availability for day, evening, and weekend hours as the role requires

What Separates Strong Candidates

The technicians who struggle in senior roles are usually excellent at repair work but haven't made the mental shift to thinking about the team's output, not just their own. The strongest candidates here will have already supervised others, ideally in multifamily, and will have a track record of closing make-readies on time without sacrificing quality. Cortland operates at scale with institutional expectations, so candidates who've only worked at smaller, loosely managed communities may find the documentation and compliance requirements more demanding than they expect. If you've kept a preventive maintenance program running correctly and can speak to how it affected unit turn time or reduced emergency work orders, that's the kind of experience that lands well in an interview here.

This role is a natural stepping stone toward Area Maintenance Director or Facilities Manager positions as your portfolio of managed properties and team leadership experience grows.

job.title: Service Manager - Cortland at Dulles
job.description:
It's 7 a.m. on a Monday and you're already triaging three open work orders, a vendor no-show, and a technician who called out sick. That's the job. If that sounds manageable rather than chaotic, keep reading.

What This Role Actually Involves

Cortland is looking for a Service Manager at their Herndon, Virginia community. You're the person responsible for keeping a large multifamily property physically sound and the maintenance operation running without constant intervention from above. That means hands-on diagnostic work across electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliances, and structural systems, plus the leadership side: scheduling, task prioritization, quality inspections, and keeping your technicians focused and accountable.

Record-keeping matters here. You'll track resident service requests, preventive maintenance schedules, vendor activity, and supplies inventory. The organizational side of this role is not secondary to the technical side. Both carry equal weight. If your documentation habits are loose, this won't be a good fit.

You'll also own the budget for maintenance supplies and vendor spend. Not just spending within it, but actively managing it. Cortland operates at scale as a vertically integrated owner-operator, so their internal standards and reporting expectations tend to be more structured than what you'd find at a smaller fee-management shop.

What You'll Need to Bring

  • Demonstrated hands-on experience in carpentry, plumbing, and electrical repair
  • Current EPA 608 certification (required)
  • Current CPO certification (required)
  • Strong written and verbal communication for reporting and team direction
  • Physical ability to perform and inspect maintenance work across a property
  • A track record of leading a maintenance team, not just working alongside one

The candidates who stand out in this type of role aren't just technically sharp. They're the ones who build a preventive maintenance culture on their team rather than constantly reacting to resident complaints. If your current team catches problems before residents notice them, that habit will translate directly here.

Cortland's Herndon location puts you in a competitive Northern Virginia rental market where resident expectations skew high. Class A residents notice deferred maintenance quickly, and turn quality directly affects renewal rates. The ability to keep make-ready timelines tight while maintaining standards is a real differentiator at properties like this one.

Cortland is an equal opportunity employer and participates in E-Verify. The position is based on-site in Herndon, VA.

job.title: Service Manager - Cortland Pentagon City
job.description:
It's 7:45 a.m. A work order came in overnight for a unit with no hot water, a vendor is due on-site for a HVAC inspection, and one of your technicians called out sick. That's Tuesday. This role runs on your ability to triage, delegate, and still move the needle on preventive maintenance before the day gets away from you.

What This Job Is

Cortland's Service Manager position at Pentagon City is a hands-on leadership role at a multifamily apartment community in Arlington, VA. You're responsible for the physical condition of the property and the performance of the maintenance team. That means you're diagnosing electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliance, and structural issues. It also means you're running the team that handles them, not just the work itself.

Day to day, you'll assign and track service requests, oversee preventive maintenance schedules, inspect completed work, manage vendor relationships, and keep the maintenance budget and supplies inventory in order. When something's outside your team's bandwidth, you coordinate. When something needs a second set of experienced hands, that's yours too.

Record-keeping matters here. Cortland runs a tight operation, and the Service Manager is the person who keeps maintenance data accurate and current. If you're someone who loses track of open work orders or lets punch lists slide, this isn't the right fit.

What You Bring

  • Demonstrated hands-on experience in carpentry, plumbing, and electrical repair
  • Current EPA 608 certification (required) and CPO certification
  • Proven ability to lead and motivate a maintenance team
  • Strong organizational skills and disciplined record-keeping habits
  • Physical ability to perform the work, including lifting, climbing, and working in confined spaces
  • A problem-solving mindset that doesn't wait for issues to escalate

Pentagon City sits in a competitive Class A submarket. Residents at this type of community have high expectations for response times and quality of repairs. The Service Managers who stand out here aren't just technically skilled. They're consistent. They follow up. They build a team culture that takes pride in the property, not just the paycheck.

This role tends to be a natural stepping stone toward regional maintenance leadership or facilities director positions for those who demonstrate strong team development and operational discipline. Cortland's vertical integration across design, construction, and operations also means there are internal pathways that aren't common at third-party management firms.

Cortland is an equal opportunity employer and participates in E-Verify.

job.title: Service Manager-Cortland Research Park
job.description:
This role runs on three skill sets: hands-on technical repair knowledge, team leadership, and operational discipline. If you're strong in two of those three, you'll struggle. Cortland's Service Manager position at Research Park in Durham, NC demands all three working together daily.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

You're diagnosing and resolving issues across the full range: electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliances, gas systems, structural, lighting, and security. Some days you're turning wrenches yourself. Other days you're reviewing your technicians' completed work, checking quality, and making sure punch list items don't quietly fall through the cracks. The resident service request queue doesn't pause, and your job is to keep it moving without letting preventive maintenance slip.

You'll own the maintenance budget, manage vendor relationships, and keep supplies stocked at levels that don't leave your team scrambling mid-turn. Record-keeping matters here. Cortland tracks maintenance data seriously, so your ability to log, assign, and follow up on work orders with accuracy is just as important as your technical skills in the field.

Leading the Maintenance Team

You're not a solo technician. You're running a crew. That means prioritizing their daily task load, coaching them on protocol, and holding the line on safety standards without micromanaging every move. You inspect their work. You identify gaps in skill or process and address them directly. Cortland operates at scale across a large national portfolio, which means there are systems and standards already in place. Your job is to execute them consistently at this property.

Strong candidates for this role tend to be the ones who've already made the mental shift from "best technician on the team" to "person responsible for the team's output." That shift is harder than it sounds, and a lot of maintenance supervisors never fully make it.

What Cortland Expects

  • Proven carpentry, plumbing, and electrical repair experience
  • Current EPA 608 certification and CPO certification
  • Sharp record-keeping and reporting habits
  • Physical ability to perform hands-on maintenance work
  • Problem-solving instincts that kick in before residents even notice something's wrong

Cortland is vertically integrated, meaning design, construction, and operations all run in-house. At the property level, that gives a Service Manager access to internal resources and institutional knowledge that smaller operators can't match. It also means the bar for execution is high. You're part of a system built around performance, and the expectations reflect that.

Durham's multifamily market has stayed competitive, with steady demand driven by the Research Triangle's continued growth. A well-run maintenance operation directly supports resident retention, and at a Cortland community, that connection between maintenance quality and NOI is taken seriously from the top down.

job.title: Service Manager - Cortland Sun River
job.description:
Cortland runs a vertically integrated operation, which means a Service Manager here carries more weight than the same title at a typical third-party managed community. You're not just keeping the lights on. You're protecting an asset that Cortland owns, designs, and operates under one roof, and your team's performance feeds directly into resident retention, NOI, and the long-term value of the property.

What This Role Actually Involves

The core of this position is maintenance leadership at a multifamily community. You'll diagnose and resolve complex issues across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, appliances, and structural systems, and you'll make sure your technicians can do the same. That means hands-on training, daily task assignment, work order oversight, and stepping in yourself when the situation calls for it. Make-readies, punch list inspections, preventive maintenance scheduling, and on-call coverage all fall within your scope.

Beyond the technical side, you're managing the full maintenance operation: vendor coordination, parts and supply inventory, budget tracking, and safety compliance. Cortland expects you to stay ahead of problems rather than react to them. If your team is consistently catching issues early, before a resident submits a request, you're doing the job right.

What Cortland Needs From You

  • 3 to 5 or more years of maintenance experience covering carpentry, plumbing, electrical, and HVAC. Multifamily background is strongly preferred.
  • Universal EPA certification and CPO certification, both required at hire.

  • Working knowledge of OSHA, ADA, EPA regulations, and Fair Housing requirements.
  • Demonstrated ability to lead, train, and mentor a maintenance team.
  • Comfort managing a high volume of details: open work orders, vendor schedules, budget line items, and preventive maintenance records simultaneously.
  • Availability for evenings, weekends, and on-call rotation.
  • Physical ability to lift, climb, and work in both indoor and outdoor conditions.

What Sets Strong Candidates Apart

A lot of applicants can show technical competence. Fewer can show they've built a team culture where technicians take ownership and flag problems proactively without being asked. At Cortland's scale, reactive maintenance is expensive. The Service Managers who advance here are the ones who run tight preventive maintenance programs, keep delinquent work orders from piling up, and communicate clearly with both their team and residents. If you've managed a maintenance crew through a heavy capex project or a lease-up push, that experience translates directly.

Cortland was founded in 2005 and has grown into one of the ten largest multifamily owners and operators in the country. The in-house structure means decisions move faster and resources are generally more accessible than in a typical fee management setup. For a Service Manager who wants visibility and room to grow into a regional or director-level maintenance role, that structure matters.

job.title: Service Manager-The Heritage at Leesburg
job.description:
Maintenance leadership at a multifamily community is where operations either hold together or quietly fall apart. The Service Manager is the person who decides which one happens. At Cortland's Heritage at Leesburg community in northern Virginia, that role carries real weight: you're responsible for the physical condition of a multimillion-dollar asset, the performance of a maintenance team, and the day-to-day living experience of every resident on the property.

You'll lead a team of maintenance technicians through the full scope of service operations. That means preventive maintenance schedules, make-ready turns, resident service requests, vendor coordination, and budget tracking, all running simultaneously. You're not just fixing things. You're setting the pace and standard for how the team works.

On the technical side, you need to be genuinely capable across electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliance repair, and structural issues. Cortland expects their Service Managers to diagnose and resolve problems independently, not just escalate. When a technician gets stuck, you're the one they come to. You'll also inspect completed work, perform tasks yourself when the situation calls for it, and make sure nothing slips through on the punch list.

Record-keeping matters here as much as the wrenches. You'll track service requests, preventive maintenance logs, supply inventory, and vendor activity. The organizational side of this role is not a secondary responsibility. It's part of how the property runs clean.

What Cortland Is Looking For

  • Demonstrated hands-on experience in carpentry, plumbing, and electrical repair
  • Current EPA 608 certification (required) and CPO certification
  • Strong record-keeping and reporting discipline
  • Physical ability to perform the full range of maintenance tasks
  • Experience leading or supervising a maintenance team
  • Resourceful approach to problem-solving under time pressure

Cortland operates as a vertically integrated firm, meaning design, construction, and property management all run in-house. That structure gives a Service Manager more internal support and clearer accountability than you'd find at a third-party management company. It also means expectations are higher. They're watching asset performance closely, and maintenance quality shows up in resident retention, renewal rates, and ultimately NOI.

The candidates who stand out in this type of role aren't just technically sharp. They build team habits. A Service Manager who can get a team to catch issues proactively, before a resident ever submits a request, is protecting occupancy and reducing costly emergency work. That's the difference between someone who maintains a property and someone who actually manages it.

job.title: Service Technician
job.description:
Maintenance technicians are the operational backbone of any multifamily community. When a resident submits a work order, the person who shows up sets the tone for the entire living experience. Cortland, one of the top 10 multifamily owners and operators in the country, is hiring a Service Technician at their Reynoldsburg, OH community for someone early in their maintenance career, up to one year of experience.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Day to day, you're handling the fundamentals: carpentry, basic plumbing, electrical repairs, grounds upkeep, and trash removal. You'll also run turns, which means getting vacant units through the make-ready process so they're rent-ready on schedule. Turns are where a lot of junior technicians build speed and discipline fast. You'll assist with resident events and take on tasks your supervisor directs, which means some days are predictable and some aren't.

Cortland runs a vertically integrated operation, meaning their design, construction, and management functions work under one umbrella. For a technician, that structure tends to mean more internal resources and clearer escalation paths than you'd find at a smaller fee management shop.

What You'll Build Here

Cortland offers something worth paying attention to: in-house proctors who are CPO, EPA, and CPR certified, so you can earn those certifications on the job rather than paying out of pocket before you're hired. That matters. EPA 608 certification alone opens doors to HVAC-focused roles, and CPO certification is a prerequisite for many senior maintenance positions. They also provide an individual growth plan with mentorship and leadership support built in.

The compensation includes monthly commission on top of base pay, plus full benefits.

  • Up to 1 year of maintenance experience required
  • Basic carpentry, plumbing, and electrical repair skills
  • Comfort with grounds maintenance and make-ready work
  • Ability to work collaboratively with a site team and follow supervisor direction

If you're thinking about where this role leads: Service Technician is the standard entry point into a track that runs through Senior Technician, Lead Technician, and eventually Maintenance Supervisor or Service Manager. Technicians who pick up HVAC, plumbing, and electrical certifications along the way compress that timeline considerably. At a portfolio the size of Cortland's, internal mobility is real.

The candidate who stands out here isn't necessarily the one with the most experience. It's the one who takes the turn process seriously, communicates clearly with residents, and treats certifications as investments rather than checkboxes. That combination compounds quickly in this business.

job.title: Service Technician - Cortland Aertson
job.description:
Multifamily operators at scale run into a specific staffing challenge: they need technicians who can handle the full range of day-to-day maintenance competently, but who also fit into a structured, systems-driven operation. Cortland, one of the ten largest apartment owners and operators in the country, built its maintenance program around that reality. This role at Cortland Aertson in Nashville puts you inside that system.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your day moves between scheduled work orders and whatever the property surfaces. That means diagnosing and repairing electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliances, flooring, and fixtures, then correcting unsafe conditions before they become liability issues. You're also responsible for keeping preventive maintenance on schedule, which matters more than most technicians realize. Equipment that gets skipped on PM cycles fails faster, and emergency repairs always cost more than preventive ones.

Security systems, automatic doors, and surveillance cameras fall under your watch too. You'll manage your own parts inventory to keep work order completion times tight. On-call rotations are part of the role, so resident and property emergencies after hours are a real expectation, not a footnote.

  • EPA certification required (Cortland offers in-house certified proctors if you need CPO or CPR)
  • 1 to 3 years of experience in facilities or multifamily maintenance
  • Hands-on competency across electrical, plumbing, HVAC, and general repairs
  • Comfortable identifying and correcting unsafe conditions independently
  • Able to manage parts inventory and coordinate with on-site staff on work completion

Why This Role Has Real Upside

Nashville's multifamily market has seen significant lease-up activity over the last several years, and that growth brought a wave of Class A properties that now need experienced maintenance staff to keep them performing. Working at a Cortland asset gives you exposure to a vertically integrated operation where design, construction, and property management sit under the same roof. That structure means decisions move faster, resources are more accessible, and the training programs are built in-house rather than outsourced.

Cortland offers a personalized growth plan alongside mentorship and leadership support, which is worth taking seriously if you're thinking about where this role leads. Technicians who build their certification portfolio and develop strong diagnostic skills typically move into lead or supervisor roles within a few years. Monthly commission is included in the compensation package alongside full benefits.

The candidates who stand out here aren't just technically solid. They communicate well with residents, close work orders without being chased, and treat preventive maintenance as seriously as emergency calls. If you approach the job that way, the path forward inside a company this size is straightforward.

job.title: Service Technician-Cortland at Dulles
job.description:
Maintenance work at an apartment community isn't glamorous. You're pulling trash off the grounds on a Tuesday morning, then crawling under a sink in the afternoon, then helping prep a vacant unit so the next resident can move in on time. That's the job. If you're someone who takes quiet satisfaction in fixing things and keeping a property running smoothly, this role at Cortland's Herndon, VA community is worth a close look.

Cortland is one of the larger multifamily operators in the country, and this position is built for someone early in their maintenance career. Up to a year of experience is the baseline. You'll handle the everyday work: carpentry, basic plumbing, electrical repairs, grounds upkeep, and make-readies on vacant units. Turns are where a lot of your time will go. Getting units punched out and ready for incoming residents keeps occupancy moving and directly affects how the property performs, so the quality of your work actually matters to the bottom line in a concrete way.

One thing worth knowing: Cortland runs its certification training in-house. CPO, EPA, and CPR are offered through proctors on staff, so you're not paying out of pocket or hunting down outside courses. For someone newer to maintenance, that's a real benefit. Most operators expect you to show up already certified.

What You'll Bring

  • Up to one year of hands-on maintenance experience, ideally in residential or multifamily settings
  • Basic working knowledge of plumbing, electrical, and carpentry repairs
  • Comfort with physical, outdoor work including grounds maintenance
  • Reliability. Showing up matters more in this role than almost anything else.
  • Willingness to assist with resident events and tasks outside your core scope

The honest challenge in any service tech role is that resident satisfaction is part of your performance, not just the work order count. You'll interact with residents who are frustrated, in a hurry, or both. Keeping a calm, professional presence when someone is unhappy about a leaking faucet that's been dripping for three days is a real skill, and it's one the best techs develop fast.

This kind of role is also a natural on-ramp to a senior tech or lead maintenance position down the road. The certifications you earn here, combined with hands-on experience across multiple trade categories, build a foundation that transfers directly to higher-responsibility roles at any operator. Cortland specifically offers individual growth plans, which suggests there's at least a stated commitment to moving people up rather than keeping them in the same seat indefinitely.

Pay includes a monthly commission component on top of base, which is less common at the entry-level tech tier and worth factoring into the total compensation picture.

job.title: Service Technician - Cortland Belmar
job.description:
Cortland Belmar in Lakewood is hiring a Service Technician at the entry-to-mid level, meaning you're somewhere in your first year of hands-on maintenance work and you're ready to build real technical depth inside a portfolio that takes operations seriously. This isn't a role where you're handed a clipboard and told to figure it out. Cortland runs a vertically integrated model, design, construction, and property management all under one roof, and that structure actually changes what day-to-day maintenance looks like at the site level. You're backed by systems and resources that a lot of smaller operators simply don't have.

The core of the job is what you'd expect from any well-run multifamily community. Carpentry, plumbing, electrical repairs. Grounds upkeep. Resident service requests. The work that keeps occupancy sticky and renewals healthy. But the piece that matters most at this level is the turn process. Make-readies are where a technician's efficiency is most visible. A tight punch list, a fast cycle, a unit that's genuinely rent-ready rather than just checked off. The faster and cleaner your turns, the faster a vacant unit starts generating revenue again. That connection between your daily work and the property's NOI is real, even at this stage of your career.

Pay runs $24 to $27 per hour, plus monthly commission. Certifications, including CPO, EPA, and CPR, are earned through Cortland's in-house proctors, not on your own dime or your own time outside of work. That's worth paying attention to. Certifications that would otherwise cost hundreds of dollars out of pocket come built into the job.

What sets strong candidates apart here

The technicians who advance quickly at companies like Cortland aren't necessarily the ones with the longest resumes. They're the ones who treat a punch list like a checklist worth finishing completely, not mostly. They communicate clearly when a repair is outside their current skill set rather than guessing. And they understand that resident satisfaction scores and maintenance response times are tracked, because at a data-driven operator, they always are.

  • Up to one year of maintenance experience in a multifamily or similar setting
  • Working knowledge of basic carpentry, plumbing, and electrical repair
  • Ability to assist with make-readies and unit turns
  • Willingness to support resident events and community upkeep as needed
  • Comfortable working toward EPA, CPO, or CPR certification if not already held

From this role, the natural path runs toward Lead Technician and eventually Service Manager. Those positions carry real P&L visibility, vendor management, and oversight of the full maintenance budget for a community. Starting here, doing the foundational work well, is how that path begins.

job.title: Service Technician - Cortland Lake Ridge

Service technician roles at large, vertically integrated operators like Cortland tend to offer something smaller ownership groups can't match: structured training, internal certification, and a visible path from entry-level wrench-turning to senior maintenance or even facilities leadership.
